Summary: Search & Replace in state transition tables or truth tables might not correctly replace variable names
Description: When using the Search & Replace function, variable names might not be correctly replaced if searching within astate transition table or truth table.

Summary: Compatibility check fails when the model contains a Function Caller block which calls a Simulink Function having bus arguments.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails when:The model contains a Function Caller  block which calls a Simulink Function  having buses as the input or output arguments.  The Simulink Function  is not defined in the model that contains the Function Caller  block.  Sample error message:  Failed to compile <model name>

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameter Writer blocks that write to different model workspace variables in same type of subsystems generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code generation occurs for Parameter Writer blocks when all the following conditions are met: Parameter Writer blocks write to different model workspace variables. The Parameter Writer blocks are in same type of subsystems. For example, the Parameter Writer blocks are in different function-call subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ?On transitions in charts that use C as the action language, count and duration operators might return incorrect value
Description: Values returned by the count  and duration  operators might be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses C action language The operators are used in a condition, condition action, or transition action of a transition The argument to the count  or duration  operator is changed elsewhere within the chart by unary operators  For example, if a state had the following during action:? during:??x++;?y = count(x>0);  and an outgoing transition:? E{z = count(x>0);}  the value of y  and z would not match as expected.?

Summary: Using nonscalar query points in table lookup may lead to wrong answer
Description: When a model contains blocks that use table lookup, it can generate a wrong answer if both of these conditions are true: A table lookup is evaluated for a nonscalar query point. The model has a high differential index and requires index reduction.
Workaround: When authoring a custom Simscape block, you can use a set of table lookup calls, each evaluated for a scalar query point, instead of using a single table lookup call for a nonscalar query point.

Summary: Accelerated simulation fails for a model hierarchy containing reference models with model arguments
Description: Accelerated simulation of a model hierarchy containing a referenced model with model arguments can fail if the referenced model has the model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model ?set to Multiple? and a referenced model higher in the hierarchy has the model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?set to?One.? In this case, when you compile the simulation target code for the referenced model higher in the hierarchy, a memcpy is executed on an undeclared identifier and Simulink reports an error similar to:? ?mMid_msf.c:250:40: error: 'fzidmdwontw' undeclared (first use in this function) ?{ return rtS ; } ( void ) memcpy ( & ( fzidmdwontw . fdvovoslul . rtm .
Workaround: Set the model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model?to Multiple for all referenced models in the hierarchy.

Summary: Unexpected gray (unreachable) code in main generator mode in presence of non-finite float variables
Description: If you explicitly initialize global variables to NaN  in your code and your code does not contain a main()  function, you might see most functions in your code as unreachable despite using the option -allow-non-finite-floats .

Summary: Genomics Viewer app fails to open
Description: Starting in January 2024, Genomics Viewer is no longer able to load the default reference genome (HG38), causing the app to not load.

Summary: Non-Direct Feedthrough Flag is not honored for bus type input ports in S-Functions generated with S-Function Builder Block?
Description: In S-Function Builder, bus ports are always Direct Feedthrough, even if you uncheck the Direct Feedthrough  option on the settings pane.

Summary: Longitudinal Vehicle block errors when the road slope port?is hidden
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Longitudinal Vehicle block generates the error message "Number of variables exceeds number of equations " when the?Show road slope inport ?parameter is cleared.
Workaround: To maintain a constant zero slope, select?Show road slope inport, and leave port PG?or port NG?disconnected.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when all of the parameters of a noninlined S-function have empty values
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model hierarchy when all of the following are true:A referenced model in the hierarchy contains a noninlined S-function That noninlined S-function has 1 or more parameters, each with an empty value (for example [] or '') The Simulation mode  parameter?of the Model block referencing that referenced model is not set to Normal   If you generate and build code for the model hierarchy, the compiled executable might also crash.
Workaround: Add an unused parameter with a non-empty value to the S-function.

Summary: Model Advisor checks 'mathworks.hism.hisl_0008' and 'mathworks.jmaab.jc_0640' might error out on large models with long block paths
Description: Running the Model Advisor checks mathworks.hism.hisl_0008 and mathworks.jmaab.jc_0640 on large models with long block path names might result in errors. An example error message is:Abnormal exit: 'Switch' is not a SubSystem block.

Summary: Simulation result is incorrect when a model contains a Parameter Writer block that writes to bus type parameter and simulates in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Model with a Parameter Writer block produces incorrect results in rapid accelerator mode?when all the following conditions occur: The Parameter Writer block is inside an Initialize Function block.?  The Parameter Writer block writes to a bus type model workspace parameter, mask parameter, or block parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation
Description: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation when all of the following conditions are true:? A model has multiple identical atomic subsystems set to reuse code if possible. The subsystems have Interpolation using Prelookup blocks whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip .  In the first instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from external Prelookup block(s) whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip.  In at least one other instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from a block other than a?Prelookup block. These non-Prelookup blocks feed out-of-range index or fraction values to the Interpolation using Prelookup block.  Execution of the generated code might produce incorrect results for the latter because the code will not have protection against the out-of-range index or fraction values.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent initial conditions for multi-instance subsystem output ports might produce incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a multi-instance subsystem that has inconsistent initial conditions for the output port. For example, one instance has initial condition 1  while other instance has initial condition 2  for the output port.  The output port is connected to a Delay or Memory block inside the subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with CG::Pst::Pst in stack trace during code generation
Description: During code generation, MATLAB may crash and produce?a stack trace that looks similar to the following:?  [? 0] 0x00007fba58062f35 CG::PstImpl::PstImpl(CG::Pst*, CG::Cfg*, MemPool*, PstEffects, CG::Dominator*) at /matlab/src/cg_ir/region/PstImpl.cpp:1098 (discriminator 2)  [? 1] 0x00007fba5805f095 CG::Pst::Pst(CG::Cfg*, MemPool*, PstEffects) at /matlab/src/cg_ir/region/Pst.cpp:150  [? 2] 0x00007fb9fa5617da CG::Pst::Pst(CG::Cfg*, MemPool*, CG::Pst::PstFlavor) at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/../../derived/glnxa64/src/include/cg_ir/region/../../../../../../src/cg_ir/export/include/cg_ir/region/Pst.hpp:82  [? 3] 0x00007fb9fa5858d3 (anonymous namespace)::HDLProcessPragmasImpl::doit() [clone .constprop.0] at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/dom_hdl/HDLProcessPragmas.cpp:97  [? 4] 0x00007fb9fa585fe1 CG::HDLProcessPragmas::doit(CG::Scope*) at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/dom_hdl/HDLProcessPragmas.cpp:177  [? 5] 0x00007fb9fa5d45f1 pir::CGTransformDriver::runAllXformsBeforeStateUpdate() const at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/dom_pir_core/CGTransformDriver.cpp:427

Summary: Simulink.LookupTable objects unable to share struct?data?type
Description: Multiple? Simulink.LookupTable ?objects are unable to share?struct ?data? types in generated code when the?PreserveDimensions ?storage class property is selected.? For example, for these two??Simulink.LookupTable ?objects,  lut1  and  lut2 ,? the objects share the?struct ?data type, but MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate code. lut1.Table.Value=[1 2 3]  lut2.Table.Value=[2 4 6]?
Workaround: Configure each Simulink.LookupTable?object with a unique Name?value for the property StructTypeInfo.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might not initialize global variables that represent outputs of reusable subsystems
Description: The generated code might not initialize global outputs for a subsystem when all ?of the following are true: The Enable local block outputs ?model configuration parameter is selected. The model contains multiple subsystems that can be optimized into a single reusable function in the generated code. Each of those subsystems has outputs that have initial values. Some, but not all, of those subsystem outputs are unconnected or attached to a Terminator block.  The code generator generates a reusable function for the subsystems. For the outputs that are unconnected or attached to a Terminator block, the generated code uses local variables. For the outputs that are connected to another block, the generated code uses global variables. The code generator might not set the initial values for global variables that represent connected outputs. This is incorrect behavior because the generated code should initialize global variables that have initial values.
Workaround: Set the PassReuseOutputArgsAs model configuration parameter to Structure reference. To do this programmatically, run this command from the MATLAB Command Window, substituting modelName?with the name of your model:set_param(modelName, 'PassReuseOutputArgsAs', 'Structure reference')To set the parameter in the UI, set the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Optimization > Pass reusable subsystem outputs as?model configuration parameter?to Structure reference.

Summary: A2L file generation might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime for a model with a lookup table block that has values for both table data and breakpoints from the same member of a Simulink bus object
Description: The?coder.asap2.export ?API parses data from a Simulink model and accumulates it into a structured format as required for the A2L file generation. When a model with a lookup table block has values for both table data and breakpoints from the same member of a Simulink bus object, data accessed by?coder.asap2.export()  is incorrect which? might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [0] 0x00007ff87cbbb1c2 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\slcg_mf.dll+06140354 coder::descriptor::types::Matrix::Impl::getBaseType+00000018[1] 0x00007ff9132b7b35 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00752437 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00601365[2] 0x00007ff9132b9d3d C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00761149 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00610077
Workaround: For values in lookup table blocks, use either explicit data or different members of a Simulink bus object for table data and breakpoints.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reinitialized triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: A triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code when all these conditions are met: The triggered subsystem is reinitialized. the triggered subsystem has Function Packaging ?set to Auto ?or  Inline.  The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has?Trigger type ?set to?rising ?or? either . The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has?Initial trigger signal state ?set to?zero ?or? negative .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Build log from SIL/PIL simulation incorrectly reports Compilation artifacts were out of date
Description: Suppose code is already generated and up to date, and you run a top-model software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation for a second time, the build log indicates that top-model artifacts were out of date. For example:? BuildSummary  Topmodel targets built:  Model?????Action????????? RebuildReason? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??  ====================================================================  mBuildTop?Code compiled.? Compilation artifacts were out of date.?    Although the compilation artifacts for the top model were not modified by the second build process, the log incorrectly displays this Rebuild Reason  message: Compilation artifacts were out of date.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Same referenced model instance inside and outside conditional subsystem might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect for a model that has an instance of a referenced model inside a conditional subsystem and an instance outside of it when all the following conditions are met: The conditional subsystem receives a variable-size signal at its input port. The conditional subsystem is disabled. The  Propagate?sizes of variable-size signals ?parameter for the control block of the conditional subsystem?is set to?Only when enabling .  The Model block inside the subsystem is set to simulate in normal mode and outputs variable-size signals. The Model block directly connects to the Outport block of the conditional subsystem. The top-level model is set to run in normal mode.

Summary: Trace matrix for Simulink model comparison incorrectly references script hRptEvalXMLQualkit.m
Description: Artifact?Simulink Model Comparison Trace Matrix  (mathworks_slcmp_trace .xlsx ) incorrectly references file hRptEvalXMLQualkit.m , which does not ship with the DO Qualification Kit.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect file names referenced in artifact Simulink Coverage Test Cases and Procedures
Description: Artifact?Simulink Coverage Test Cases and Procedures  (qualkitdo_slcov_tcp ) incorrectly references files: certkitiec_slcov_cc_run.mlx  certkitiec_slcov_mc_run.mlx   The correct files names are:qualkitdo_slcov_cc_run.mlx  qualkitdo_slcov_mc_run.mlx

Summary: UDP Send block ignores UDP packet size parameter value
Description: The UDP Send block parameter "'UDP packet size'' setting is ignored, and not changing the packet size as expected. The size of the sent packet is always the default 512 bytes.? This issue also occurs when programmatically setting the OutputDatagramPacketSize  parameter.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Model block with initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port produces incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions occur:? The model is set to simulate in rapid accelerator mode. The model contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port  ,?Show model reinitialize ports,? Show model reset ports  , or Show model terminate port   option selected. The Model block has Schedule rates   option deselected. The top model uses variable step solver or has the configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  deselected. Alternatively,?get_param(model,'SolverMode') ?returns?'SingleTasking' . The referenced model contains only one discrete periodic sample time. The signal driving the initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port of the Model block has different discrete periodic sample time than the discrete periodic sample time set for the referenced model.  You might observe that the Model block executes on the sample hits that are common in both the signal driving the initialize, reset, or terminate port, and the referenced model, instead of executing solely on the sample hits set for the referenced model.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Test case IDs in the Simulink Test trace matrix do not match the IDs provided in the title for the test case in Test Manager
Description: When viewed by using the Simulink Test Test Manager, the ID identified in the title of the test case does not match the test case ID in the Simulink Test trace matrix. Affected tests include: tCov1  tCov2  tCov3  tCov4  tCov5  tCov6  tCov7  tCov8  tCov9  tCov11  tCov12  tCov13  tCov14?
Workaround: Update the test case IDs in artifact Simulink Test - Trace Matrix to match the IDs in the title of the test case.?Depending on the release version, use trace matrix file:? ?For R2023a and earlier, use qualkitdo_sltest_trace.xlsxFor R2023b and later, use mathworks_sltest_trace.xlsxIn the trace matrix, open these worksheets and update the ID:?tCov1, replace SLT_COV_010 with SLT_COV_001tCov2, replace SLT_COV_020 with SLT_COV_002tCov3, replace SLT_COV_030 with SLT_COV_003tCov4, replace SLT_COV_040 with SLT_COV_004Cov5, replace SLT_COV_050 with SLT_COV_005tCov6, replace SLT_COV_060 with SLT_COV_006tCov7, replace SLT_COV_070 with SLT_COV_007tCov8, replace SLT_COV_080 with SLT_COV_008tCov9, replace SLT_COV_090 with SLT_COV_009tCov11, replace SLT_COV_020 with SLT_COV_011tCov12, replace SLT_COV_050 with SLT_COV_012tCov13, replace SLT_COV_080 with SLT_COV_013tCov14, replace SLT_COV_090 with SLT_COV_014
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Importing and cancelling spreadsheet and MAT-file into Root Inport Mapper causes busy icon
Description: Linking and cancelling spreadsheets with the Root Inport Mapper tool might cause a busy icon. The issue occurs with this sequence: In Root Inport Mapper toolbar, click From Spreadsheet .? In the From Spreadsheet dialog, click Browse ?and then click Cancel . Close or cancel the From Spreadsheet dialog. In Root Inport Mapper toolbar, click?From MAT-File . In the From MAT-File dialog, click?Browse ?and then click Cancel .  The busy icon also occurs if you start the sequence with?From MAT-File ?and then From Spreadsheet .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Call to dlarray method softmax?that includes?'DataFormat'?name-value argument
Description: In the generated code, a call to the?dlarray  method?softmax ?might produce incorrect results if the call uses the 'DataFormat' ?name-value argument and either  of these conditions is true:  The value of the input has singleton  U ?dimensions. For example:    dlIn = dlarray(ones(5,4,1,3));out = softmax(dlIn,'DataFormat','CTUB');  The input has an internal permutation. For example:  dlIn = dlarray(ones(5,4,3));out = softmax(permute(dlIn,[3 1 2]),'DataFormat','TCB');
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Test Generation analysis incorrectly reports objectives as Unsatisfiable on a model with dirty subsystem references.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports Invalid Simulink object handle  error  if you create a harness for a subsystem reference and perform test generation analysis without saving the subsystem reference Subsequently, if you save the subsystem reference and rerun the analysis, Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports all objectives from that subsystem reference as Unsatisfiable . This erroneous behavior is noticed only when the parameter?Rebuild model representation ?is set to If change is detected.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: crossentropy, huber, l1loss, and l2loss return incorrect values for masked loss
Description: When you set the NormalizationFactor  option of crossentropy , huber , l1loss , and l2loss  to "mask-included" , the functions do not divide by the number of observations when normalizing the loss values.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: extractFileText fails to extract text from PDF
Description: extractFileText  might skip parts or all of the text in some PDF files where the internal structure contains one or more of the following: XObjects using the same name as other XObjects? XObjects that do not have a /Type  entry
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Setting InputPipeline on subsystem introduces twice the number of latency cycles than expected
Description: When HDL block property InputPipeline  is set to X, HDL Coder introduces X cycles of latency. However, it may introduce 2X cycles of latency under all  these conditions: InputPipeline  is configured for the subsystem that is supposed to be flattened. This means that FlattenHierarchy  is set to on  for either the subsystem itself or one of its parent subsystems. A fan-out signal, which drives multiple inputs of the subsystem, is present.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Counterbalance Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization might cause unexpected behavior
Description: The Counterbalance Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization options from the manufacturer Sun Hydraulics do not extrapolate the supplier data sheet to the full relief stage opening. This can result in incorrect results during a load lowering simulation scenario in heavy machinery.

Summary: AUTOSAR software component with provide-require port (PRPort) can produce mismatch between model and SIL/PIL simulation results
Description: Suppose you use the SIL/PIL Manager to test code generated from a model that implements?AUTOSAR port-based sender-receiver (S-R) communication. You might observe a numerical mismatch between model and SIL/PIL simulation results when these conditions apply: An AUTOSAR software component uses a sender-receiver interface. The component has root-level Inport and Outport blocks that are mapped to provide-require ports (PRPort ) with 'DataAccessMode ' set to 'ImplicitReceive ' and?'ImplicitSend ' respectively. Within a single execution of the runnable, the generated code reads from an input PRPort  after writing to the corresponding output PRPort . For example:?  void Runnable(void)  {     ? ...   ? Rte_IWrite_Runnable_PRPort1_PRPort1(x);   ? ...  ? y = Rte_IRead_Runnable_PRPort1_PRPort1();  }?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU atomic operation moved inside for-loop
Description: When using GPU Coder, the generated code might be incorrect if all  the following conditions are true: The MATLAB code contains a pair of for -loops, with one nested inside the other. A GPU atomic operation such as?gpucoder.atomicAdd  is nested between the two loops. The atomic operation operates on a scalar variable.  In such situations, in the generated code, the atomic operation might be incorrectly moved inside the inner loop. For example, the following design function produces incorrect code: function [A,t] = design()A = coder.nullcopy(zeros(100));t = 0;for j = 1:100    t = gpucoder.atomicAdd(t, 1);    for i = 1:100        A(i,j) = i+j;    endendend

Summary: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms
Description: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when the inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms. For example, this inertia tensor is affected: [100 10 20;10 200 30;20 30 150]  This inertia tensor is not affected: [100 0 0;0 200 0;0 0 150]
Workaround: Do not use an inertia tensor with nonzero off-diagonal terms. Instead, use a similarity transformation to make the inertia tensor diagonal.A similarity transformation changes the new body axes to be the eigenvectors and the diagonal terms of the inertia tensor to be the eigenvalues. Calculate the eigenvectors and the diagonal matrix with the eigenvalues using the eig function:[eigenVector,diagonalInertiaTensor]=eig(inertiaTensor);
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread in basic mode can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Certain XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread  in basic  mode can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, in the following code, crash.xls ?causes a MATLAB crash because it contains invalid UTF-8 characters and is read with xlsread  in basic  mode. >> [num, txt] = xlsread("crash.xls", "", "", "basic") The resulting crash dump includes the following phrase. mxCreateString called with non-UTF-8 input xlsread  uses basic  mode by default on platforms that do not have a Microsoft Excel COM server implementation.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem code might be incorrect when the subsystem contains a masked Model block
Description: The generated code for a model that meets all these conditions might be incorrect: The model contains at least two identical instances of a subsystem that contains a Model block. The Model block references a model that defines a model argument. The referenced model is either a function-call model or a model with a virtual bus at a root input or output port. Either the referenced model defines a system mask, or the Model block is directly masked. The model argument?values passed into the two different instances of the Model block are different.  The code generator might produce one function for all instances of the subsystem, using a hard-coded value for the model argument.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is incorrect when a Parameter Writer block writes to a model workspace variable used by a Model block and any other built-in block with tunable parameters
Description: Generated code is incorrect when a Parameter Writer block writes to a model workspace variable that is used by a Model block with storage class for the variable set to?Model default  and used by? any other built-in block with tunable parameters.

Summary: Collection of signal range coverage is disable for model mSignalRangeLimit.slx in the Simulink Coverage qualification test suite
Description: In model mSignalRangeLimit.slx , the Constant block that provides the signal input disables collection of the signal range coverage by Simulink Coverage because the signal width exceeds the specified limit.

Summary: Design Verifier compatibility check errors out if Code Generation settings from referenced configset are overridden.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check may error out if any of Code Generation  settings from the referenced configuration set are overridden in a model. For example: Design Verifier reports the following error if the?Custom Code ?settings under Code Generation ?are overridden to specify custom headers and source files for a C Caller block:Error:Compiling model failed: C Caller block '<path_to_C_Caller_block>' requires model to have associated importable custom code. There is no custom code specified, or 'Import custom code' is disabled.  Error:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: '<model_name>' is incompatible for <design_verifier_analysis_mode> with Simulink Design Verifier.?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing call sites of Stateflow temporal functions
Description: Simulink PLC Coder generated code is missing call sites of Stateflow absolute time temporal logic functions after  and before? when the => syntax                for function block outputs is configured. Since R2023a  the configuration option PLC_UseCallOutputArgSyntax ?can be found under "PLC Code Generation\  Interface\Use output argument syntax in function?and function block calls ". In R2022a  and R2022b  this option is available as fUseCallOutputArgSyntax  plugin option in the custom target IDE workflow.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Code Prover analysis stuck at Software Safety Analysis Level 112 (P_C) with exception "Core.Match(nonexhaustive match failure)"
Description: When your code contains floating point management of NaN or Inf, in certain cases, the analysis might get stuck at Software Safety Analysis Level 0. This can happen when using the option -allow-non-finite-floats . You might see an error message such as the following: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 112 (P_C) exception Core.Match(nonexhaustive match failure) raised. raised at: Raised at Infos.Range_List.collapse in file "../../verifier/kernel_api/common/Infos.sml", line 1812, characters 31-36
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unassigned fields of a nested structure conditionally defined via a function call
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: On one execution path, your MATLAB code creates structure s  containing structure x , such that structure  x  has more than one field  On different execution path, ?your MATLAB code sets s equal to the return value of function? foo  Function? foo ?returns a nested structure that has fewer fields than x   For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function y = test_struct(exp)if exp    s = struct('x', struct('f1',5,'f2',15));else    s = foo();endy = s.x.f2;endfunction out = fooout.x.f1 = 3;end In MATLAB execution, test_struct(false)  produces an error, while test_struct_mex(false) ?returns an undefined value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink.?
Workaround: If your code is affected by this bug, adding a temporary variable that saves the call result should prevent the bug from happening. In the example above, this amounts to changing    s = foo();to?    tempVar = foo(); s = tempVar;
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing when passing a multi-dimensional structured type input to a Model block that references a Simulink model
Description: When passing a multi-dimensional structured type as an input to a Model block that references another model, the code generator might generate incorrect array indexing if?all ?of the following conditions are met.?  The ArrayLayout  parameter of the models in the model hierarchy is set to Row-major .  The input to the Model block is of a structured data type and has multiple dimensions. The input is configured to preserve dimensions in the generated code. The elements from the input are extracted by using blocks such as the Selector block and then passed as an input to the Model block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Checks removed from the Model Advisor in R2021b were not removed from the Simulink Check qualification test suite
Description: These checks were removed from the Model Advisor in R2021b: Check usage of equality operators in MATLAB Function blocks (mathworks.hism.himl_0009 ) Check usage of shift operations for Stateflow data (mathworks.hism.hisf_0064 )   However, the checks were still supported by the Simulink Check qualification test suite.
Workaround: To prevent the test procedure from executing the tests for the checks, delete from artifact?Simulink Check - Trace Matrix?(qualkitdo_slchk_tcp.xlsx) the rows for these test cases:SLCHK_HI_Q074_SFSLCHK_HI_Q059

Summary: "readmsg" limited to TLOG messages with messageIDs less than or equal to 255
Description: Reading Mavlink TLOG messages by using the "readmsg" function and specifying the "MessageName" name-value argument returns empty output for messages with messageIDs greater than 255.
Workaround: Use the "readmsg" function without specifying Name-Value arguments to read TLOG messages with messageIDs greater than 255For example:?tlogReader = mavlinktlog("flight.tlog");?msgData = readmsg(tlogReader);

Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code?from MATLAB code that uses fixed-point data type with a word length greater than 64.? Summary: Simulink Coverage qualification test report can include false negative results for out-of-scope parameters
Description: Some Simulink Coverage parameters are not relevant for model coverage testing. When executing the qualification test procedure, comparison of the actual results against the expected results for these parameters can return an incorrect false negative.

Summary: TLC error when using the S-Function target to generate code
Description: When generating code for a model that uses the S-Function target, the code generator might return an error similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\prm_constant.tlc Line: 902 Column: 28  The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier  Main program:  ==> [00] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\prm_constant.tlc:SLibCacheUnstructuredConstantPrmBuffers(902)?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB might crash during model update phase of model containing Math Reciprocal block with Algorithm Method set to Newton-Raphson
Description: MATLAB might crash during model update phase of a model if all of the following conditions are true: Math Function block?Function ?is set to reciprocal .  Math Function block Algorithm Method ?is set to Newton-Raphson . Output of Math Function block is input to an Outport block. Outport block data type is specified explicitly.?
Workaround: Set the Outport block?Data type parameter to inherit data types, such as Inherit: auto.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation might error or hang for models that contain Integrator blocks with multirate inputs
Description: Accelerator and rapid accelerator simulations incorrectly handle certain zero crossings for Integrator blocks that satisfy all of these conditions: The input signal to integrate is a multirate vector. The first element in the multirate vector signal has a discrete sample time. The Integrator block is configured to limit the output signal value within a specified range. Zero-crossing detection is enabled for both the model and the Integrator block.?  If the simulation uses a variable-step solver, the software might issue an error related to zero-crossing detection or appear to hang.  If the simulation uses a fixed-step solver, simulation might slow down and the solver might miss zero crossings.
Workaround: Replace affected Integrator blocks with multiple Integrator blocks that each integrate a scalar input signal.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect if Inport blocks inside referenced models use a storage class other than Auto
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a Simulink model that meets all  of these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. At least one Inport block inside the referenced model uses a storage class other than Auto .  The generated code might contain uninitialized local variables which are accessed or read in code.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Terminate Function block might give wrong answer in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Terminate Function block gives wrong answer in rapid accelerator mode when all the following conditions occur: The Terminate Function block calls a Data Store Read block which reads data from a Data Store Memory block. The data values change when the Terminate Function block executes. A Data Store Write block writes the updated data to the same Data Store Memory block. The data store updates twice. The Terminate Function block is inside a model that has multiple execution rates. The model containing the Terminate Function block?is referenced by another model that simulates in rapid accelerator mode.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for MATLAB function containing consequential while loops, which can be infinite once entered
Description: Code generation for MATLAB code that contains a while  loop with a consequential body, which can be an infinite loop once entered, might produce an?assertion error. For example, consider this MATLAB code:? function y = foo(x)  ? ? y = 0; ? ? ? while x == 1  ? ? ? ? y = y + 1;  ? ? end  end   The while  loop in the function foo  is infinite once entered because the value of x  does not change in the loop. The code generation for the MATLAB code might result in a crash with the following message:  Assertion at B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\region\PstImpl.cpp line 822: In computeEnclosingRegions: Pushing null enclosing Region:
Workaround: Change the while loop condition to true,?and wrap the while loop in an if statement.For example, the MATLAB function foo?can be converted to this:?function y = foo(x)? ? y = 0;? ? if x == 1?? ? ? ? while true? ? ? ? ? ? y = y + 1;? ? ? ? end? ? end?end??
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Code generation for a referenced model produces error "Unexpected sigKind outputAddr"
Description: Generating code for a referenced model?produces an "Unexpected sigKind outputAddr " error under the following conditions:The model contains a Simulink Function block. The Simulink Function?block contains a Message Send block or a Stateflow chart that sends a message. The Message Send?block or Stateflow chart is connected directly to an output port of the Simulink Function block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Generated C++ class code might be uncompilable for models configured to use fixed-point data types
Description: C++ code generation might produce uncompilable code for a model under the following conditions:The model has code interface packaging set to generate C++ class code. The model contains Inport or Outport blocks configured with fixed-point data types.  Under these conditions, the generated code contains declarations for methods ModelExternalInputInit  and ModelExternalOutputInit , but it does not contain the corresponding method definitions.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Simulink models that use variable-size signals with model reference might produce a silent wrong answer
Description: Simulating a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all these conditions apply: A nonvirtual bus containing at least one variable-size signal is input to a Model block. The Model block is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The Model block has only input ports and no output ports. The simulation logs data for the variable-size signal in the bus.
Workaround: Configure the Model block to simulate in normal mode. Then, simulate the top model in normal mode.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from Inport blocks does not honor Receiver Service specifications when model also contains In Bus Element blocks
Description: The generated code for an Inport block?might not honor the Receiver Service  specification when all of the following conditions are true:The model is configured with a service code interface.? The model contains both Inport  blocks and In Bus Element blocks. The?Receiver Service ?setting for an Inport block is specified as an option other than?Dictionary default.   The code generated for the In Bus Element?blocks complies with the?Receiver Service? specifications, as intended.
Workaround: Replace all Inport blocks with In Bus Element blocks, or alternatively, replace all the In Bus Element blocks with Inport blocks.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Variable Scaling Analyzer shows no output for some Data Import/Export settings
Description: The Variable Scaling Analyzer shows no output when running a model with the following model configuration settings:? Dataset signal format  is set to?timetable .? Log Dataset data to file ?is on .
Workaround: When using the Variable Scaling Analyzer, make sure that both?the following settings are set appropriately:Set the dataset signal format to timeseries. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Data Import/Export tab, under?Advanced parameters, set Dataset signal format to?timeseries. You can also use?set_param(model,'DatasetSignalFormat','timeseries').?Turn logging to file off. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the?Data Import/Export?tab, clear the Log Dataset data to file?check box. You can also use?set_param(model,'LoggingToFile','off').
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: False positive violation of CERT-C EXP46-C on parenthesized expression
Description: Polyspace raises an incorrect violation of CERT-C EXP46-C when you use parenthesized expressions to indicate a bitwise operation. For example, in the following code, Polyspace reports a false positive violation even though the bitwise &  operates on a parenthesized expression: int A, B, C;     if (A & (B == C))   // false positive violation{   //...}
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for a MATLAB Function block that contains vector indexing code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block that uses vector indexing. For example, consider a simple model that contains a MATLAB Function block that contains this MATLAB code. function P12 = set0001_funToCall(IA4) [P12] = set0001_funOnPath2(IA4);  endfunction P12 = set0001_funOnPath2(IA4) coder.inline('never');P8 = int32(ones(2));P8(:) = transpose(IA4(1,2:1:5));P12 = P8(2,2);  end The MATLAB Function block receives input from an Inport block and outputs to an Outport block. The Inport block outputs a 4-by-5 matrix signal.? The code generator incorrectly produces an empty step function. void model_step()?   {  }?  The code generator should produce code similar to the following.  double??set0001_funOnPath2(double IA4[20])  {  ? return IA4[16];?  }?  void model_step()?  {  ? Out1 = set0001_funOnPath2(In1);  }?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Simulating multiple referenced model instances in normal mode might show incorrect port value labels
Description: When debugging the visible model simulation with port value labels displayed, the port label values might incorrectly display values from a non-visible instance instead of the visible instance. Incorrect port value labels might occur when:?  The model with multi-instance model reference is simulated in normal mode There is a Bus Assignment block upstream of the referenced model
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Parameter overrides with array data might not be saved in test file and display incorrectly in Test Manager
Description: Test cases that contain parameter override array data with greater than ten elements might result in the parameter override data not being saved in the test file. Additionally, these parameter values might be displayed as Derived from file ?in the Test Manager.??

Summary: Signal Editor block signal properties do not save correctly if?Active Signal?parameter is changed before clicking?Apply
Description: The Active Signal ?parameter of the Signal Editor block determines which signal is selected for signal property modification. If the Apply  button is not clicked before the Active Signal ?is changed, the modified signal properties might not save correctly.
Workaround: After changing a signal property, click the Apply?button before changing the Active Signal?parameter value.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Error when generating C++ class interface code from a model reference hierarchy using parallel builds
Description: The code generation process may stop with and error out for a model hiearchy that meets these conditions: The model hierarchy is built in parallel. For all models in the hierarchy, the configuration set parameter?CodeInterfacePackaging ?has the value?C++ class .   The error is as follows: CGIR assertion 'In getComponentInterfaceForModelRef: Assertion failed: mxProxyComponentInterface()' failed in 'B:\ matlab\ src\ rtwcg\ rtwcg_utils\ rtwcg_entryfcn_mgr. cpp:108'
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Simulating a model directly after receiving the Insufficient memory budget for delay expressions history?error might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If a model involves delays, and insufficient memory budget has been allocated in the Solver Configuration block, simulating the model twice in a row might cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Do one of the following:?Close the model after receiving this error, and then reopen it before simulating again. Increase the Delay memory budget [kB] in the Solver Configuration block and then simulate again.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point division in Simulink with non-power of 2 scaling might not saturate on overflow
Description: Fixed-point division with non-power of 2 scaling might not saturate on overflow when all of these conditions are met:? The division is set to saturate on overflow. The division has rounding mode set to "Simplest" or "Zero". The input types are signed and the output type is unsigned. At least one of the input types or the output type has a slope that is not a power of 2.  Negative values will wrap on overflow instead of saturating to 0. Simulation and code generation will both wrap the results.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Destination state is not executed properly when entered from an Entry Port connected to a supertransition
Description: If the destination of a supertransition is an Entry port on a state, Stateflow does not correctly execute the state or its children. State actions might not run, and outgoing transitions might not be taken.?
Workaround: Replace the supertransition with multiple transition segments connected by Entry Ports and Junctions at each level of the state hierarchy.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model that has TLC code that updates a ground value
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code if a block has TLC code that attempts to update a built-in or alias data type ground value to an incorrect value.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Error when generating code from a model that has an atomic subsystem that interfaces with elements that have a storage class that results in macros in generated code
Description: The code generation process might produce an error when all  of the following are true: The model contains an atomic subsystem. That atomic subsystem interfaces with elements that have a storage class that results in macros in generated code.  For example, if a parameter uses the?Define ?storage class to generate a?macro?such as?#define myParam 5 ?and that parameter is connected as an input for an atomic subsystem, then the code generator might return an error similar to the following:   Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.    Caused by: Violated uniqueness constraint.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Error while running MEX functions generated for MATLAB functions creating matrices of?less than intmax() elements
Description: You might get an error while running a MEX function generated from a MATLAB function which creates a matrix that meet these conditions:You create the matrix using a function whose arguments represent the size dimensions. For example, the function NaN . Any argument corresponding to the second size or later is a variable or a function of a variable. For example, NaN(x, y) , when using the?X = nan(sz1,...,szN ) form of syntax.? The argument is not guaranteed to be a positive integer.?  You might receive the following error even if the matrix being created by the MATLAB function does not have more than?intmax() ?elements : ? ? Cannot grow a matrix with greater than?intmax()?elements.
Workaround: To create the matrix with the MATLAB function, instead of using arguments that represent size dimensions, use a vector input whose elements represent the size dimension of the output.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2024a

Summary: From Spreadsheet block parameter Extrapolation before first data point has no effect when Range parameter includes range with only header row
Description: The Range  parameter of the From Spreadsheet block can include multiple, comma-separated ranges. If one of these ranges is only the first row (e.g. A1:C1,A5:C20 ), the block ignores the?Extrapolation before first data point? parameter. The data before the range is output according to the value of the parameter Data interpolation within time range .
Workaround: Modify the Range parameter to no longer include the header row. For example, if the Range was A1:C1, A5:C20, change the range to A5:C20.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor fails to reopen when you try to load from a restore point during?NI FPGA Bitfile Generation workflow.
Description: In the?NI FPGA Bitfile Generation  workflow, when you perform certain tasks in the HDL Workflow Advisor and close the session. And if you try to reopen the HDL Workflow Advisor from the restore point again, it fails to open and may not respond. HDL Workflow Advisor throws following error: Unable to Load from restore point. Loading from restore point is not supported for 'NI FPGA Bitfile Generation'.?

Summary: Parameter tunability loss with System Mask
Description: Ability to tune the parameter in the generated code is lost for instance specific parameters on the model reference block which is referring a model with System Mask.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Training or making predictions with a?DAGNetwork?object errors for networks with Convolution2DLayer objects with non-symmetric padding
Description: When training or making predictions with a? DAGNetwork ?object, the software can throw an error for networks with?Convolution2DLayer ?objects with non-symmetric padding. Unrecognized function or variable 'iPadArray'.    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.util.ConvolutionAddReLUGPUStrategy/forward (line 28)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? X = iPadArray(X, paddingSize);  The error can happen when all of these conditions are met: The model contains a Convolution2DLayer  object with non-symmetric padding (for example, when the Padding  option is [2 2 0 1] ?or in some cases when the Padding  option  "same" ). One of these: An AdditionLayer ?object then a ReLULayer ?object immediately follow the?Convolution2DLayer ?object in the network A BatchNormalizationLayer  object, an AdditionLayer ?object, then a ReLULayer ?object immediately follow the?Convolution2DLayer ?object in the network  The?ExecutionEnvironment  training or prediction option is  "gpu"  (or  "auto"  when a GPU is available to use).  If using the prediction function, the? Acceleration  option is  "auto" .
Workaround: As a workaround, try one of these options:Add a?FunctionLayer?object with function @(X)X?after the convolution layer.If training the network, train the network using a custom training loop.If making predictions with the network, convert the network to a dlnetwork object.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Description: If you run a MIL vs. SIL equivalence test in the Test Manager and have coverage collection enabled, MATLAB might crash. The stack trace includes: fl::filesystem::remove  stm::CoverageResult::addCoverageResults

Summary: Simulink crashes when using a Model block with messages and not exposing message payload data type to top model
Description: Using a Model block with Simulink messages and not exposing the data type of the message payload to the top model cause a Simulink crash during simulation or model compilation.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Expose all the data types to the top model.Configure the Model block to run in?accelerator?mode.?

Summary: TLC error when running SIL/PIL simulation of subsystem within context of parent model
Description: When you run a SIL/PIL simulation of a subsystem within the context of its parent model, you might observe this?TLC? error: Error: File: <path_to_file ><file_name >_cgt.tlc?  Attempt to call a non-function value: <function_name >    The problem might occur when all of these conditions apply: The parent model uses a custom system target file that is derived from ert.tlc  or autosar.tlc .   The parent model uses custom file processing (CFP) templates to customize code generation.  The CFP templates invoke?TLC ? functions that are defined in the custom system target file.
Workaround: Modify the CFP template files so that the files do not invoke?TLC?functions that are defined by the custom system target file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Schedule events from C action language charts are not honored
Description: When you trigger a schedule event (events that are sent to the Schedule Editor to schedule the execution of aperiodic partitions) from a Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language nothing happens.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 1, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using a For Each Subsystem block in AUTOSAR might generate incorrect code when allowed stack size is limited
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model when all?of the following conditions are met:The model is an AUTOSAR model. The model contains a For Each Subsystem block. Signals that are connected to the For Each Subsystem introduce internal buffers. An internal buffer is larger than the Maximum stack size (bytes) ?model configuration parameter or the TLC variable?MaxStackVariableSize .  Consider this incorrect generated code.void model_Step(void){? ? sint32 ForEach_itr;? ? // ...? ? /* Inport: '<Root>/In1' */? ? (void)Rte_Read_ERIWD_ERIWD? ? (model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].tmpRead);?? ? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 500; ForEach_itr++) {? ? ? ? // ...? ? ? ? /* Update for UnitDelay: '<S1>/Unit Delay' incorporates:? ? ? ? ?* ForEachSliceSelector generated from: '<S1>/InSS'? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? ? model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE =? ? ? ? ? ?model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].tmpRead[ForEach_itr];? ? }} The code generates a temporary buffer,?tmpRead , for the ExplicitReceive?in each of the iterations, instead of only once. See the following correct generated code.void model_Step(void){? ? sint32 ForEach_itr;? ? // ...? ? /* Inport: '<Root>/In1' */? ? (void)Rte_Read_ERIWD_ERIWD? ? (model_ARID_DEF.tmpRead); ?? ? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 500; ForEach_itr++) {? ? ? ? // ...? ? ? ? /* Update for UnitDelay: '<S1>/Unit Delay' incorporates:? ? ? ? ?* ForEachSliceSelector generated from: '<S1>/InSS'? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? ? model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE =? ? ? ? ? ?model_ARID_DEF.tmpRead[ForEach_itr] ;? ? }}
Workaround: If possible, increase both the MaxStackSize?and the TLC variable?MaxStackVariableSize?to be larger than the internal buffer.If stack size is a hard requirement (for example, due to target constraints), applying some or all of the following might work around this issue.?Disable the optimization option?LocalBlockOutputs.Insert a Signal Conversion block between the For Each Subsystem block and the block that generates the internal buffer in question. Enable the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter for the Signal Conversion block.If the internal buffer is generated for root I/O due to a specific DataAccessMode (for example, ExplicitReceive), try to change the DataAccessMode.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Error aggregating coverage for top model and subsystems at different model depths
Description: When you record coverage using the Test Manager in Simulink Test for a combination of test cases for a top model and test harnesses on subsystems in the model, an error might occur when all of these conditions are met: The coverage results for the subsystems-under-test are not compatible with each other (for example, due to a change in active variants). The subsystems are found at different depths within the model (for example,?"model/subsystem1"  and "model/subsystem2/nestedSubsystem" ). The path to one of the subsystems has the same number of characters as the path to the parent ?of another subsystem-under-test.? For example, "model/subsystem1"  has same character length as "model/subsystem2"  which is the parent of subsystem-under-test "model/subsystem2/nestedSubsystem" .  When these conditions are true, you might see an error message similar to: failed assertion in B:\matlab\toolbox\slcoverage\src\slvnv\cv_mlapi.cpp at line 65:  id = ( Object )*(double*)mxGetPr(prhs[0])   You might also see the text "**** Error **** " in the Errors section of your test results in the Test Manager.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: svd?function with?certain input sizes and using cuSOLVER library
Description: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code for the?svd ?function if all  these? conditions are true: You generate?standalone code (static library, dynamically linked library, or executable program).  Input matrix to svd  has size m -by-n , where?m < n . You enable the use of the NVIDIA cuSOLVER library.  The issue occurs if the?svd ?function call is from a user-written function or? other MATLAB toolbox functions. To verify the existence of this issue in an application, enable GPU error checks in the generated code. For example, use the?SafeBuild  property of the GPU code configuration object. If the issue exists, the application might exit with a?CUSOLVER_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE ?error code .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when the function called by?gpuArray/arrayfun contains a function assigning a single output in square brackets
Description: When you apply a function using gpuArray/arrayfun ?and that function contains a call to another function that assigns a value to a single output using square brackets (i.e. using multiple left-hand-side assignment syntax to assign a single value), MATLAB crashes.  For example, the call to arrayfun ?in the following code causes MATLAB to crash : X = ones(2,"gpuArray");arrayfun(@foo,X)  function y = foo(x)[s] = local(x); % Assignment into single value using [ ] syntax causes the crash.y = s+2;end  function y = local(x)y = x*2;end
Workaround: To avoid this issue, remove the square brackets. For example:X = ones(2,"gpuArray");arrayfun(@foo,X)function y = foo(x)s = local(x); % Assign s instead of [s].y = s+2;endfunction y = local(x)y = x*2;end
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: A2L file generation might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime for a model with a Prelookup block connected to a Bus Selector block
Description: The?coder.asap2.export ?API parses data from a Simulink model and accumulates it into a structured format that A2L file generation requires. When a model has a Prelookup block connected to a Bus Selector block, arguments passed to?coder.asap2.export() ?from function calls contain incorrect data that might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime.? The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [0] 0x00007ffac9b93682 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023a\bin\win64\slcg_mf.dll+02242178 coder::descriptor::BreakpointDataInterface::Impl::getFixAxisMetadata+00000018  [1] 0x00007ffa80308e9d C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023a\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00757405 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00570029  [2] 0x00007ffa802dc3fb C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023a\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00574459 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00387083?
Workaround: Use a Prelookup block in conjunction with an Interpolation block without intermediary buses.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not produce an error for a Message Triggered Subsystem that uses a service interface
Description: The code generator incorrectly generates code instead of returning an error for a model that contains a Message Triggered Subsystem block that is configured to use a service interface.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect fixed-point information is generated in C-API file for root-level outputs
Description: The C-API file (model _capi.c) generated by the code generator may contain incorrect fixed-point information for root-level outputs present in rtwCAPI_Signals  structure. Instead of the actual number, the fxpIndex  (fixed-point index) is incorrectly assigned with zero. This indicates wrongly?that the signal does not have fixed-point information.
Workaround: Use the Code Descriptor API to get the correct data type from a MATLAB script. For example:codeDesc = coder.getCodeDescriptor("MODEL_NAME")outputPorts = codeDesc.getDataInterfaces("Outports")outputPorts.Implementation.Type
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Code generation fails with 'unhandled AUTOSAR memory category' assertion for AUTOSAR classic models
Description: During code generation for a classic AUTOSAR model, Simulink might throw an error similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'unhandled AUTOSAR memory category' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_interface_context.cpp'.  This code generation error might occur for a classic AUTOSAR model that meets the following conditions: Configuration parameter?Code interface packaging ?is set to?Reusable function . The model contains a Merge block with its output connected to a Unit Delay block. The inputs of the Merge block are from conditionally executed subsystems. For example, an Enabled Subsystem or If Action Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Loop streaming optimization may generate incorrect code for loops that write to a persistent variable
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for MATLAB code with loops when loop streaming optimizations are enabled. For example, in the code shown below, generated code would contain an extra cycle updating the state p1.function y = fcn(u1, u2) persistent p1if isempty(p1)    p1 = int32(0);endfor count = 2:100    temp = p1;    p1 = int32(3) + int32(temp)*int32(-2);endy = p1;end
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB might crash if a model is forced to close while external mode simulation is terminating
Description: During an external mode simulation, MATLAB might crash if the model window closes while the simulation is terminating. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [? 0] ExtModeOpenProtocolSendCommand in matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll  [? 1] ExtModeStopTargetSim \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll  [? 2] StopMenuSimIfNotStopped \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll  [? 3] SLRunSimulationCommand \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a reference model and signals using?Reusable?storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets?these conditions: The model contains a referenced model. The top model contains a signal that uses a Reusable ?storage class. Another signal inside of the referenced model is specified to use a Reusable ?storage class. The signal in the referenced model also uses the same variable name as the signal in the top model that uses a?Reusable ?storage class.  While generating code from a model that meets the above conditions, the code generator issues a warning message similar to the following: Warning: The 'PORT' of block 'REFMODEL' can not reuse with some other usages of the Reusable CSC 'VARNAME'.? In most cases, this will result in an additional buffer in the generated code.  The PORT , REFMODEL , and VARNAME ?refer to the port number, referenced model name, and variable name, respectively.?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Diagnostic Feature Designer app fails to open plots when parallel computation is enabled
Description: When plotting a signal trace within the Diagnostic Feature Designer (DFD) app, the app attempts to honor the Options > Use Parallel Computing  setting while reading ensemble data for the plot. If this setting is enabled when a parallel pool is not yet open in MATLAB, the DFD app opens a new parallel pool with the current cluster profile. In the scenario where opening the parallel pool fails (because of, for example, a bad cluster profile), the DFD app does not handle the error properly. Instead, the app creates a signal trace plot that is not fully constructed. This scenario might result in seemingly unrelated error messages such as Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds .?In the MATLAB Command Window, there are likely to be errors and warnings triggered by a failure in the gcp  command. These error and warning messages persist even if you subsequently disable parallel computation or fix the cluster profile. The only way to reopen the same signal trace plot is to start a new session in the DFD app.
Workaround: Clearing the Use Parallel Computing option or resolving any issues with the parallel cluster profile allows new plots in the Diagnostic Feature Designer app to be created. However, for any plots which failed, starting a new session is required to replot the variable.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model simulation and generated code might produce different results for reinitialized triggered subsystem
Description: Triggered subsystems might produce different results for model simulation and code generation when all the following conditions are true: Show subsystem reinitialize ports  parameter is enabled for the Triggered Subsystem block. For the Trigger block inside the subsystem, the?Trigger type ?parameter is set to?rising  and the Initial trigger signal state  parameter is set to?zero . The trigger signal does not trigger the execution of the subsystem and the Reinitialize Function block inside the subsystem is activated by a reinitialize function-call event.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Model Testing and Model Design Dashboards show errors for files inside a MATLAB Compiler application bundle
Description: If you use MATLAB Compiler onmacOS to create an application bundle and you place the bundle inside a projectroot folder, the Model Testing and Model Design Dashboards will try to analyze the files contained in the bundle. Since the bundle containscompiled M files, the dashboards generate theerror:Unable to parse MATLAB file

Summary: Specifying a timetable or timeseries in Satellite Scenario pointAt method results in an error if the step size of the data is less than 1e-12 seconds
Description: Specifying a timetable  or timeseries ?in the pointAt  method of Satellite ?and?Gimbal ?objects will result in an error if the step size of the data is less than 1e-12 seconds (1 picosecond).
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Cannot set excitation voltage?for bridge measurement on some devices
Description: When you try to set bridge measurement excitation voltage to 3.3 v,? the setting reverts to its last value with no indication of error. This was observed on an NI-9237, and can be reproduced using a simulated device. Related devices might also be affected.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Delay balancing can result in an error for a feedback loop through hierarchy even though clock-rate pipelining is enabled
Description: When a Simulink model has a feedback loop going through subsystem hierarchy, clock-rate pipelining might not reliably handle the full loop in the presence of minimal optimization requests (i.e., pipeline requests only exist at the top level of loop hierarchy while the state for the loop exists at a lower level). This might lead to unexpected delay balancing errors for feedback loops.
Workaround: Enabling?FlattenHierarchy?on the top level of the feedback loop hierarchy can help to resolve a delay balancing error.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unnecessary casts in the generated code for a model with symbolic dimensions
Description: The code generator might produce unnecessary casts in the generated code for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a signal,?rtu_u , ?which is a matrix with symbolic dimensions such as A*B  or A+B . A Bus Assignment block assigns rtu_u ?to a nonsymbolic element of a bus,rtu_y . A Selectorblock selects an index of rtu_u ?which an Assignment block then assigns to anindex of the nonsymbolic bus element rtu_y .??  For example, suppose rtu_u  has dimensions AxB  withvalues of 2 and 3 and rtu_y  has nonsymbolic dimensions of 2x3 .? The unnecessary cast is generated for rtu_u  when assigned to rtu_y . The incorrect generated code will show?rtu_y[0] = (*(real_T (*)[6])&rtu_u)[0]  instead of correctly showing?rtu_y[0] = rtu_u[0] .
Workaround: If symbolic dimension propagation is not required for the code generation, clear the?Allow symbolic dimension specification configuration parameter.If the symbolic dimensions are required in the generated code, then you should change the dimension of nonsymbolic signals to which the symbolic signal is assigned to its symbolic type in the model. For example, if the non-symbolic signal rtu_y with dimensions [2,3] is assigned a symbolic signal rtu_u with symbolic dimension [A,B], change the dimension of rtu_y from [2,3] to [A,B] and assign the value of A with 2 and B with 3 from base workspace to both maximum and minimum fields.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling parsim on Simulink?models?that contain Simscape blocks
Description: Under some conditions, MATLAB can crash when calling parsim  on Simulink?models?that contain Simscape blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code for the subsystem that has input port connected to the Vector Concatenate block and Scalarize Ports option is enabled.?
Description: When the input port of the subsystem is connected to the Vector Concatenate bock and?Scalarize Ports ?option is enabled, the generated HDL code for the subsystem might be incorrect.?

Summary: The problem-based workflow might error when using a Global Optimization Toolbox solver with UseParallel=true
Description: When using the problem-based workflow and selecting one of the Global Optimization solvers ga , surrogateopt , or patternsearch , with UseParallel=true , the solve  function might error. For example: x = optimvar('x',2, 'LowerBound', -5, 'UpperBound', 10);prob = optimproblem; prob.Objective = 4; prob.Constraints.con1 = x(2) <= 1;prob.Constraints.con2 =  1 <= x(1) + x(2);gaOptions = optimoptions('ga', 'UseParallel', true);x0.x = [-2 3];[xga, fga, efga, outga] = solve(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga', 'options', gaOptions); The returned error can appear as follows:? Error using fcnvectorizerFunction to evaluate must be represented as a string scalar, character vector, or function_handle object.Error in makeState (line 69)        Score = fcnvectorizer(state.Population(initScoreProvided+2:end,:),FitnessFcn,1,...Error in galincon (line 24)state = makeState(GenomeLength,FitnessFcn,Iterate,output.problemtype,options);Error in ga (line 420)            [x,fval,exitFlag,output,population,scores] = galincon(FitnessFcn,nvars, ...Error in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/callSolverError in optim.internal.problemdef.ProblemImpl/solveImplError in optim.problemdef.OptimizationProblem/solveCaused by:    Failure in user-supplied fitness function evaluation. GA cannot continue.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can use the prob2struct function to generate the function files for the objective and constraint in the current folder, and then call the solver directly on the output structure.For example:probStruct = prob2struct(prob, x0, 'solver', 'ga');probStruct.options = gaOptions;[xga, fga, efga, outga] = ga(probStruct);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using sender services on a root outport driven by an IRT subsystem might result in incorrect code.
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code when all ?of the following are true: A sender service is mapped to a root outport. That sender service uses the During execution ?or  Outside execution ?communication method.  The root outport is driven by an IRT subsystem. The output signal of the IRT subsystem is mapped to a measurement service.

Summary: MATLAB checks out Simulink Check license when displaying edit-time errors and warnings
Description: If you have Diagnostics > Edit-Time Errors & Warnings  enabled, the Simulink Editor displays model design errors and warnings while you design your model. When you click the error or warning symbol, MATLAB unnecessarily checks out a license for Simulink Check. The Edit-time Errors & Warning  option is enabled by default.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code reuse subsystem inside Initialize Function block generates incorrect initialization code
Description: A CodeReuseSubsystem block generates incorrect initialization code when all these conditions apply: The CodeReuseSubsystem block is placed inside an Initialize Function block.? The Initialize Function block is placed inside an Atomic Subsystem block. The Atomic Subsystem block is placed inside a Function-Call Subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem block is in an export-function model. The Function-Call Subsystem block executes at union rates, where one sample time unites two or more event sample times.
