Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output of accelerated simulation is incorrect for model containing data store of string type
Description: The simulation output of a model is incorrect when all  of the following conditions are true: The simulation mode is Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator. The model contains a Data Store Write block that is inside a reusable subsystem.? The Data Store Memory block that corresponds to the Data Store Write block is outside of the reusable subsystem.? The data store is of string data type.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Simulation indices of logical and temporal assessment results in equivalence tests are reversed
Description: Logical and temporal assessment results in equivalence test cases have the order and indices reversed. The symbols for Sim1 are shown in Sim2 and vice-versa, which produces incorrect results.?

Summary: Comments in custom .cgt files might not correctly appear in shared files
Description: When you generate code for a model reference hierarchy, comments in the banner or trailer of a?custom .cgt  file might not correctly appear in shared files. This can occur if the banner or trailer contains multiple comments that are separated by newlines and any ?of the following conditions are met: You delete the slprj ?directory but not the  .slxc ?files and then regenerate the code  You utilize a parallel model reference build  For example, if one of those conditions is met, this banner might generate incorrect comments because it contains multiple comment blocks that are separated by newlines: /* First banner comment */?  /* Second banner comment */
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect number of output ports for SIL block generated from subsystem
Description: A SIL block generated from a subsystem might not have the correct number of output ports when all these conditions apply: The subsystem contains a Model block. That Model block has a port that outputs a nonvirtual bus. That port corresponds with an Outport block in the referenced model.
Workaround: For each Outport block that provides a nonvirtual bus to one or more blocks in the parent subsystem, select Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect time unit conversion for the Stateflow duration operator
Description: Simulink PLC Coder generated code for Stateflow duration operator does not convert time units to step counts. Only in the case where the time unit matches the sample time of the chart the generated code is correct.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Error recording coverage for top model and subsystems that contain variants
Description: When you record coverage using Test Manager in Simulink Test for a combination of test cases for a top model and test harnesses on subsystems in the model, where the subsystem tests activate different variants, you might see an assertion failure message such as: failed assertion in B:\ matlab\ toolbox\ shared\ slcoverage\ slcoverage_core\ slvnv\ utils\ object. cpp at line 2471: id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When pulse generator sample time values are mismatched, these values are ignored in certain subsystems with periodic rates
Description: In fixed-step  models, pulse generator blocks compute their sample time from a combination of the "Period" and "Pulse Width" parameters. This computed sample time must match the sample time of its parent subsystem. However, Simulink is unable to detect this mismatch. When this mismatch occurs and is not detected, incorrect behavior will result. The incorrect behavior may look like:? Blocks which ignore the sample time specified in parent atomic subsystems. Generating incorrect code for blocks in periodic function call subsystems.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Generating code for an AUTOSAR model results in MATLAB crash due to presence of a Message Merge block
Description: Generating code for an AUTOSAR model results in a MATLAB crash if following conditions are met. The model logic creates messages, either by using Message Send blocks or using Stateflow message send ?operator. The model contains two or more of these output message lines. Message lines are merged using a Message Merge block.  The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:?? slmsg::MsgCGReservoir::constructMsgFcn slmsg::MsgCGReservoir::getValidMsgFcn?  The Message Merge block is only supported for AUTOSAR?models to simulate composition-level communication between model references. Message Merge blocks should not be used in a component model to generate code.
Workaround: Remove the Message Merge block from the AUTOSAR component model. Message Merge blocks can be used in the model hierarchy for simulation?to model RTE behavior of N:1 communication.

Summary: Custom attributes do not import from DOORS Next module when you use non-default stream
Description: When you import a DOORS Next  module into Requirements Toolbox , if you select a non-default stream for the module, the imported requirements do not contain the custom attributes defined in that stream.

Summary: Schema-based file headers written by the cdfx function are not compliant with the ASAM standard
Description: The header section of a CDFX-file produced by the cdfx  funtion contains the following statement: xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.asam.net/schema/CDF/r2.1 cdf21.xsd" This line is not compliant with the ASAM standard. The correct statement is: xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.asam.net/schema/CDF/r2.1 cdf_v2.1.0.sl.xsd"
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when using alias data type for boolean on a Relational Operator block with CastingMode set to Standards Compliance
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect answers for a model meeting the following conditions: 1. When the model contains a Relational Block and one of its inputs is fed by a Constant Block, whose value is positive, and the other input is fed by a signal with unsigned data type. 2. Either the Output Data Type of the RelationalBlock is set to AliasType with base type boolean or an outport/signal whose data type is Aliased with base type boolean or both are set to AliasType with base type boolean. 3. Casting mode parameter is set to Standards Compliant.

Summary: Running custom programs or exporting program code or arguments from the Model Analyzer app generates an error
Description: Running custom programs saved in R2019a or earlier in the?SimBiology Model Analyzer ?app might generate the following error: Operands to the logical AND (&&) and OR (||) operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.? Use the ANY or ALL functions to reduce operands to logical scalar values.? The View Program Code...  and Export Arguments for Program Code...  actions from the browser context menu might also silently fail.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Rebuilding test harness crashes MATLAB when harnessInfo.xml file is corrupted
Description: If the Simulink Identifiers (SIDs) stored in the harnessInfo.xml ?file get corrupted (for example, from a bad file merge), when you rebuild the harness, MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: Overwrite the SIDs of inConvSS/outConvSS in the harnessInfo.xml file.If the harness is saved internally, convert it into externally saved harness, which will generate the harnessInfo.xml file.Open the?harnessInfo.xml?file.In the harnessInformation section, find the corresponding harness name.Go to the BlockSIDs line and replace the corrupted SIDs with the correct ones, as needed. To find the correct SIDs of a block, select the block in the model, and use get_param(gcb,'sid') at the MATLAB command line.Save and close the?harnessInfo.xml?file.Close and reopen the harness and then rebuild it.

Summary: Subsystem block does not show all code generation parameters
Description: All of the code generation parameters are not shown for a Subsystem block when the Subsystem block has all of these settings: Treat as atomic unit  parameter? is selected. Function packaging ?is set to?Nonreusable function.  System target is not directly derived from ERT.? ?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when a custom TLC storage class interacts with inlined S-function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if asignal or state of a block downstream of inlined S-Function is? assignedto?a?storage classusing a custom TLC file , whereby?the contents  data access is requested where the set  access isexpected.? For example, get_data() ?might be generated on theleft-hand side of an assignment resulting in code such as get_data() =tmp;? instead of set_data(tmp); . The generated code might not compile depending on how the data access is implemented. A summary of the conditions are:? The model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs ?is selected Use of a storage class using a custom TLC file Use of an inlined S-Function with output set as local and reusable The storage class is not directly assigned to the S-Function output and is assigned downstream consisting of only data copy operations (that might be optimized)
Workaround: Option 1:Directly specify the storage class on the output of the inlined S-Function.Option 2:?Configure the output of the inlined S-Fuction as a test point.?Option 3:Force the output of the inlined S-Function to be global and non-reusable, usingssSetOutputPortOptimOpts(S, PortIndex,?SS_NOT_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL)Option 4:?Clear the model configuration parameter Enable local block outputs.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Display?block in referenced model does not display signal values during simulation when full path to block contains newline characters
Description: The Display block does not show signal values during simulation for a model where all of the following conditions are true: Contains a Model block that references a different model Specifies the full path to the Display block in that referenced model with newline characters
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: too few arguments for call compilation error in atomic subsystem SIL/PIL simulations
Description: An atomic subsystem SIL/PIL simulation might fail to compile xil_interface.c , producing a too few arguments for call  error.The error occurs when?xil_interface.c  invokes a subsystem function, e.g. subsystem_init , with an incorrect number of arguments and?the function argument is a pointer to an enum  data? type.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to enumeration?function with string input
Description: For MATLAB code containing calls to the enumeration  function with a string input, the generated code returns an empty matrix instead of the contents of the enumeration. This bug does not affect calls to the enumeration  function? using character vector input.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef LightColors < uint8    enumeration        red(1)        yellow(2)        green(3)    endendfunction out = getColors()out = enumeration("LightColors");end? In MATLAB execution, calls to getColors  return the contents of the enumeration. If you generate a MEX for getColors , calls to getColors_mex  incorrectly return an empty matrix.? ?This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, replace string input to the?enumeration?function with character vector input.For example, in the code in the bug description, replace double-quoted input to the?enumeration?function ("LightColors") with single-quoted input ('LightColors').?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Adding justification for the violations of the check?mathworks.hism.hisl_0011?does not work as expected
Description: When you add a justification to any one of the violations of an element for the check?Check usage of Switch Case blocks and Switch Case Action Subsystem blocks  (Check ID:?mathworks.hism.hisl_0011), it results in the addition of the same justification to all the violations of that element.

Summary: Simulink might include incorrect header file in the generated code when multiple models share an imported enumeration type that has inconsistent?HeaderFile?property values across different models
Description: Simulink might include an incorrect header file in the generated code when all ?of the following are true: You configure more than one Simulink model use the shared utilities.  Multiple models use an enumeration type that has the DataScope ?property set to Imported .   Multiple models use different HeaderFile ?property values for the enumeration type.  The enumeration type is not used by any of the shared utilities.  The enumeration type is used in the generated files in the?model  folder.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing bus?signals that have array elements?with inplace update specifications
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a bus signal that has an array element. For example,  ? ? ? ?? ? ? ? ?struct {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? int val[10];  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? }? BusA;  The elements of the bus signal are updated inplace by using one of these methods: Defining a MATLAB function interface in the form of y = foo(y) . Defining a?Reusable ?storage class and using the same variable name for the input and output of the update operation. Using function prototype control to specify the same name for the input and output of the update operation when the input and output of the update operation are the root input and output, respectively.   If your model satisfies the above conditions, for a defined bus X ? of type?BusA , the code generator might incorrectly?eliminate an assignment operation like the following: ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?X.val[index] = X.val[index -1];  // For arbitrary index values.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when attempting to import a third-party FMU with scalar internal variables of String?data type
Description: MATLAB crashes when attempting to import a third-party FMU that includes scalar variables of type String  defined as Internal Variables in the original model used for FMU generation.

Summary: Custom folders in sl_customization.m file are not visible in the?Library pane of Model Advisor Configuration Editor (MACE)
Description: When you add custom folders to the sl_customization.m  file, they are not visible as additional tabs in the Library  pane of the Model Advisor Configuration Editor (MACE).

Summary: MATLAB is frozen when user tries to open the library file.
Description: Consider there are two libraries, library1 and library2 and a block is moved from library2 to library1. There is circular reference when library1 is opened after moving the block for these settings: The link for moved block in library1 is disabled and the disabled link (or AncestorBlock parameter) still provides reference to library1. Replacing the block in library2 with block from library1 creates active link to library2.  Operation performed: library2/block? ? ? ? -move->? ? ? library1  library2/block? ? ? ? -move->? ? ? library1/block (disable link)?  library2/block? ?<-replace-with-? ?library1/block (disabled link)   Result:? library2/block? ? -active-link-to->? ? library1/block  library1/block? ?-inactive-link-to->? ?library2/block  The go to library functionality shows the circular reference for these blocks. This circular reference causes MATLAB to freeze in infinite loop.
Workaround: Perform the break link operation on the moved block in library1 and then open library2 to replace the block with instance from library1. The recommended workflow to move a block from one library to another library is to break the link for the block after moving it to new library.library2/block? ?-move->? ?library1/block (break link)

Summary: Exporting a Simulink model to Standalone FMU errors when there are leading or trailing whitespace and newline characters in port block names
Description: Exporting a Simulink model to Standalone FMU might result in an error under the following conditions:? The inport or outport block names in the Simulink model have leading or trailing whitespace characters. The inport or outport block names in the Simulink model have leading or trailing newline characters.  When you try to export the model, Simulink might show an error similar to:? ? The specified key is not present in this container.

Summary: Error during update diagram or simulation when a referenced model contains Initialize Function block?connected to inactive root outport due to variants
Description: Simulink model containing a model block may throw an error during update diagram or simulation when the referenced model contains Initialize Function  block connected to root outport and can become inactive due to variant source/sink. Sample error message seen:? "Error:The Outport block <blockPath>' must be virtual. However, Simulink determined that a signal buffer is required at some point along the path from the source output port. Ensure that the connection from the source output port to the Outport block is a straight full port connection without any element reordering or selection. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.  Caused by:  ? ? The Outport block <block>' must be virtual because its source signal originates from an Initialize, Terminate, Reinitialize or Reset Function block. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.
Workaround: Ensure that the Outport block?originating from an Initialize Function block within the referenced model does not have any variant condition.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Array indexing might be incorrect for inplace MATLAB functions that accept variable-size arrays as inputs
Description: Incorrect code might generate from a MATLAB function when all of the following conditions are true: The MATLAB function is an inplace function (that is, uses the same input and output variable) whose input is a variable-size array. Inside the MATLAB function, an inplace operation changes the size of the input/output variable. The code in the MATLAB function performs some inplace operations on the input/output variable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You generate code that uses N-dimensional indexing by setting the?PreserveArrayDimensions ?property to  true .  In R2021b or later,  EnableImplicitExpansion  is turned off.  Consider this MATLAB function that uses the same input and output variable x  and accepts a variable-size array as an input. The size of x ?is changed inside of the function through an inplace operation. function x = foo(x)? ? ? ? % In-place function with variable-sized input/output x = x(1,4:1:5);? ? ? ? ? ? % The size of x is changed  x = x + (x + ones(1,2));? ?% Inplace operation  end   When you configure the code generator to use row-major array layout and N-dimensional indexing,?you may see these code lines in the generated code: x_data[0] += x_data[0] + 1.0;  x_data[1] = x_data[0] + (x_data[0] + 1.0);   The generated code calculates the value assigned to? x_data[1] ?using?x_data[0] ?incorrectly.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrectcode generated for Weighted Sample Time andWeighted Sample Time Math blocks for?add and subtract operations with large numbers
Description: Incorrect code generated for the?Weighted Sample Time?and?Weighted Sample Time Math?blocks under these conditions: Operation  parameter is set to +  or - .? Input signal is a  large number. Input signal type is set to either double  or single . Output data type  is set to a fixed-point data?type.  For example, if a Weighted Sample Time?or Weighted Sample Time Math block has these settings:Operation ?parameter is set to?+ .   Input signal value is set to  1e+20 .  Input signal data type is set to?single. Output datatype is set to fixdt(1,32,12) .   The output in the generated code does not match that of the normal simulation output.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Model Maintainability Dashboard and metrics might return incorrect results for Simulink decision count and Simulink cyclomatic complexity
Description: The Model Maintainability Dashboard and metric results for the metrics?slcomp.SimulinkDecisions , slcomp.SimulinkDecisionDistribution , and?slcomp.SLCyclomaticComplexity ?might return values that are too low? because the metrics do not include Simulink decisions from certain block types.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Accelerator mode simulation results when Stateflow chart contains a Parameter Writer block
Description: When you use?Accelerator  mode to simulate a model with the Parameter Writer block in a Stateflow chart, the simulation results are not correct because Simulink does not execute the Parameter Writer block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Binary operations might incorrectly expand singleton dimensions of dlarray operands
Description: The generated code might produce an incorrectly sized dlarray ?output,?if all  these conditions are true: A binary operation such as +, -, .*, etc. is performed on input?dlarray ?objects  For this operation, one operand is a formatted dlarray  and the other is an unformatted dlarray  The unformatted dlarray  operand was previously permuted  The formatted  dlarray ?operand has singleton dimensions? The corresponding dimensions in the unformatted  dlarray  operand are non-singleton   The following example produces a 1 x 4 x 5 x 4 shaped dlarray  instead of a 1 x 1 x 5 x 4 shaped dlarray . out = permute(dlarray(ones(1,4,1,1)),[3 4 1 2]) + dlarray(ones(1,1,5,4),'SSCB');?
Workaround: Extract the dlarray?objects before performing binary operations.For example, modify the code in the bug description as follows:out = extractdata(permute(dlarray(ones(1,4,1,1)),[3 4 1 2])) + extractdata(dlarray(ones(1,1,5,4),'SSCB'));?out = dlarray(out, 'SSCB');?

Description: With the NI-RFSG driver installed, when you connect to a signal generator using the Wireless Waveform Generator App, either of the following might occur when you select the Transmitter tab: The app becomes unresponsive (the interface becomes greyed out).  The following error displays in the MATLAB command window: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for eventSelectedTabChanged defined for classwirelessWaveformGenerator.ToolstripWithoutMainTab:Error using matlab.ui.control.internal.model.AbstractStateComponent/set.Items'Items' must be a 1-D cell array of character vectors or a string array.Error in wirelessWaveformGenerator.transmitter.InstrumentDialog/updateDriver (line 295)      obj.DriverGUI.(obj.DropdownValues) = names; % update the GUIError in wirelessWaveformGenerator.transmitter.InstrumentDialog/findHardware (line 245)        obj.updateDriver(res{1});Error in wirelessWaveformGenerator.waveformGenerator/selectedTabChangedCallbackError in wirelessWaveformGenerator.waveformGenerator>@(src,event)selectedTabChangedCallback(obj,src,event)Error in matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip.TabGroup/sendSelectedTabChangedEvent (line 297)            this.notify('SelectedTabChanged',data);Error in matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip.TabGroup/PropertySetCallback (line 347)                    sendSelectedTabChangedEvent(this,old_tab,new_tab);Error in matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip.base.PeerInterface>@(event,data)PropertySetCallback(this,event,data) (line 53)                this.Peer.addEventListener('propertySet', @(event, data) PropertySetCallback(this, event, data));> In matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip/TabGroup/sendSelectedTabChangedEvent (line 297)In matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip/TabGroup/PropertySetCallback (line 347)In matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip.base.PeerInterface>@(event,data)PropertySetCallback(this,event,data) (line 53)

Summary: Very slow importing of Simulink Test results containing temporal assessments
Description: It takes a very long time to import?Simulink Test results that contain many temporal assessments or temporal assessments for long-running simulations.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Error when running SIL/PIL simulation of subsystem within context of parent model that is mapped to AUTOSAR component
Description: When you run a SIL or PIL simulation of a subsystem within the context of a parent model that is mapped to an AUTOSAR component, you might observe this type of error: Unable to run SIL or PIL simulation for subsystem pathToSubsystem .?This error can occur if parent model cannot be compiled or subsystem contains blocks that are not supported for SIL and PIL simulations. For example, subsystem contains a Simulink Function block that is called by a Stateflow chart. ?  The error can occur when non-built-in data types, e.g. an enumeration, are present at the subsystem interface and the header file for the data type is not set to Rte_Type.h .
Workaround: For the non-built-in data types used at the subsystem interface, set the header file field to Rte_Type.h. You can use this approach even if the data types are internal and not used at the interface of the AUTOSAR component.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Issues validating checks with updated Input Parameters
Description: When you use Model Advisor configuration files containing checks with updated Input Parameters , the JSON configuration files become corrupted, and the System tests do not run.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation when an enum type changed between builds
Description: Generating code for a model with an enum type that has changed since the previous build might cause MATLAB to crash.? For example, MATLAB might crash if: The properties of a Simulink.data.dictionary.EnumTypeDefinition  object change between builds. The Simulink.defineIntEnumType  function changes the properties of an enum type between builds.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for A(idx,:) = []?does not remove the intended elements if?A is N-D array (N>2) and idx is logical column vector or matrix
Description: The generated code does not remove the intended elements from the array?A ?if all  ?these conditions are true:? Your MATLAB code contains patterns like A(idx,:) = [] ?or ?A(idx,:,:) = [] .  A  is an array of dimension greater than or equal to 3. idx  is a logical array. idx  is either a column vector or a matrix. (This bug does not occur if idx  is a row vector.)  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Convert the logical array idx?to a row vector.For example, replace the code line?A(idx,:) = []?with the following two lines:?idx2 = reshape(idx,1,[]);A(idx2,:) = [];
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Inactive choices of a VariantSubsystem are built with inherited update diagram?activation time with?Model Reference?as choices
Description: The inactive choices within a Variant Subsystem get built when each of the following holds true:The Variant Activation time  of the Variant Subsystem is inherit from Simulink.VariantControl . The Variant Subsystem block has its choices as Model Reference  blocks. The ActivationTime  of the corresponding Simulink.VariantControl is update diagram .  You may also notice additional folders within the slprj  directory, corresponding to the inactive choices.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for dataflow subsystems configured for concurrent tasking with a user specified stack size
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that contains a Dataflow Subsystem block and meets these conditions: The configuration parameter Allow tasks to execute concurrently on targets  is selected. The Maximum stack size (bytes)  value is specified.  In the generated code, some local variables might be promoted to global variables to comply with the stack size limit. If these global variables are accessed concurrently by multiple threads, it can potentially lead to race conditions.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, increase the stack size value or do not specify a stack size in Maximum stack size (bytes) field.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unsupported export-function modeling patterns generate incorrect code instead of throwing an error
Description: The code generator incorrectly generates code instead of throwing an error when all ?of the following are true: Export-function code is being generated. A root inport is connected to a referenced model. The root inport has function access (for example, the GetSet  storage class). The referenced model signal line to which the root inport is connected to drives a Function-Call Subsystem and one or more Function-Call Subsystem, Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Functions.  For example, consider a top model, top , that includes a referenced model, bot . The referenced model has a reset function, reset , and a function call subsystem, fcn1 . A root inport, In1 , is connected to reset  and fcn1 . The code generator produces this code for the top model:? void top_reset(void)  {  ? bot_reset(&top_B.Access_In1_to_Model,?&top_Y.Out1[0]);  }  void fcn1(void)?  { ? ? bot_fcn1(&top_B.Access_In1_to_Model,?&top_Y.Out1[0]);  }   The generated functions do not assign a valid value to? top_B.Access_In1_to_Model  before passing it to the  bot_reset  and  bot_fcn1  functions. The generated function should assign a value by using an access function.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Top-level model with multiple instances of same referenced model with a Parameter Writer block might produce wrong simulation results
Description: Simulation results might be wrong for a top-level model with multiple instances of the same referenced model when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model has a Parameter Writer block that writes to a built-in block parameter, mask parameter, or Model block instance parameter. The Parameter Writer block is connected directly or indirectly to a root-level Inport block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL FIFO block might generate incorrect output values when the Reset and Pop signals are asserted at the same time.
Description: The simulation and generated HDL code for HDL FIFO block output incorrect values when you use the following settings for the block: Reset and Pop signals are asserted at the same time.? Duration of Reset signal is equal to one time-step.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Compilation error in C++ generated code due to the absence of namespace in C-API file
Description: Generated C++ code from a Simulink model that contains a referenced model or is configured to generate reusable code for the top model might produce a compilation error stating use of an undeclared identifier.  The error is due to the absence of a namespace in the second argument of the function model_InitializeDataMapInfo  in C-API file (model_capi.h ).

Summary: Assertion error when sending events that could cause recursive execution from transitions connected to exit or entry ports
Description: If the effects of sending an event cause a transition path to terminate execution, and that transition path is connected to an exit or entry port, Stateflow might throw an assertion error. For example, if the sending of the event causes a different state to be active, early return logic will terminate the execution of that transition path.

Summary: Network latency mismatch between deep learning processor profiler and estimator results
Description: When using certain sizes of the fully-connected (FC) layer, the estimated FC layer latency may be around 400 cycles different from the profiler results. The total network latency may also differ depending on the number of FC layers used. For example, an LSTM that contains four FC layers could have a difference of 400 cycles per FC layer, leading to a total network latency difference of 1600 cycles."

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink parameter with non-Auto storage class might be incorrectly overwritten in initialize function when used to specify constant value of bus element signal connected to the outport of a conditionally-executed subsystem.
Description: The code generator might dynamically initialize a statically-initialized Simulink parameter when?all ?of the following are true: A Simulink.Parameter  is used as a constant value for a constant block. This Constant block?is connected as an input port of a Bus Creator block. The output port of the Bus Creator block is driving an Outport block of a conditionally-executed subsystem such as Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem. The parameter Source of initial output value  of the Outport block is set to 'Input signal' .  When the above conditions are met, Simulink might generate code similar to the following. /* Exported block parameters */  uint8_T myParam = 15U;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* Variable: myParam?  /* Model initialize function */  void simplify1_initialize(void)  {   /* SystemInitialize for Outport: '<S1>/Raw_Bytes' incorporates:   ?*? Constant: '<S2>/Const_dummy_Byte_8'   */   ?myParam = simplify1_ConstP.Raw_Bytes_Y0.Raw_Byte_8;?    }?   Note that the Simulink.Parameter  myParam   is statically initialized with value  15U , but it gets dynamically initialized? incorrectly?to some other value?inside the model initialize function.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When using a storage class with?Data Access set to Function and Allowed Access set to Write-only, the generated write method ignores the identifier naming rule
Description: The code generator generates code that does not honor the identifier naming rule when all ?of the following are true: You use a storage class that has Data Access ?set to Function  That storage class also has Allowed Access ?set to Write-only   For example, consider a model that as an import testa , a Gain block that has a value of 2, and an outport  testb . The outport uses  Write-only ?access and the Name of Setter ?identifier naming rule is  Write-$N . The code generator produces this code:  ?set_rttestb_value(2.0 * get_rttesta());   The pseudocode?preview shows this set function: void Write_DATANAME(CONST DATATYPE DATANAME);   The pseudocode preview honors the identifier naming rule, but the generated code does not.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Debugging Stateflow chart with dangling transitions causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you update or run a model containing a Stateflow chart where all of the following conditions are true: The chart contains a?dangling transition  You have set a breakpoint on any state or transition in the chart  The generated stack trace refers to sf_cdr.dll.
Workaround: Delete all dangling transitions. They are illegal and ignored by Stateflow.?

Summary: Error when generating code from a model with Simulink Function configured with return argument
Description: The code generation process might produce an error for a model that meets all ?of these conditions: The model contains a Simulink Function for which a customized C/C++  Function Interface is present with return argument as nonvoid data.? The Array layout  model configuration parameter is set to Row-major . At least one of the arguments for the Simulink Function is a matrix or array.  The error resembles this:  CGIR assertion 'In checkUntyped: Assertion failed: !UntypedType::isa(src)' failed in 'B:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\lowering\majorityLowering\MajorityLowerType.cpp:1132
Workaround: Change the Array layout model configuration parameter to?Column-major.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB fails to authenticate SSH connections using RSA keys to remote machines running OpenSSH server 8.8 onwards
Description: When you authenticate an SSH connection to a remote machine with an identity file in the RSA format, MATLAB Parallel Server fails to establish the connection and produces an error like the following: Identity file not accepted by remote host?'machine32.mycompany.com'. Check the identity file is valid and has correct permissions.  Error from library: Username/PublicKey combination invalid  This error occurs when authenticating an SSH connection to a machine running OpenSSH server version 8.8 or later. The RSA private key might work when you authenticate using OpenSSH client on the command line. A private key is in RSA format if it begins with the following line: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY -----
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a private key in a format other than RSA, such as Ed25519.Use a password to authenticate.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Reading the BLE descriptor data on macOS version 12.0 or above can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you?read the descriptor data?on a bluetooth low energy device?on macOS version 12.0 or above, MATLAB crashes. For example, the following code causes this crash: b = ble("device"); c = characteristic(b,"Service","Characteristic");d = descriptor(c,"Descriptor") read(d)
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP?option for an S-Function block is not honored when another such S-Function block is placed inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy
Description: When you specify the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for an S-Function block, the block is supposed to be placed before other blocks in the sorted list. However, if you specify the same option for another S-Function block and place it inside a virtual subsystem at the same level of model hierarchy as the S-Function block outside the subsystem, the SS_OPTION_PLACE_ASAP  option for the S-Function block outside the subsystem is not honored.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Updating model containing Stateflow charts and custom code might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when updating a Simulink model where all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple Stateflow charts The model has custom code specified in its?Simulation Target  configuration parameters Simulation Target ?> ?Advanced parameters >? Import custom code ?is selected  The generated access violation stack trace refers to "CG_Obj::propagateTags ".
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Property Inspector in App Designer is blank in Japanese locale
Description: After dragging a component from the Component Library onto the App Designer canvas, the Property Inspector is blank.
Workaround: Set the Help language to English. On the Home tab, in the Environment section, click?Preferences?and select MATLAB > Help > Language?> English?and restart MATLAB. Then, to view the documentation in Japanese, use the translated online documentation.

Summary: Code generation might fail with 'Invariant Transpose Typing?violated by object' assertion
Description: Code generation for MATLAB code that does not contain a matrix transpose expression might result in a crash with the following message: Invariant Transpose Typing violated by object : transpose output type has dimension of wrong size  The intermediate optimizations might produce a matrix transpose expression and an internal check incorrectly flags the dimensions of the expression.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: 3-D View does not update Feed/Via/Load and Dielectric Layers
Description: In the PCB Antenna Designer App, 3-D view of a PCB antenna model retains the previously rendered feed, via, load and dielectric layers even after deleting them from the tree/2-D Canvas.
Workaround: Save the PCB antenna model and open the saved model in a new app instance then view the model. This correctly shows the updated model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when a block with initial conditions is connected to an Assignment block having an?external index source
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect initialization code for a model containing a block with initial conditions when all  of these conditions are true:?? The block with initial conditions is connected to the?Input Data Port? (U ) of an Assignment block. The Assignment block is configured to have an external index port by setting the?Index Option? parameter? to?Index vector (port) ?or?Starting index (port) . The external index port is connected to an external indices source.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: reshape function with dlarray input
Description: Code generated for the?reshape ?function that is passed a?dlarray ?input might return incorrect answer if?all ?these conditions are true: The size argument of reshape  is a row vector. The number of elements of this row vector is equal to the number of dimensions of the input.? One or more elements of this row vector?are identical to the sizes of certain dimensions of the dlarray  input.  For example, this code might produce incorrect answer in code generation: dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, [1 3 20]);
Workaround: Call the extractdata function on the input before calling the reshape function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = dlarray(reshape(extractdata(dlx), [1 3 20])); Alternatively, pass the size arguments as a comma separated list to the?reshape?function.dlx = dlarray(rand(3,4,5), 'SCB'); y = reshape(dlx, 1, 3, 20);
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Calling visastatus on a VISA-USB device causes Query Unterminated?device error
Description: When connected to a USB device using the visadev  interface, calling the visastatus  function on it incorrectly reads the status multiple times. This causes the device to issue a Query Unterminated  error, which results in a MATLAB error. The visastatus  function behaves as expected for all other?visadev  interfaces (TCP/IP, GPIB, Serial, VXI, and PXI) by reading the status a single time.

Summary: Error thrown during code generation when a Simulink Function or Function Caller has a custom function prototype defined or called in a Stateflow chart.
Description: When the function prototype for a Simulink Function or Function Caller is customized for a function that is defined or called from a Stateflow chart, an error is thrown during code generation. Prior to R2022a, customizing the function prototype for Simulink Functions and Function Callers was not supported. In R2022a and later, you can customize the function prototype from the context menu of the Simulink Function or Function Caller block by selecting?C/C++ Code > Configure C/C++ Function Interface .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer and Model Builder apps do not warn when opening a project saved in a later release
Description: SimBiology does not support forward compatibility of its project format. The apps might try to load projects that were saved in later versions without displaying any warnings or errors, but the integrity of the project cannot be guaranteed and the project data structures might become inconsistent with any particular version.? You should not attempt to load projects saved in later versions of SimBiology Model Builder  and SimBiology Model Analyzer .
Workaround: You can save and export models to MAT-files and use them in any release from R2019b and later.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Automatic parallelization of for loops containing saturating reduction operations on integer types
Description: This issue affects the generated code if all  these conditions are true:?? Your MATLAB code contains a for  loop that contains reduction operations on integer data? The configuration parameters Enable automatic parallelization? (EnableAutoParallelization ) and Optimize reductions  ( OptimizeReductions ) are enabled  The configuration parameter? Saturate on integer overflow ?(SaturateOnIntegerOverflow )?is enabled  In such situations, the code generator produces parallel?for  loops that perform arithmetic reduction operations on variables of saturating integer type. However, saturating integer reduction operations are not associative because the final result of several such operations depend on the order in which the threads execute. This might cause the concurrent code to produce numerically incorrect results when compared with the results produced by the sequential code.
Workaround: Do any one of the following:?Disable the configuration parameter?Optimize reductions?(OptimizeReductions). However, this action disables parallelization of for loops that contain any type of reduction operations - arithmetic, logical, min, max.If you can identify the loop that might cause this issue, you can disable parallelization of that specific loop by adding the coder.loop.parallelize('never') pragma immediately before that loop in your MATLAB code. For example,coder.loop.parallelize('never');for ii = 1:numel(arr1)    var = var + arr1(ii);end
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code for For Each blocks which have silent vector conversion on their inputs

The For Each Subsystem block may convert the vector before partitioning if these conditions are not met: If a For Each Subsystem is partitioning a row vector, then the Partition Dimension ?parameter must be set to 2 . If a For Each Subsystem is partitioning an unoriented or column vector, then the Partition Dimension  parameter?must be set to 1 .

Summary: Instrumentation overhead of PIL execution-time profiling not removed for target applications that use downward timers
Description: If the instrumentation overhead for execution-time profiling is estimated by a benchmark program at the start of a processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation, the computed overhead is null for target applications that use downward timers. Therefore, the reported execution times for the generated code includes the overhead associated with code instrumentation. For PIL target applications that use upward timers, the benchmark program calculates the code instrumentation overhead correctly.?
Workaround: You can use the target package to specify values for the code instrumentation overhead. For more information, see?Remove Instrumentation Overheads from Execution Time Measurements.

Summary: Build process might fail if the generated code contains class properties whose names match with other class names
Description: Generated C++ code might fail to build if the name of a user-defined class property matches with the name of another class. For example, consider this generated C++ code:? typedef class {}conflict;class someclass{    int conflict;    conflict b[2]; };? If you try to build the generated code, the code generator issues this error: ?error: 'conflict' does not name a type |     conflict b[2]; |           ^~~~~~~~ Compiler returned: 1? The build failure happens because the code generator does not rename the fields that are of a pointer or matrix type.

Summary: Entropy weight optimizer of rlSACAgent is reset when the agent is saved during training
Description: The entropy weight used in rlSACAgent  controls the balance between exploitation and exploration. It is automatically adjusted if LearnRate  of EntropyWeightOptions  is positive. The agent uses an optimizer to update the entropy weight. However, this optimizer is incorrectly reset every time the agent is saved during training (the agent is saved when SaveAgentCriteria  of rlTrainingOptionsis  is different than none ). Specifically, when the agent is saved, a new entropy weight optimizer is created using the?Algorithm ?and OptimizerParameters ?properties in?EntropyWeightOptions , hence any internal information in the optimizer from the previous episode is discarded. As a consequence, the training results will differ between a training session in which the agent is not saved and one in which the agent is saved.
Workaround: You can use one of the following workarounds to avoid the issue:Do not save the agent during training. Set SaveAgentCriteria and SaveAgentValue in rlTrainingOptions to none.Use a fixed entropy weight. Set LearnRate of EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to 0, and adjust EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions. Try 0.5, 0.2, or 0.1 for?EntropyWeight,?and increase this value if the agent needs more exploration, or reduce this value if more agent exploitation is desired.Use rlTD3Agent instead of rlSACAgent. TD3 agents are generally competitive with SAC agents.

Summary: polyspace-configure fails with?error: Invalid cross-device link
Description: if you run polyspace-configure  on Linux, the command might fail when copying files from the Polyspace installation folder to the system temporary folder (/tmp ) and you see an error message similar to this: ERROR: Could not copy "<installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test" to "/tmp/polyspace_configure_tmp-.../target.cpp": Invalid cross-device link  The issue occurs when the Polyspace installation folder and the /tmp  folder are mounted on two different filesystems, and has been observed with NFSv4.2.
Workaround: To work around this issue try one of these options:Install Polyspace on the same filesystem as the /tmp folder, or use the option -tmp-path to specify a temporary folder path that points to a location that is on the same filesystem as the Polyspace installation folder. For instance:polyspace-configure -tmp-path <path on same filesystem as installation> ...Create a folder on the same filesystem as your Polyspace installation, manually copy files to that folder, and use the option -tmp-path to specify the path to that folder:mkdir mytmpcp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/target.ccp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/target.cppcp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/sniff_implicit_includes.ccp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/sniff_implicit_includes.cpppolyspace-configure -tmp-path mytmp -keep-all-files ...Option?-keep-all-files keeps the temporary folder instead of deleting it after each run. When specify this option,?polyspace-configure?skips the copying of files after the first run.

Summary: Entropy weight of rlSACAgent is reset when the agent is saved during training
Description: The entropy weight controls the balance between exploitation and exploration. It is automatically adjusted if LearnRate  of EntropyWeightOptions  is positive.  However, the entropy weight of rlSACAgent  is incorrectly reset every time the agent is saved during training. The agent is saved when SaveAgentCriteria  of rlTrainingOptionsis  is different than none . When the agent is saved, the entropy weight is reset to the EntropyWeight  value in EntropyWeightOptions.  This incorrect behavior can lead to poor training results depending on the value of EntropyWeight  in EntropyWeightOptions . The training results will differ between a training session in which the agent is not saved and one in which the agent is saved.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, use one of the following workarounds:Do not save the agent during training. Set?SaveAgentCriteria?and?SaveAgentValue?in?rlTrainingOptions?to?none.Set EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to a smaller value such as 0.5, 0.2, 0.1 or 0.01, so that the entropy weight is reset to these values, and it does not encourage too much exploration.Use a fixed entropy weight. Set LearnRate of EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to 0, and adjust EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions. Try 0.5, 0.2, or 0.1 for?EntropyWeight,?and increase this value if the agent needs to explore more, or reduce the value if the agent needs to explore less.Use rlTD3Agent instead of rlSACAgent. TD3 agents are generally competitive with SAC agents.

Summary: Model Advisor Check Compliance metrics report incorrect check result status for child models
Description: When a Model Advisor Check Compliance metric executes on a model that references other models, the metric can report an incorrect result status for the checks run on the child model. Model Advisor Check Compliance metrics include the Model Advisor Check Compliance metric for High-Integrity Systems, the Model Advisor Check Compliance metric for MAB, and custom metrics that use the family ID mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckCompliance .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: polyspace-configure unable to extract compiler configuration
Description: When you run polyspace-configure  to extract your build configuration, and use gcc  or a gcc -based compiler, Polyspace cannot compute the compiler predefined macros, implicit include folders, or target characteristics. The command console output or log indicates a message similar to this: ERROR: Compiler error when trying to build a simple file before sniffing implicit includes WARNING: The predefined include folder could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project. WARNING: The target characteristics could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project.WARNING: The predefined macros could not be computed and might result in an incomplete project.   These issues occur because?polyspace-configure  incorrectly parses the compiler command option --sysroot .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, open file?<polyspaceRoot>/configure/compiler_configuration/gcc.xml in a text editor and replace<opt numArgs="1">-sysroot</opt>with:<opt numArgs="1">--sysroot</opt>where, <polyspaceRoot> is your Polyspace installation folder, for instance,?C:\Program Files\Polyspace\R2022b.

Summary: Incorrect results for a model containing an Observer Reference when both the models have?floor or ceil rounding operations
Description: When a model containsan Observer Reference ?block such that both the design model as well as the observermodel logic involves performing floor  and/or ceil  truncating operations, thenSimulink Design Verifier analysis on the design model may ignore suchoperations on one of the two models thereby providing incorrect analysisresults.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a

Summary: Thermostatic Expansion Valve (2P) results in compilation error when the Valve parameterization parameter is Tabulated data - quadrant diagram and the Bulb temperature dynamics parameter is On
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Thermostatic Expansion Valve (2P) where the Valve parameterization ?parameter is Tabulated data - quadrant diagram  and the Bulb temperature dynamics  parameter is On  results in the following error message: ?Invalid use of non-parametric value 'T_evap'.  All entities referenced in this location must be parameters.?

Summary: Generating PDF report containing nonprinting control characters in document heading or caption crashes MATLAB
Description: If you generate a PDF report that contains nonprinting control characters in a document heading, the following error occurs and MATLAB crashes. Unsupported control characters include characters with ASCII codes between 1 and 31, except horizontal tab (ASCII code 9), line feed (ASCII code 10), and carriage return (ASCII code 13). Error using mlreportgen.internal.fop.FOPObject/executeCannot convert "C:/workspace/root.fo" to "C:/workspace/test.pdf". Reason: Invalid Unicode character: U+001B? ? If the nonprinting character appears in a table of contents or list of captioned report elements, such as figure captions or table titles, MATLAB crashes.? For example, the following code adds the escape character to a document. MATLAB crashes when the document is closed. d = mlreportgen.dom.Document("output");append(d,mlreportgen.dom.TOC);append(d,mlreportgen.dom.Heading1(char(27))); close(d);
Workaround: Remove unsupported characters from the input string before adding the string to a DOM component or document. For example,inputChar = ['line feed: ',char(10),'escape character: ',char(27)];supportedChars = [9, 10, 13]; % Horizontal tab, line feed, and carriage return are supportedtoRemove = ~ismember(inputChar,supportedChars) & (inputChar < 32);inputChar(toRemove) = []
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong code generated when?Simulink.Signal object is attached to string signal
Description: Simulink.Signal  does not support string ?data type. However, Simulink fails to return an error if a Simulink.Signal  object with DataType  set to?auto ?is attached to a string signal in a model. Instead, the generated code produces wrong answers.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: C++ compilation error due to assignment operations to this pointer
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate assignment operations to this  pointer when generating C++ code from MATLAB code that satisfies all  of these conditions: The MATLAB code contains a handle class. The handle class has a non-static class method. The class method contains an arguments block  . The first argument declaration in the?arguments?block  ?includes a specification of the size of the argument.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef MyClass < handle  ??? ...   ??? methods   ??? ...   ??????? function Foo(obj)  ? ????????? arguments   ? ? ? ????????? obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}  ? ? ? ????? end   ? ????????? ...   ??????? end   ??? end   end   MATLAB Coder generates this C++ code for the above MATLAB code:   void MyClass::Foo()  {  ??? this = handle_init();  ??? ...  }   In the generated code,? the first argument of the non-static class method in the MATLAB code is represented as?this  pointer, ?and an assignment operation happens to the pointer.?  Assignment to this  pointer ?is not allowed in C++. MSVC gives the following error message: error C2106: '=': left operand must be l-value  GCC gives the following error message: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment   Clang gives the following error message:  error: expression is not assignable
Workaround: Remove the size specification from argument declarations in the arguments block.? In the MATLAB code example given above, replacing the code line obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}?with?obj {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")} resolves the error.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?predict?function might produce incorrect results if 'CU' inputs are passed to dlnetwork
Description: Code generated for the dlnework ?object function?predict  might produce incorrect results if all  of these conditions are true:? Target library is 'cudnn '. The dlnetwork  does not have any input layer. For example, it has been initialized with example inputs. The data format of the?dlarray  you pass to predict ?is 'CU '.  Here is an example workflow that produces an incorrect answers: % Create example entry point function 'mpredict'function out = mpredict(matFile, in) %#codegennet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork(matFile);out = predict(net, in);end% Define dlnetwork objectexampleInput = dlarray(ones(5,'single'),'CU');dlnet = dlnetwork(reluLayer, exampleInput);save('trainedNet.mat','dlnet');% Define input types to entry-point function 'mpredict'I = randi(255,[5,5]);dlIn1 = dlarray(single(I),'CU');Inputs = {coder.Constant('trainedNet.mat'), dlIn1};% Generate codecfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('cudnn');codegen mpredict -config cfg -args Inputs
Workaround: Do either of these:When specifying function input types in the codegen command, change the data format from 'CU' to 'CB'.?Change target library from 'cudnn' to 'tensorrt'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when a referenced model contains Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks connected in series
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. The referenced model contains two Bus Creator blocks and two Bus Selector blocks. The outputs of the Bus Creator blocks are of the same data type. One Bus Selector block chooses an element from the first Bus Creator block and passes it to the second Bus Creator block as an input. The other Bus Selector block chooses an element from the second Bus Creator block. The index of this element is higher than the index of the element chosen by the first Bus Selector block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Motor & Drive (System-Level) block inaccurately extends tabulated efficiency into other quadrants
Description: If you set the  Parameterize losses by  parameter to  Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed andtorque   and you provide data for only one or two quadrants?(the values of the  Vector ofspeeds (w) for tabulated losses  or  Vector of torques (T) fortabulated losses  parameters are only positive), the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block extends the full four-quadrant efficiency map inaccurately.  Consequently, if you run a simulation where the motor operates in a quadrant in which you did not specify the efficiency table, the power that the motor dissipates might be inaccurate.
Workaround: The best practice is to provide tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque, rather than tabulated efficiency data, because:Efficiency becomes ill-defined for zero speed or zero torque.Using losses, you can also account for fixed losses that are still present for zero speed or torque.To fix the quadrant extension issue, provide tabulated loss data instead of efficiency data:Set the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque.Convert the efficiency map into an equivalent loss map. Use the attached function?getExtendedLossTableFromEffTable.m or compute the equivalent loss map from first principles.Set the Corresponding losses, P(w,T)?parameter value to the resulting loss table.To automatically set the block parameters, use the attached function convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m.In your model, click the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block that you want to convert.At the MATLAB Command Window, enter:? >> convertEffToLossInMotorBlock(gcb)If you set?the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed, torque, and DC supply voltage,?you must modify the attached function?convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m?accordingly.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when you select the latch option for a function-call model input port that is in a feedback path
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all  of these?conditions are met: In the referenced model, there is a Trigger Port block of root level of the model with the?Trigger type ?parameter set to function-call . In the referenced model, there are root-level Inport blocks with the?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs ?parameter selected. In the top model, the corresponding input port of the Model block is on the feedback signal path.?
Workaround: Clear the Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs?option on the referenced model root level Inport blocks and insert Function-Call Feedback Latch blocks in front of corresponding input ports of the Model block in the top model.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: duration operator returns incorrect results in some atomic subcharts
Description: The?duration ?operator in an atomic subchart might return incorrect results if the following conditions are true: The atomic subchart has a periodic sample rate The atomic subchart has an input or output data that is mapped to a main chart data with a different scope OR has a data that is mapped to one bus field or array element of a larger main chart data The duration  operator uses some data mapped to the main chart. Note that the data the duration  operator uses does not have to be one of the data from the previous condition.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if a chain of Bus Creator blocks passes across subsystem boundaries and connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all  of these conditions:? The model contains an atomic subsystem and a chain of Bus Creator blocks. The chain of Bus Creator blocks passes across the subsystem boundary to get inside of the subsystem. Inside the subsystem, the output of the chain connects to a Unit Delay block.  This happens because of an optimization that incorrectly reuses variables for the outputs of Bus Creator and Unit Delay blocks.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:??Insert a Signal Conversion block between the last Bus Creator block of the chain and Unit Delay block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter.In the model's Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, set the Optimize global data access to None.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

MICRO_FPGA_SOC?   HSP Setup Wizard  is not supported for Microchip Devices.?

Summary: Code Prover analysis generates Bad instr error
Description: The following specific C++ construction leads to a?Bad instr ?error during Code Prover analysis:  You instantiate a template where the template parameter is a pointer to a function that takes non-const  parameters. The instantiation happens with a pointer to a function that takes?const  parameters.  The error occurs because of the conversion (cast) required during the template instantiation.  For example, the template below is initialized with a pointer to a function foo()  that takes a const float ?parameter: template <void (*pFcn)(float)>void callFcn() {    pFcn(2.0f);}void foo(const float);int main() {    callFcn<foo>();    return 0;}
Workaround: To work around this issue, instantiate the template with a pointer to a function that takes non-const parameters. For instance, in the previous example, you can change the prototype of the function used to instantiate the template as follows:void foo(float);

Summary: Segmentation violation when updating Simulink model containing multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when updating a Simulink model that contains multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal  objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block. ?Your model might be affected when all the following conditions are true: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem such as enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem contains an Outport block with Initial output  set to []  (empty matrix). The Outport block is driven by a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block. The source signal for the second input and above of the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The storage class (CoderInfo.StorageClass ) of the Simulink.Signal  object in the model workspace has a value other than 'Auto' . Initial value (InitialValue) ?of the Simulink.Signal  object is not empty.  A segmentation violation is more likely with higher number of input ports for the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Exported ARXML does not contain SwDataDefProps for inlined elements of structure implementation data types

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect backfolding of variable assignment into function calls
Description: In a MATLAB function, when a global variable is declared and used in only one MATLAB function and the functions that it calls, it might be converted to a local variable and passed into the called function as an input. Code generation might incorrectly fold back the assignment that copies the value of the variable into the call site of the called function. This incorrect backfolding results in a change of evaluation order and incorrect results. For example, this MATLAB function calls the function callee : function out = caller(in1, in2)?  ? ? global P2. ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (1) Declaration of global variable P2  ? ? [P1] = callee(in1,in2);? ? ? ?// (2) Callsite of function callee , which directly access global P2  ? ? P1(1,1) = P2;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (3) Copy of P2  end?  ?In the generated code, the variable P2  is converted to a local variable and passed into callee  as an input in statement (2). The copy of P2  in statement (3) is folded back to the callsite (2) and statement (3) is eliminated. The logic in the the generated code is: ? ? [P1] = callee(in1, in2, P(1, 1))  ?This optimization incorrectly changes the evaluation order of P(1, 1)  .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might return incorrect output if a?dlarray?object is constructed from another dlarray object

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when reusable subsystem inlines a function call from referenced model
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code when all ?of these conditions are met:  The top model contains a Reusable Subsystem. The top model is configured with Code interface packaging  set to Nonreusable function .? The Reusable Subsystem outputs more than one function call.? Summary: Alignment calculated incorrectly when using Tasking/Tricore compiler
Description: If you run an analysis on a project that is configured to use the Tasking  compiler with target Tricore , Polyspace might calculate the alignment of variables and structure fields incorrectly. The issue occurs when you use polyspace-configure  to extract the build configuration and pass the --eabi  option to the Tasking compiler. polyspace-configure  ignores this option.? For example, for this structure declaration:? typedef struct {  ? float myFloat;  ? char myChar;  } MyStruct;   The Tasking  compiler uses a half-word alignment by default, which means that the alignment requirement for MyStruct  is 2, leading to a size of 6. If you pass option --eabi=-half-word-align  to the compiler, the half-word alignment is disabled and the alignment requirement becomes 4. The size of MyStruct  then becomes 8. Because the disabling of the half-word alignment is ignored, if, for instance, you cast a pointer to myStruct  to pointer to a 32-bit or 64-bit int  such as in this code snippet:typedef unsigned long long int uint64_t;  char a;  MyStruct b;  volatile uint64_t *c = (volatile uint64_t*) &b;?   Polyspace reports a red Illegally Dereferenced Pointer (IDP) for the access to *c.
Workaround: A possible workaround for this issue is to pass option -compiler-parameter --no-half-word-align to Polyspace to disable half-word alignment.In the Polyspace graphical interface, you specify this option by going to Advanced Settings on the Configuration pane and entering the option in the Other field.?
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size struct field incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when all ?of these are true: An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size struct field The right-hand side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size struct field using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the statement. The generated code modifies the size of the variable-size struct field prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This causes incorrect behavior when the read of the variable-size struct field also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. The generated code might read uninitialized memory or incorrect numeric results. The attached example ReproductionModel.zip  demonstrates this issue. This code snippet from the example shows the coding pattern described above: s.f1 = s.f1(2,2) - temp;   See this corresponding generated code: i = s->f1->size[0] * s->f1->size[1];  s->f1->size[0] = temp->size[0];  s->f1->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(sp, s->f1, i, &d_emlrtRTEI);  b_s = s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1];  for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) {  ? s->f1->data[i] = b_s - temp_data[i];  }   The expression? s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1] ?in the generated code?corresponds to the MATLAB expression? s.f1(2,2) . Note that the size of the struct field  s->f1  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. The generated code should update the size of  s->f1 ?after that expression.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Variants are applied in incorrect order in Group Simulation or Fit Data programs
Description: The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app sometimes applies variants in the incorrect order when you run the Group Simulation  or Fit Data  programs. The incorrect order might be used when you run the program after making a change to the variants, such as unchecking the box to use a baseline variant. The order of variants might also differ when running the program versus viewing the program code. If you have fewer than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, then variants are in the correct order when you view the program code even if they are not applied in the correct order when running the program. If you have ten or more variant columns, then the variants will never be in the correct order when you view the program code. Note that the order of variants matters only if multiple variants modify the same quantity. In such cases, the last value is taken from the last variant to modify the component.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, update the variants to remove any overlapping content. Then, the results do not depend on the order in which variants are applied.If it is not practical to remove overlapping content, you can ensure correct results when running?a program by saving, reloading, and then rerunning a program after making any changes to the configuration of variants in that program. Reloading the project avoids the issue by updating the variant information stored in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app. However, if you have more than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, variants are still applied in the incorrect order when viewing the program code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a
