Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent changes to the IANA time zone database, including time zones in Mexico
Description: MATLAB uses the IANA time zones database for up-to-date time zone information. MATLAB is missing recent updates to this database. As of the R2022b general release, MATLAB includes database version 2022a. However, in R2022b MATLAB does not include the 2022f version of the IANA database, which also captures time zone updates from database versions 2022b, 2022c, 2022d, and 2022e. Most notably, these updates include changes to a number of time zones which no longer observe daylight saving time (DST). For example, in October 2022 the government of Mexico announced that the country no longer observes DST except near the US border.?The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. IANA Time Zone Database:?https://www.iana.org/time-zones  In versions of MATLAB that do not have these changes, datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period will have incorrect UTC offsets. Specifically, they will be one hour ahead of the actual times in those time zones.
Workaround: If possible, update to a release with an IANA time zone database version of 2022f or later.To check the IANA time zone database version in MATLAB, use the second output of the timezones function. When the database is up to date, the second output is '2022f'.>> [~,vers] = timezones vers =    '2022f'https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/timezones.htmlIf you are unable to update to a newer release, you can obtain the correct time zone information for datetime arrays on or after October 30th 2022 (where the desired time zone is in Mexico) by using?the corresponding fixed time zone offset. For example, replace the time zone 'America/Chihuahua'?with the time zone 'UTC-6' on or after?October 30th, 2022.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using the editor with tab completion and import statements
Description: While using the MATLAB editor with tab completion enabled (i.e. "Show suggestions automatically" is checked for the MATLAB Editor Automatic Completions Preferences) and import statements, MATLAB may crash when saving the file. The crash happens when the file contains expressions that end in dot (e.g. "apgtl.EventType.") or use dynamic expressions to access members of a class (e.g. "apgtl.EventType.(dynamicExpression)"). For example, the following function with import statements and a dynamic expression with a class enumeration member causes a crash when it is saved: function myFunction(enumName)  import matlab.alias.AliasDefinition;  table(apgtl.EventType.(enumName))  end  Note that "apgtl.EventType" is a MATLAB enumeration class defined within a namespace called "apgtl". The crash happens when MATLAB attempts to tab complete the line "apgtl.EventType.(enumName)". The stack trace in the crash log? contains references to mcos_impl, m_dispatcher and tabcompletion: ?[? 0] 0x00007ffd131c8fa4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03575716 [? 1] 0x00007ffd132ded5f C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04713823 [? 2] 0x00007ffd132d18c3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04659395 [? 3] 0x00007ffd241ae7be C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\m_dispatcher.dll+00845758 [? 4] 0x00007ffd241b0a37 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\m_dispatcher.dll+00854583 [? 5] 0x00007ffd0ae2e3dd C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\tabcompletion.dll+00648157
Workaround: The crash can be avoided by doing any of the following:Remove any MATLAB expressions that end in dot or use dynamic expressions to access members of a class.Omit import statements.Turning off tab completion. Go to the MATLAB toolstrip Home tab and click on Preferences,? Editor/ Debugger, Automatic Completions and uncheck "Show suggestions automatically".

Summary: Models might simulate slowly, stop responding, or produce a wrong answer if the Minimize algebraic loop?occurrences?parameter is enabled for referenced model

One or more referenced models in an algebraic loop has the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter enabled.?
Workaround: To avoid or resolve this issue, use the full perturbation solver Jacobian method in the top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.Select the Solver?pane.?Expand Solver details.Set the Solver Jacobian method?to Full perturbation.For some models, the full perturbation solver Jacobian method might not be an appropriate choice or might slow simulation. If using the full perturbation solver Jacobian method does not work for your model, you can disable algebraic loop minimization for the referenced models instead.To disable the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences?parameter for a referenced model:Open the referenced model as a top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.?Select the Model Referencing?tab.Under Options for referencing this model?clear Minimize algebraic loop occurrences.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point QR Givens rotation in MATLAB produces wrong answer when the input has a pivot element that is purely imaginary (imaginary part is non-zero, real part is zero)
Description: These functions use the fixed-point QR Givens Rotations and are impacted by this bug:? fixed.qrAB  fixed.qlessQR  fixed.cordicDivide  fixed.svd    This bug affects both MATLAB execution and code generation. The corresponding Simulink blocks are not impacted.? Follow these steps to reproduce the bug: Create A  with purely imaginary numbers (imaginary part is non-zero, real part is zero).  >> A=[1i,2i;3i,4i];   Create B  to be the identity matrix with dimension the same as size(A,1) .  >> B=[1,0;0,1];   Compute the QR decomposition with two inputs.  >> [C,R]=fixed.qrAB(A,B);   In fixed.qrAB , if the input A  is non-singular and the input B  is the identity matrix, then output C  corresponds to Q' from the QR decomposition and R  corresponds to the R from the QR decomposition.  The bug causes Q  to be singular (it should be unitary).  >> Q=C'    Q =  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 0.4472i  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 0.8944i   Additionally, R  is singular and A  is not singular.  >> R    R =  ? ? ? ? ?0? ? ? ? ?0  ? ? ? ? ?0? ? 4.4721   Q*R  should reproduce A , but Q*R  produces a matrix that has all zeros in the first column, when the first column should be [1i; 3i] .  >> Q*R    ans =  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 2.0000i  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 4.0000i    >> A    A =  ? ?0.0000 + 1.0000i? ?0.0000 + 2.0000i  ? ?0.0000 + 3.0000i? ?0.0000 + 4.0000i   And the difference between Q*R  and A  is large.  >> Q*R-A    ans =  ? ?0.0000 - 1.0000i? ?0.0000 - 0.0000i  ? ?0.0000 - 3.0000i? ?0.0000 - 0.0000i

Summary: App help text is removed when loading an app that has code containing the less than sign surrounded with whitespace
Description: When loading an app that contains any code or comments with the less than sign surrounded with whitespace (e.g. " < "), other than with the class definition on line 1, any app help text previously specified for the app will be removed from the generated code in App Designer. If edits to the app are then saved, the former app help text will be permanently lost.
Workaround: The issue can be prevented from occurring by removing any whitespace surrounding any use of the less than sign: "<".If the app already contains code or comments with the less than sign and surrounding whitespace, first get a copy of the current help text by executing >> help MyApp.mlapp where MyApp.mlapp is the name of the app. Second, load the app, remove any whitespace surrounding all uses of the less than sign, and re-add the app help text copied from the first step.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 7, R2023a

Summary: Connections to certain IP cameras fail when using an ipcam object
Description: After you install MATLAB Support Package for IP Cameras, the ipcam  object creation fails to establish a connection to certain IP cameras. Even though the user credentials of the IP camera are correct, the following error message appears:   "Unable to connect to the IP Camera Stream URL due to incorrect Username or Password."

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Structure variables in data dictionaries cannot be tuned in?Simulink Compiler
Description: In Simulink Compiler, tuned values of structure variables in data dictionaries are ignored without warning.?? Consider the following Simulink Compiler script. This script attempts to tune the field x  of the structure variable p . If p  is in a data dictionary, the simulation in the script does not use the tuned value of p.x . Also, the simulation does not issue a diagnostic indicating that the tuned value was ignored.? model = 'exampleModel';?  simInput = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);?  simInput = simInput.setVariable('p.x', 2);?  simInput = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInput);?  sim(simInput); ?
Workaround: If a structure variable in a data dictionary must be tuned, consider moving the variable to the base workspace (structure variables in the base workspace can be tuned in Simulink Compiler).?Non-structure variables can be tuned in Simulink Compiler whether or not they are in data dictionaries. If only a few fields of a structure variable need to be tuned, consider turning those fields into standalone variables.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated when mapping a sender service with the During Execution data communication method to a root level outport that is connected to nested For Each subsystem blocks
Description: When you map a sender service with the During Execution  data communication method to a root level outport that is connected to nested For Each subsystems, the generated code might incorrectly localize and optimize away the outport buffer in the subsystem calls. This leaves the buffer outdated before it sends information out through the service interface.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for AUTOSAR Adaptive model might lead to data race condition
Description: The generated code for an AUTOSAR Adaptive model might result in a data race condition if all the following conditions are true.? The model contains a provider port that provides an implementation to an adaptive method. The model contains?a Simulink Function block that provides a call through a function port. The function port is mapped to the adaptive method.  Under these conditions, the generated code initializes the provider port with MethodCallProcessingMode  as kEvent  instead of kEventSingleThread , which can lead to a data race if the method implementation does not synchronize data access.

Summary: Error when bus elements on inputs or outputs of design are single or half data type
Description: When bus elements on the inputs or outputs of a DUT are of type single or half, HDL Coder throws the following error message:?Failed Unrecognized method, property, or field 'isVector' for class 'hdlturnkey.data.TypeSingle'.
Workaround: For a workaround, use one of these options:Type cast the inputs to a fixed-point number outside the design and then convert the inputs back to single or half type inside the design.?Move the single or half type inputs outside of the bus.

Summary: Checkbox "Add a shortcut tothe desktop"?is missing in the Standalone Applicationinstaller UI
Description: The checkbox "Add a shortcut to the desktop "? is missing in the Standalone Applicationinstaller created by the Application Compiler  appor?the compiler.package.installer  function.
Workaround: You can manually create the desktop shortcut by going to the installed application folder (e.g. C:\Program Files\ApplicationName\application), right-clicking on the application (e.g. ApplicationName.exe), and selecting "Send to" and "Desktop (create shortcut)".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing depth-wise convolution with dilation values might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network that contains depth-wise convolution with non-unity dilation factor might produce incorrect results. This issue affects the ARM Mali target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect accelerator mode simulation results when sample time independent model has initialize, reset, or terminate ports but no data ports
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy produces incorrect simulation results when all of the following conditions are met: A referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. That referenced model is a sample time independent model. That referenced model contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. That referenced model has no root-level input or output ports. The corresponding Model block has the?Show model initialize port ?and Schedule rates ?block parameters selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root inputs or outputs of large,nested bus with Auto or ExportedGlobal storage class generate incorrectmemset statement in model initialization function
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect memset ?statement in the model initialization function when all ?of the following are true:  You generate code for a model that contains a nested bus that is 2560 bytes or larger. The bus uses the Auto ?or  ExportedGlobal ?storage class.  The bus includes fields of integer type.   The code generator might incorrectly use a memset ?statement in the model initialization function which causes nested properties to not be initialized.  For example, consider the following typedef ?statements:  typedef struct {  ? ? uint8_T bad1;  ? ? boolean_T bad2;  } SubBusType;    typedef struct {  ? ? SubBusType SubBus;  ? ? real_T f1;  ? ? real_T f2;  ? ? real_T f3;  ? ? // other fields from f4 to f320  } MainBusType;?  Suppose you initialize a variable FooSignal ?with these values:  static const MainBusType tmp = {  ? ? {?  ? ? ? ?1U,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* bad1 */  ? ? ? ?false? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* bad2 */  ? ? },? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* SubBus */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f1 */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f2 */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f3 */?  ? ? // other fields from f4 to f320  };  FooSignal = tmp;  The generated model initialization function might include code similar to the following: (void)memset(&FooSignal, 1, sizeof(MainBusType));  FooSignal.f1 = 10.0;  FooSignal.f2 = 10.0;?  FooSignal.f3 = 10.0;  // other field assignments from f4 to f320  This code does not correctly initialize SubBus.bad2 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A for loop in generated code with narrow floating point parameters might execute a different number of times than the corresponding MATLAB for loop
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for MATLAB code that contains a for  loop that has parameters with the single ?or  half_t ?type. The generated code might execute the corresponding? for  loop a different number of times from the MATLAB code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Imprecise dectection of actor collisions in simulation 3D environment
Description: The Unreal Engine uses invisible collision meshes to detect collisions between actors. This mesh is not the same as an actor's physical mesh.? By default, the simulation 3D environment creates a simple collision mesh for every actor. The default simple collision mesh is generally larger than the physical mesh. Therefore, the simulation 3D environment can imprecisely detect actor collisions. For example, cones created on a large plane appear to float above the plane itself.??
Workaround: To more precisely detect actor collisions with a plane surface, consider using the createShape function to create a thin box. Use the thin box to represent the plane surface. Unreal Engine uses the box dimensions to create the collision mesh. For example, the following code snippet creates a thin horizontal box which can represent a plane surface.>> box = sim3d.Actor('ActorName','Box');>> box.createShape('box',[10 10 0.001]);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root outports that are directly driven by a bus signal with ExportedGlobal storage class might generate incorrect initialization
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect initialization when?all ?of the following are true: The model contains a root outport that is driven directly by a bus signal. That bus signal has?ExportedGlobal ?storage class.  The code generator might assign an uninitialized value to the root outport in the model initialization function. For example, consider this generated code:? / * Model initialize function */  void simple_initialize(void)  {  / * Registration code */    / * block I/ O */    / * exported global signals */  {  myBus temp = {   0. 0F    };     myOut = temp;   }    {  real32_T rtb_speed;     / * SystemInitialize for BusCreator: '<S1>/ Auto Bus Creator v2' */   myOut. speed = rtb_speed;   }  }  ?The generated code initializes the bus signal?myOut.speed ?with the local uninitialized variable?rtb_speed .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Error when scheduling remote verification using a third-party job scheduler
Description: If you offload your Polyspace code verification to a cluster configured with a Parallel Computing Toolbox cluster profile, and you use a third-party job scheduler to schedule the remote verifications, the process throws an error message similar to this: ?Error: Cannot determine MJS Security Level for scheduler 'profileName'  Where profileName  is the cluster profile name.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic blocks.
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628) ?gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic  blocks when upper or lower saturation limit inputs are not connected.

Summary: Executing the mcc command by specifying the -s and -j options together results in the auto-generated p-code not being packaged
Description: Executing the mcc? command with the -j  option generates p-code from the user's .m file and packages it into a deployable archive (.ctf  file).? Executing the mcc  command with the -s  option obfuscates folder structures and file names in the deployable archive (.ctf  file) from the end user. However, when executing the mcc  command by specifying the -s  and -j  options together, the resulting auto-generated p-code is not packaged. Instead, the .m files are packaged.

Summary: Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that new version of the source document is available after opening requirement set from Git repository
Description: When you use MATLAB to clone a Git repository and then open an imported requirement set in the Requirements Editor, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document in available. Similarly, when you pull the latest changes for an imported requirement set from Git and open it, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document is available.

Description: Using struct containing matrix fields imported from custom code, having "long long" field types, can produce wrong simulation results when C Caller and C Function blocks are involved.

Summary: Type redefinition compiler error when simulating a model containing struct with ND array element
Description: If your custom code struct type has a tag?that is the struct name followed by a suffix "_tag", for example: typedef struct mystruct_tag{  int flag;  double data[4];  } mystruct;  Using such struct inside Simulink with blocks like C Caller and C Function can cause type re-definition error similar to:? 'mystruct_tag': 'struct' type redefinition

Summary: keyHash?function produces a collision if the only differences between numeric arrays are the locations of zeros
Description: Collisions occur if the only differences between numeric arrays are the locations of zeros. For example, these two calls to the keyHash  function return the same hash code. >> keyHash([0 1 2 3])>> keyHash([1 2 3 0])

Summary: The getAttribute function returns incorrect values for the PaintColorattribute
Description: If you use thegetAttribute(actorModel, 'PaintColor')  function of a Simulink.ActorModel object, the returned PaintColor  structure holds incorrect values. Specifically, the values of the?g , b  and a ?structure fields (green color, blue color, and color transparency, respectively)?are incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses deep learning libraries might produce incorrect answers if 1x1?convolution2dLayer?post-dominates?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Generated code that uses MKL-DNN, ARM Compute or cuDNN library might produce incorrect answers if your deep learning network satisfies all  these conditions: The network contains a  maxPooling2dLayer ?followed by a  convolution2dLayer .  All paths in the layer graph that start at the  maxPooling2dLayer ?go through the  convolution2dLayer ?(that is, the? convolution2dLayer ?post-dominates ?the?maxPooling2dLayer ). The  convolution2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  equal to  [1 1], ?and ?has equal strides that are greater than  1 , and ?has no spatial padding .  The  maxPooling2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  greater than  1 , or ?has strides greater than  1 , or  has spatial padding.  The only layers between the? maxPooling2dLayer ?and  convolution2dLayer ?are  reluLayer, additionLayer, clippedReluLayer, leakyReluLayer.   This is an example network layer graph that might produce incorrect answers in code generation:? ?lg = layerGraph([imageInputLayer([5 5 3], Normalization = 'none', Name = 'input')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? maxPooling2dLayer([2 3], Name = 'maxpool')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? reluLayer(Name = 'relu')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer(1,3,Stride = [2 2], Name = 'conv')]);?  This issue affects code generated by using any  of these products: MATLAB Coder  Simulink Coder  GPU Coder when using  TargetLibrary='cudnn'?
Workaround: For code generation targeting Intel, ARM, or AMD based CPUs, use?TargetLibrary='none'.For code generation targeting NVIDIA GPUs, use TargetLibrary='tensorrt'.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing depth-wise convolution with dilation values might produce incorrect results

Summary: insEKF?incorrectly computes numeric Jacobian for custom sensor or custom motion models
Description: If you use an? insEKF  object with a custom sensor model or custom motion model, and that custom model implements a  stateTransition  method, but not?a  stateTransitionJacobian  method, the Jacobian is computed incorrectly.  These standard sensors and motion models are not affected by this issue: insMotionPose  insMotionOrientation  insGPS  insAccelerometer  insMagnetometer  insGyroscope .?
Workaround: For each custom sensor model or custom motion model used with an?insEKF, check that if the model?implements a?stateTransition?function, it also implements a stateTransitionJacobian function.

Summary: RoadRunner Scenario Reader and Writer blocks output incorrect pose values when Topic is set to Actor Pose (Driving Scenario Compatible)
Description: When the?Topic ?parameter of a RoadRunner?Scenario Reader or Writer block is set to?Actor Pose?(Driving Scenario Compatible) , the actor pose is converted between a 4-by-4?Pose  matrix  and Roll, Pitch, Yaw, and Position ?values. The conversion uses an incorrect sequence of rotations which causes Roll  and Pitch  angles to get swapped.

Summary: Simulink Check validation test cases SLCHK_HI_Q026, SLCHK_HI_Q101, and SLCHK_MS_Q002 fail when optional licenses are not installed

SLCHK_HI_Q026 : m_hism_hisl_0070_WARN   When Simulink Design Verifier is not installed, these test cases fail with error mathworks.hism.0101 is not a valid check ID : SLCHK_HI_Q101 : m_hism_hisl_0101_PASS   SLCHK_HI_Q101 : m_hism_hisl_0101_WARN?   When Stateflow is not installed, test case SLCHK_MS_Q002 : MdlMetricsInfo_PASS  fails with error mathworks.iec61508.MdlMetricsInfo is not a valid check ID .?

Description: Generating a report from a System Composer model that contains an Adapter block may produce the following error: Error in line: append(rpt,  ComponentElements);?Unrecognized function or variable 'id'.

Summary: Admin Center fails to start on Linux
Description: When you start Admin Center with the admincenter command on a Linux operating system, you might encounter the following error: . / admincenter: 165: . / admincenter: / bin/ java: not found
Workaround: To work around this issue,?set the MJS_JAVA environment variable before you start Admin Center. Execute the following command:export MJS_JAVA=<matlabroot>/sys/java/jre/glnxa64/jre?<matlabroot> is the path to the root folder of your MATLAB installation.

Summary: MATLAB hangs if MQTT subscribe and write calls are made simultaneously
Description: If an MQTT client is subscribing to multiple topics and any client publishes into those same topics while the subscriptions are establishing, MATLAB hangs after a few subscription calls.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add a pause?of 0.2s?or more, after each subscription call.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox

Summary: Review scope values in Configuration Settings section of Polyspace report are incorrect
Description: If you generate a Polyspace report and all the following are true: You use option Only include currently displayed results . You apply any review scope other than "All results" to your results.  Then the Review Scope  column in the tables of the Configuration Settings  section of the report might show incorrect values.? For instance, if you disable CERT C rule PRE30-C in your custom review scope but the review scope value for this rule is set to ON in the report. Summary: ROS Node generation fails for Simulink models using Simulink Configuration Reference

Clicking on the 'Build' or 'Build and load' or 'Build and run' button in the Simulink toolstrip throws these errors. Parameter update is not supported for a configuration reference.  Update parameter in the configuration set that it references.
Workaround: You can generate code by using the rtwbuild?MATLAB function.If your model, <modelname> uses a Simulink Configuration Reference, then use rtwbuild?function to generate code instead of the DEPLOY?buttons in Simulink Toolstrip under ROS tab.?>> rtwbuild(<modelname>)?

Summary: Unexpected license checkout when using "Other uses of functionName" links in help text
Description: After calling help on a function that is overloaded in several classes, clicking on the "Other functions named functionName  " link at the bottom of the help text can cause licenses to be checked out.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Simscape Grid Tied Converter example hits timing violations on hardware
Description: In the Simscape Grid Tied Converter example, the target frequency specified for the desktop simulation might not be achieved on the hardware. This hits timing violations on the hardware. The base sample time (Tbase) value for desktop simulation and the Tbase specified in the HDL Workflow script are different. However, both use the same variable name: ˇ®TbaseˇŻ. The Tbase specified for desktop simulation is faster but is overridden by the Tbase specified in the HDL Workflow script which is slower.

Summary: Updating models with Simulation 3D Physical Vehicle blocks can result in errors or inadvertent Aerospace Blockset??license checkouts
Description: If your model contains a Simulation 3D Physical Vehicle block,?updating the model can result in an error or inadvertent Aerospace Blockset??license checkout.?

Summary: Degraded Model block SIL/PIL performance when referenced model contains many tunable parameters
Description: The initial build of SIL/PIL harness files during a Model block software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation takes a long time if the block parameter Code interface   is set to Model reference  and the referenced model contains hundreds of tunable parameters.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an MQTT connection to the broker is interrupted
Description: After an MQTT client is successfully created, a disruption in the MQTT broker connection might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Ctrl+L does not open linked library
Description: Using Ctrl+L  to go to a linked library does not work for saved or newly created Simulink libraries.

Summary: Write failure and unexpected read behavior with WAV files
Description: There is a permission error when trying to write a WAV audio file with sample rate greater than 655,350 Hz using?audiowrite . For example: >> audiowrite("example.wav",zeros(1000,1),655351)Error using audiowriteCould not open the file.  Permission denied. There can also be unexpected behavior when reading such files with?audioread . For example the data type of the output of audioread  can be wrong when reading the file with the dataType  argument set to "native" : >> y = audioread("example_int16.wav","native"); % the file contains 16-bit audio data sampled at 655,351 Hz>> class(y)ans = 'single' % should be 'int16' This issue is due to a bug in the?1.1.0 version of the third-party libsndfile  library:?https://github.com/libsndfile/libsndfile/issues/850 . This issue can occur in: MATLAB release R2022b on Windows and Mac platforms any MATLAB release on a Linux platform that has libsndfile ?Version 1.1.0 installed

Summary: DataLoggingOverride parameter is ignored when 'UseFastRestart' is on
Description: When running simulations using sim ?or?parsim  command, any value specified on the Simulink.SimulationInput  object for DataLoggingOverride? model parameter is ignored if 'UseFastRestart'  option is 'on'.?

Summary: Simulating a model with C Caller or C Function block and custom code structure type containing only square matrices may cause CGXE assertion
Description: If a custom code structure type contains only square matrices, using the struct  with C Caller or C Function blocks can cause an assertion during simulation. For example, using this custom code structure type with the square matrix:? typedef struct {    double a;    double b;    double c;    double D[2][2];}AddStruct; can cause the following assertion during simulation:? CGXE assertion in B:\ matlab\ src\ cg_ir\ util_cg\ SanityCheckerImpl. cpp at line 628? ValidCore violated: Types not equal: theConst->type(): function (struct { Fields: { { Name: a Type: double}{ Name: b Type: double}{ Name: c Type: double}{ Name: D Type: matrix 2x2 double} } } *) -> ,  aConstExpr->type(): function (struct { Fields: { { Name: a Type: double}{ Name: b Type: double}{ Name: c Type: double}{ Name: D Type: matrix 2x2 double} } } *) -> ?
Workaround: Introduce a matrix field in the custom code struct that does not have a square shape. For example:typedef struct {    double a;    double b;    double c;    double D[2][2];     double F[2][3];  }AddStruct;

Summary: ARXML import fails with the error "Unsupported type double"
Description: When importing ARXML files containing enumeration literals that reference an ApplicationValueSpecification  of category string, the importer might return an unexpected error with the text "Unsupported type double".

Summary: Attitude Profile?block returns incorrect results when Port coordinate frame is ITRF and Constraint coordinate frame?is LVLH
Description: The?Attitude Profile block returns incorrect results when both of the following conditions are true: Port coordinate frame  ?is set to ITRF .  Constraint coordinate frame  ?is set to LVLH .

Summary: Model Testing Dashboard shows "file not found" error for test case results included in test result report
Description: When you create a test result report in Simulink Test, the Model Testing Dashboard  shows the included test case results under the report file name in the?Artifacts ?panel. If you try to open included test case results, the dashboard shows a ˇ°file not foundˇ± error.

Summary: HDL code generation fails for a model that has tunable port which outputs an array signal as a first input port

Summary: After installing a MATLAB update, optimization solvers error when setting UseParallel to true and running on a parallel cluster
Description: After installing R2022a update 4 or later on a host computer or on a cluster, but not both, running an optimization solver such as?fmincon  or ga  on a parallel cluster by setting option UseParallel = true  results in the following error: Error using @()SetupWorker(objfun,confun)Undefined function 'SetupWorker' for input arguments of type 'function_handle'. The error is caused by a mismatch between the software versions on the client and the cluster.
Workaround: Ensure both the client MATLAB installation and cluster MATLAB Parallel Server installation have the same R2022a update version.If the cluster MATLAB Parallel Server installation has version R2022a update 4 or later, but the client MATLAB installation has a prior version, update the client to the most recent release.If the client MATLAB installation has version R2022a update 4 or later, but the cluster has a prior version, then either:Update the cluster MATLAB Parallel Server to the latest update release, orRevert the client MATLAB installation by reinstalling R2022a and not updating to update 4 or later.

Description: When you provide input samples with an (N-K)/2 bursterror to the block,?the generated HDL code output may not match theSimulink simulation output. Here, N and K represent the block mask parameters,Codeword length (N) and Message length (K).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder produces incorrect code when a global variable is written on GPU as a function output
Description: When generating code using GPU Coder, the code generator might fail to insert necessary memory copies between CPU and GPU when the following conditions are true: A global or persistent variable appears on the left-hand side of an assignment with a function call on the right-hand side. The output of the function is returned directly on GPU instead of on the CPU (This is an optimization GPU Coder might apply depending on the function and how it is called).  This might cause the generated code to produce incorrect results. For example, consider the following entry-point function: function entryPoint??? % G is numeric array??? global G;??? coder.gpu.kernelfun;??? G = foo();??? coder.ceval('readData', coder.rref(G));??? G = G + 1;??? coder.ceval('readData', coder.rref(G));endfunction Y = foo??? coder.inline('never');??? coder.gpu.kernelfun;??? Y = ones(1000,1);end  In the generated code, the call to  foo ?writes directly to global variable  G  on GPU. However, GPU Coder fails to insert a data copy from GPU to CPU before the first  coder.ceval  call, which causes incorrect data to be read.

Summary: CAN blocks drop floating-point digits in DBC file
Description: The CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, and CAN FD Unpack blocks can be configured to use CANdb specified signals, in which case numerical parameters are read from a DBC file to perform the conversion from messages to signals. If the DBC file contains signal definitions whose scaling  and offset  parameter values are floating-point, these values are rounded to four decimal places. These rounded values are used in simulation and in any generated code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: SoC Builder validation fails for "Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver" example in SoC Blockset
Description: The "Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver" example in SoC Blockset?fails to build with the following error from the SoC Builder  tool on the Validate Model  screen:    Error: The value for 'Frequency (MHz)' in the 'Advanced' panel of the 'Mem Controller' block must be equal to the value for 'Controller clock frequency (MHz)' in the model configuration settings.?
Workaround: On the System on Chip tab, click?Hardware Settings.In the Hardware board settings pane, expand Target hardware resources.Click?FPGA design (PS mem controllers).Re-enter the value for the Controller clock frequency (MHz) parameter as 150.Click Apply.Click OK.Re-run the validation step in the?SoC Builder tool.

Summary: Error or missing contents when loading some projects with fit programs in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: Some SimBiology projects with fit programs might not open correctly. When opening such a project from SimBiology Model Analyzer , you might get the following error message: Unable to load project, the client encountered an exception : TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'ID')  When you open SimBiology Model Builder  while Model Analyzer  is already open or is opened afterward, the project contents (model, data, and programs) will not be displayed in the Model Analyzer  browser pane.?
Workaround: This issue occurs when loading projects with the Fit Data or Group Simulation programs that have unchecked responses in the Data Map section. Before saving a project in the original release of R2022b without updates, delete any unchecked response rows from the Data Map section in the Data step for all Fit Data or Group Simulation programs.

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector displays wrong signal hierarchy for nested array of buses
Description: When a virtual bus is marked for logging and one or more leaf elements of the virtual bus is an array of buses, the table of signals on the Inspect  pane of the Simulation Data Inspector shows an incorrect signal hierarchy.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the?activation?function when called on additionLayer might produce incorrect results for MKL-DNN target
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB code uses the activation  function to compute the activations of additionLayer ?in your deep learning network. In such situations, the generated code might produce incorrect results for the activation values if your network contains these layers in succession: convolution2dLayer?  batchNormalizationLayer  (optional) additionLayer  (the activation  function is called on this layer) Activation layers like?reluLayer  and leakyReluLayer   This issue affects the MKL-DNN target.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: False check colors using return value of lookup table function when function takes pointer to a volatile memory zone
Description: If you analyze this generated code with Code Prover using the option -stub-embedded-coder-lookup-table-functions , the User assertion  check on the final assert  statement is green: /* Lookup table function */  double  look1_linlcpw(double  u0, const double  bp0[], const  double  table[],                     unsigned int  maxIndex); double  u = 2.5;? /* Lookup table step */  double  bp[3] = { 1.0, 2.0, 3.0} ; /* Lookup table axis */  void  main(void ) {?  ?volatile  double  x[3];? ?double  *p, res;? ?p = (double *)&x[0];? ?p[0] = 0.0;? ?p[1] = 0.0;? ?p[2] = 0.0;? ?res = look1_linlcpw(u, bp, p, 3);? ?assert(res == 0.0);?}   Because x  is an array of volatile double , the check should be orange.  Code Prover assumes that?volatile  variable values?can be modified without explicit write operations in the code. At any point in the code, these variables can have any value allowed by their data type. This assumption means that when a pointer to array?x ?is passed as the "table" argument of the lookup table function?look1_linlcpw() , the function is also expected to return any double  value.??When this return value is compared to 0.0, Code Prover is expected to show an orange check.

Summary: Compilation error in C++ generated code due to missing "const" qualifier in generated abstract interface class for a port-scoped Simulink function
Description: Generated code for a model containing a port-scoped Simulink function produces a compiler error?under the following conditions: The model is a referenced model The port-scoped Simulink function has an input argument with a bus type The ERT system target file is selected The target language is set to C++  Under these conditions, the code generator generates an abstract class for the port associated with the Simulink function where the prototype for the Simulink function is missing a "const" qualifier.?

Summary: Code generation fails for S-Function Builder blocks with bus ports and the code is generated in multiple languages
Description: Code generation for models containing S-Function Builder blocks with bus port(s) fails if the code is generated in multiple languages.  For example, build fails in a model when the code is generated in C++ for an S-Function Builder with bus ports and in C for the model, or vice versa. The build fails due to the use of undefined variable "__<busName>BUS" in the generated file named "<sfcn>_cgen_wrapper.c(pp)".?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Requirements Editor shows incorrect verification status for links to test assessments that have iterations
Description: The Requirements Editor ?might show an incorrect verification status for requirements that have links from logical or temporal assessments in the Simulink Test Manager if the test case that contains the assessment also has test iterations that ran in a Simulink Test Manager session.

Summary: gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel produces incorrect results in MATLAB for complex type inputs
Description: For complex type inputs, gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel  produces incorrect results during MATLAB simulation. However, the generated code produces the expected results.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant-folding expressions that accept nonvirtual buses as inputs might generate incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect results when all  of the following are true: ?You generate code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block. The code in the MATLAB Function block has an expression that accepts a nonvirtual bus containing a matrix as an input. The code uses the matrix in the nonvirtual bus input to initialize a matrix variable. A value is assigned to an element of the initialized?matrix variable. The code uses the coder.const  directive to constant-fold the expression.   Constant-folding the expression in the generated code might produce incorrect results. For example, consider the function  loca l. The function is called by passing the input  u, ? which is a nonvirtual bus that contains a matrix of complex numbers in the field f . function y = fcn(u)  ? ? u.f = repmat(0+2i,2,1);  ? ? t=coder.const(local(u));  ? ? y=t.f;  end  function ovar = local(ivar)  ? ? ovar.f = ivar.f;  ? ? temp = ivar.f;  ? ? temp(1) = 5+5i;  end  The function local ? initializes the variable temp  using the input and assigns the value 5+5i  to a matrix element. The generated code constant-folds the expression local(u) ,?which ?produces the incorrect result?[0+2i,5+5i], ?where t he correct result is [0+2i,0+2i] .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: ID strings used to identify joint position and velocity variables of a KinematicsSolver object might be inconsistent between releases
Description: If a KinematicsSolver object is created in R2022a or R2022b from a model that contains weld joints, the ID strings of the joint position and velocity variables might differ from the ID strings of a KinematicsSolver object created from the same model in other releases.

Summary: Sine Wave block or Uniform Random Number block inside For Each subsystem might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When you simulate a Simulink model that contains a For Each Subsystem block, the simulation might produce incorrect results when all the following conditions are true: The model contains a For Each subsystem with partitioned mask parameters. Either a Sine Wave block or a Uniform Random Number block is inside the For Each subsystem. At least one of the block parameters of the Sine Wave block or Uniform Random Number block references the partitioned For Each subsystem mask parameters.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: RoadRunner Scenario Writer block writes incorrect values to?Actor Pose?(Driving Scenario Compatible) topic
Description: When you set the?Topic ?parameter of the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block to?Actor Pose ?(Driving Scenario Compatible ), the corresponding actor pose does not update correctly. The pose that is sent as input to the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block is not implemented during co-simulation.
Workaround: Manually convert the?Roll,?Pitch,?Yaw, and?Position?fields of input structure type?BusActorPose?to the?Pose?field of input structure type?BusActorRuntime. Then, write the?BusActorRuntime?structure type with the newly calculated?Pose?to the?Actor Pose?topic by using the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization for code-reuse subsystem contents in initialize function
Description: A CodeReuseSubsystem block generates initialization code that is not called when all these conditions apply: The CodeReuseSubsystem block is in an Initialize Function block. That Initialize Function block is in a Function-Call Subsystem block. That Function-Call Subsystem block is in an export-function model. That Function-Call Subsystem block is called at union rates, where one sample time unites two or more event sample times.

Summary: Comments on blocks participating in multi-rate sharing may be duplicated in the generated HDL code
Description: When you enable resource sharing on blocks that contain comments, the comments may appear more than once on the shared resources in the generated HDL code. These comments can come from user comments on the block, comment-based traceability, or block requirements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for MATLAB code that contains syntactically equivalent logical indexing
Description: The code generator has an optimization capability to remove code lines that do not affect program results. For some MATLAB coding patterns, the code generator can incorrectly apply this optimization to code lines containing syntactically equivalent logical indexing, which leads to incorrect results.Consider the following MATLAB code: function y_sat = mymlsat(u,u_min, u_max)  ? ? ? ?y_sat=u;  ? ? ? ?tempInds = u<u_min;  ? ? ? ?y_sat(tempInds) = u_min;  ? ? ? ?tempInds = u>u_max;  ? ? ? ?y_sat(tempInds) = u_max;  end?  The code contains two logical indexing expressions of y_sat(tempInds) .?They look the same but produce different results because of the different values of tempInds . The generated C code for the MATLAB function y_sat: void mymlsat(const real_T u[3], real_T u_min, real_T u_max, real_T y_sat[3]) {  ? ? ? ? ? (void)u_min;  ? ? ? ? ? ?y_sat[0] = u[0];  ? ? ? ? ? ?if (u[0] > u_max) {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y_sat[0] = u_max; }  ? ? ? ? ? ?y_sat[1] = u[1];  ? ? ? ? ? ?if (u[1] > u_max) {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y_sat[1] = u_max; }  ? ? ? ? ? ?y_sat[2] = u[2];  ? ? ? ? ? ?if (u[2] > u_max) {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y_sat[2] = u_max; }  ? ? ? ? ?}  The code generator incorrectly removes the expression y_sat(tempInds) = u_min  from the generated code, which affects the program results.

Summary: SIL/PIL app with custom code can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: An SIL/PIL app might crash MATLAB if all the following conditions are true: Custom C/C++ code is attached to a Simlink model. The custom code contains a switch  statement. The switch  statement uses enum  values in a switch  expression. The switch  statement uses enum  values in case  labels.  For example, the following code might cause this issue: enum e { ??TYPE_ZERO};void foo() {??switch (TYPE_ZERO) {   // enum value in switch expression????case TYPE_ZERO: ;    // enum value in case label??}} If you add this type of code to a model by using the Code Generation > Custom Code window of the model'sModel Settings, then select? SIL/PILManager from the Apps menu and click Run SIL/PIL, MATLAB crashes.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Model generation may fail for masked subsystem or library that is configured with mask initialization code
Description: HDL Coder generates an internal assertion during model generation of masked subsystem or library if the mask initialization code contains cell array of structs.? The following assertion is generated during model generation:? First input must be numeric.
Workaround: Consider disabling model generation.Model Configuration Parameters -> Global Settings -> Model Generation -> Generated Model

Summary: Lookup Table Editor cannot recognize deprecated lookup table blocks
Description: Lookup Table Editor returns an incorrect error message when you try to view Lookup 1D and 2D blocks that have been replaced.

Summary: Workflow Advisor errors out for MATLAB to HDL code generation when using Generic ASIC/FPGA workflow with Microchip Libero SoC Tool
Description: MATLAB to HDL code generation fails in the Workflow Advisor when you use the?Target Workflow ?as? Generic ASIC/FPGA ? and?Synthesis Tool ?as?Microchip Libero SoC . ? The following error message is generated:?  Unrecognized function or variable 'PirInputPorts'.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when the scope of a port on a Stateflow block is changed using the Property Inspector
Description: MATLAB crashes if you change the Scope  property of a port on a Stateflow block from the Data tab of the Property Inspector . For example, MATLAB crashes when the Scope  of a data port is changed from Input to Output.The generated crash logs include lines similar to the following:? [? 0] 0x00007f916c07aed4 slPort::getGrIsBlockOwner() const at /matlab/toolbox/simulink/src/sl_editor/../../../../derived/glnxa64/src/include/SimulinkBlock/../../../../../src/SimulinkBlock/export/include/SimulinkBlock/slPort.hpp:293  [? 1] 0x00007f91924777ac std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > > >::operator[](unsigned long) const at /mathworks/hub/3rdparty/internal/5566149/glnxa64/gcc-8.3.0/include/c++/8.3.0/bits/stl_vector.h:951  [? 2] 0x00007f91924274fb CosSLPortPropertySchema::setPropertyValues(SLPortPropertyModel*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > > > const&, bool) at /matlab/src/simulink/sl_glee/CosSLPortPropertySchema.cpp:19  [? 3] 0x00007f9192429bed mcos::factory::impl::MethodInvokerImpl<mcos::factory::mxArrayProxy (*)(SLPortPropertyModel*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char16_t, std::char_traits<char16_t>, std::allocator<char16_t> > > > const&, bool)>::invoke(mcos::factory::impl::Invoker const&, mcos::COSClient const*, int&, mcos::COSValue*, int, mcos::COSValue const*) at /matlab/src/simulink/../../derived/glnxa64/src/include/mcos_factory/../../../../../src/mcos_factory/export/include/mcos_factory/mi/impl/MethodImpl.hpp:1049
Workaround: Change the scope of a port from the Symbols Pane of a Stateflow block, instead. Navigate inside the respective Stateflow block and click Symbols Pane from the Design Data section of the Modeling tab. Change the scope of the port to the required value from the Type column.

Summary: Coverage data aggregation for multiple subsystem harnesses with a top model harness causes error
Description: If you have multiple subsystem test harnesses and a top-level model harness, and run tests using the Test Manager in Simulink Test, coverage data aggregation for all three harnesses might result in an error.  For example, assume you have two subsystem harnesses of the same subsystem, U1 and?U2, and a top model harness, T. Aggregating coverage data for U1 + U2 is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U1 + T is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U2 + T is compatible Aggregating coverage data for?U1 + U2 + T is not compatible and causes an error that looks like:  ? ? ? ? ? ?The structure of model "modelName" is incompatible with the coverage data collected on model "harnessModelName".

Summary: Some UIs appear blank when accessing via Remote Desktop
Description: When using tools like Remote Desktop or TeamViewer to remotely access a Windows machine, some UIs can appear blank. Affected UIs include, but are not limited to: App Designer AddOns Explorer AddOns Manager Simulink Test Manager Simulink Data Inspector? Any UI produced with UIFigure or App Designer  The UIs run, and the window appears with the correct tile, but the contents of the window remain blank.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, run the following commands as the first commands in a MATLAB session:wm = matlab.internal.webwindowmanager.instance;wm.setStartupOptions('ExternalProcess', '--ignore-gpu-blacklist --disable-gpu-compositing');?You can add these commands to a?user-defined startup script?to ensure that they run early in the MATLAB startup process.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Unrecognized method, property, or field error during Model block SIL/PIL simulation
Description: Consider a Model block with a referenced model that logs a signal. If the logged signal implementation resolves to a custom storage class, then running a simulation with block parameter Simulation mode  set to ?Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?can produce this error:? Unrecognized method, property, or field 'BaseRegion' for class 'coder.descriptor.BasicAccessFunctionExpression'?  ?
Workaround: Identify the logged signal that is causing the issue.Configure the logged signal as a test point. For more information, see?Configure Signals as Test Points.

Summary: Unable to view custom UI component in App Designer Component Library if the description for the component contains newlines
Description: When configuring a custom UI component to be usable from App Designer, if the description field contains newlines, the configuration will successfully complete but the component will not appear in the App Designer Component Library.
Workaround: Reconfigure the custom UI component by launching the App Designer Custom UI Component Metadata dialog. Remove any newlines from the description field and click OK.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might output values from the current time step instead of the previous time step
Description: The Roadrunner Scenario Reader block should output actor or vehicle poses from the previous time step. However, if a Simulink model has both Roadrunner Scenario Reader and RoadRunner Scenario Writer blocks, the Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might incorrectly output pose values written in the current time step by the?RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.? This situation might occur if the Roadrunner Scenario Writer block, through its message lines or signal input lines, cannot be traced back to the RoadRunner Scenario Reader block.

Summary: spectrumAnalyzer release method if called after hide does not unlock the object
Description: If the spectrumAnalyzer object is provided with data, then hide method was called followed by release, the scope will remain locked preventing any changes to non-tunable properties.
Workaround: Before calling release method on spectrumAnalyzer object, if hide was called, call show method on the scope then call release.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assertion can occur during HDL code generation when using a Gain block and enabling adaptive pipelining
Description: An internal assertion can occur when you generate HDL code for a model that has: A Gain block? Adaptive Pipelining  set to on ? The Synthesis Tool  and Target Frequency  set?  The assertion that occurs is the following:matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\compcoreop.cpp:406:Input signal to Gain block is empty
Workaround: To avoid this assertion, either:Model the Gain block as a Product block by moving the gain value to a Constant block and setting it as one of the inputs for the Product block.Disable adaptive pipelining.

Summary: Computing frequency-domain features in the Diagnostic Feature Designer app using imported spectrum data generates an error
Description: When you import your own spectrum data into theDFD app using the New Session dialog box, the app does not assign the samplingfrequency correctly. This problem is not apparent until you try to use thatspectrum data to compute a feature option from the Frequency-Domain Featuresmenu. The conflict between the correct sampling frequency and the incorrect appversion of the sampling frequency causes the app to throw errors in the commandwindow.? The error message includes the following: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ModeChanged defined for class ctrluis.toolstrip.ModeManager:Error using predmaintguis.internal.ciapp.plots.frequencydomainfeatures.SpectralFeaturesMode/computeDependentSpectrumStatesError setting property 'MaxFrequency' of class 'predmaint.internal.options.SpectralFeatureOptions'. Value must be positive.

Summary: Wrong initial values in simulation results for export-function model with bus element port and Mux block at the root level
Description: Incorrect zero values are reported in simulation results for the first time step in normal mode simulation when all ?of the following conditions apply: The model is an export-function model. The model contains an In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model contains a Mux block at the root level. The Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to Array , Structure , or Structure with time .  The zero values are reported instead of the initial values of the signals that drive the root Out Bus Element and Outport blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Generating CUDA code for?pcfitcuboid produces a compile-time error for GPU compute capability greater than or equal to 6.1 and single type
Description: Generating CUDA code for the pcfitcuboid ?function throws the following compile-time error when the inputs are of the single data type and the GPU compute capability is greater than or equal to 6.1 . Error:?  Inputs to gpucoder. atomicAdd function must be of the same data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior when periodic function-call subsystem uses invalid periodic sample time
Description: Updating a model produces an incorrect error when all of these conditions are true: A periodic function-call subsystem has a Trigger block with Sample time type  set to periodic . In this periodic function-call subsystem, a block explicitly specifies a different periodic sample time. This periodic function-call subsystem is one of multiple subsystems in the block diagram.  The incorrect error is similar to this: The enabled subsystem 'subsystem-name' executes in multiple tasks.  This can cause corrupted data or non-deterministic behavior in a real-time system.  Building a model that produces this incorrect error might generate incorrect code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: HDL IP core generates code with AXI3 interface for Microchip SmartFusion2 board?in HDL Workflow Advisor.
Description: Using IP core generation workflow in HDL Workflow Advisor for Microchip SmartFusion2 board, the SmartFusion2 based IP core are created with AXI3 interface instead of AXI4 interface. HDL Workflow Advisor infrastructure only supports the AXI4 based Interface.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for export-function model with State Writer block that writes to Outport block
Description: Simulating an export-function model produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: The?Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to?Dataset . The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. Each function-call signal connects to a function-call port of a function-call subsystem. The first function-call subsystem contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block writes to an Outport block in the second function-call subsystem. The corresponding output port directly drives a root Outport block.  The output of the root Outport block is logged each time either function-call subsystem executes. When the first function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged incorrectly. When the second function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged correctly.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Build error due to incorrect preprocessor if conditions guarding function declaration and definition
Description: A build error can occur when all the following conditions are met :?A modeling element generates functions - e.g Stateflow charts This modeling element is contained within an inlined variant region. For example, inside a choice of a variant subsystem, with the FunctionPackaging  set to Inline . This variant region is triggered by a function call output from a reusable Stateflow chart.  Example error message: myModel1.c: In function 'myModel1_Pos':  myModel1.c:103:18: error: 'D_Work_myModel1' has no member named 'is_c2_myModel1'  myModel1_DWork.is_c2_myModel1 = myModel1_IN_Neg;
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may return incorrect results for a deep learning network with a LeakyReluLayer with 'Scale' property set to negative
Description: Code generated for a SeriesNetwork , DAGNetwork , or dlnetwork  with a?LeakyReluLayer ?may produce incorrect results if both? conditions are true: The 'Scale ' property of?LeakyReluLayer ?is set to negative. Target library is set to? 'none '.
Workaround: There are a few walkaround options to resolve this issue:1. Generate code for a different target such as mkldnn.2. If you want to use target none, you can replace the?LeakyReluLayer?in the network with a custom layer that performs a leaky reLU operation.

Summary: SimBiology projects lose data and become corrupted when apps are left open overnight
Description: If you leave a SimBiology project open overnight in the SimBiology Model Builder  app or the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the project might lose data and become corrupted. This data loss can lead to errors when saving or loading the project or when trying to load data or results from the project. This corruption happens because the operating system sometimes deletes temporary files needed by the SimBiology project.
Workaround: You can prevent the operating system from deleting SimBiology temporary files by changing the MATLAB temporary folder. Set the appropriate environment variable to a different location, such as your documents folder. On Windows, set the environment variable TMP. On macOS or Linux, set the environment variable TMPDIR.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB may crash when destroying multiple copies of a handle object whose destructor accesses the caller's workspace
Description: If the user creates an instance of a handle class whose destructor accesses the caller's workspace, then creates a copy of that instance, MATLAB may crash when destroying those variables. MATLAB may not crash every time those variables are destroyed. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: An unused struct field or class property assigned with a function call that has side effects might generate incorrect code
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all? these conditions:  You assigned the result of a function call to a class property or a struct field. This function has side effects. (A function has side effects if the function modifies something and that change is visible outside the scope of the function.) The class property or struct field is unused in your code.  An example MATLAB function affected by this issue: function out = fcn(u)    s = struct('a',[u u*4 u],'b',u+2,'c',u,'d',u*2);    s1 = [s s];    s.d = fcn2(s1); % function call with side effect    out = s1(2).b + s1(1).c;end
Workaround: Perform either of these actions:?If you have Embedded Coder license, enable the configuration setting Preserve unused fields and properties?(PreserveUnusedStructFields).cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.PreserveUnusedStructFields = true;In your MATLAB code, before the assignment of the unused struct field or class property, assign the result of the function call to a temporary variable. Then use this temporary variable to assign the field or property. For example, modify the example code in the previous section as follows:function out = fcn(u)    s = struct('a',[u u*4 u],'b',u+2,'c',u,'d',u*2);    s1 = [s s];    tmp = fcn2(s1); % assign function call with side effect to temporary variable    s.d = tmp;    out = s1(2).b + s1(1).c;end

Summary: Exploration noise in DDPG, TD3, and epsilon in DQN do not decay in parallel training
Description: The exploration noise in DDPG and TD3, and epsilon in DQN, do not decay during parallel training. This happens because the EnableNoiseDecay  property (for DDPG and TD3), and the EnableEpsilonDecay  property (for DQN) in the exploration policy is not handled correctly. Due to this problem, the DQN agent does not typically learn a good policy in parallel training when the default value of epsilon is used (because this default value is too large). Similarly, TD3 and DDPG agents fail to learn a good policy if the initial standard deviation is large.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, use one of the followoing workarounds:  To prevent parallel training for DQN/DDPG/TD3 agents, set UseParallel in rlTrainingOptions to false. Use small values for standard deviation or epsilon:  DQN: Set Epsilon in EpsilonGreedyExploration of rlDQNAgentOptions to a relatively small value.  DDPG: Set StandardDeviation in NoiseOptions of rlDDPGAgentOptions to a relatively small values if  the value of StandardDeviationDecayRate is greater than zero.  TD3: Set StandardDeviation in ExplorationModel of rlTD3AgentOptions to a relatively small values if the value of StandardDeviationDecayRate is greater than zero.
