Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bit to Integer Converter block with Input bit order set to?LSB First wraps to incorrect value when overflow occurs
Description: The Bit to Integer Converter block fails to wrap input data correctly when converting a number that exceeds the number of bits specified for the output data type when all of the following conditions are true: ? Bit to Integer Converter block?After bit packing, treat resulting integer values as ?parameter is set to an unsigned type. Bit to Integer Converter block?Input bit order ?parameter is set to LSB First . Bit to Integer Converter block?Output data type ?parameter is set to a signed type that is smaller than the number of bits in the input bitstream.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating n-D Lookup Table block with fixed-point data types?in a model reference hierarchy
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model if all of the following conditions are true: Parent model contains a Model block with at least one breakpoint or table passed in as a model argument. Model block contains an n-D Lookup Table block with?Source? set to?Input port ?and ?at least one breakpoint or table input.? The model argument passed from the model block is set as a parameter value to the breakpoint or table parameter.  The n-D Lookup Table block inputs, breakpoints, and table data types are fixed-point.
Workaround: Replace the n-D Lookup table block with its equivalent Prelookup and Interpolation Using Prelookup block combination.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Exporting Simulink Design Verifier generated test cases to Simulink Test causes Export operation aborted error
Description: Exporting test cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test displays an Export operation aborted  error dialog box when all the following conditions are true: The analysis is performed on a subsystem block The subsystem contains Function Caller  blocks without corresponding Simulink functions or blocks in the AUTOSAR Blockset Basic Software block library that model component calls to AUTOSAR Basic Software (BSW) services Harness source is set to Signal Editor  or Signal Builder

Summary: In IP Core Generation Workflow, changing the Native Floating Point Latency Strategy for a model, and rerunning step 3.2, the generated code is not updated based on the new Latency strategy.?
Description: While generating HDL code in the IP Core Generation Workflow, if you change?Latency Strategy ?for a model and?then re-run step 3.2 to generate HDL code, the HDL code does not get updated as per new Latency Strategy .?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code returns incorrect results?for deep learning input layers with normalization using identical?Min?and Max?values
Description: Generated code returns incorrect results?for deep learning networks if both  these conditions are true:   The network has an input layer whose Normalization  property is set to?'rescale-symmetric' ?or?'rescale-zero-one'  The  Min ?and Max ?properties of this input layer are set to identical values

Summary: UAV Scenario Designer app scales custom geometry to unit scale.
Description: In the UAV ScenarioDesigner app, importing a custom geometry using SceneObject > Custom Object option causes the imported geometry to besized down to unit scale between [-0.5,0.5].?This issue alsooccurs if you import a custom platform using Platform> Custom Platform.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for svd might produce incorrect results if the input is an empty matrix
Description: In the generated code,?[U,S,V] = svd(A) ?might return incorrect results for the outputs U  and V  if: You specify the input matrix A ?to be variable-size during code generation, and  The input matrix?A  reduces to an empty matrix at runtime.?  This issue can arise if any  of these conditions are true:?  ?You generate a MEX file ?You use svd  in a MATLAB function block ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable, and instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Long responses from?readline?and writeread?on?visadev?objects can contain corrupt data
Description: When using?readline ?or?writeread ?for?visadev ?objects, responses greater than 4096 characters might contain corrupt data. The following example shows data that is not corrupted and data that is corrupted from the same GPIB resource. resource = "GPIB0::5::INSTR";vd = visadev(resource);%% Set up the DMM to return 256 readings (after ~1 sec) - no data corruptionwriteline(vd, "*RST")writeline(vd, "SAMP:COUN 256") writeline(vd, "SAMP:SOUR TIM") writeline(vd, "SAMP:TIM 0. 01") writeline(vd, "TRIG:SOUR BUS") writeline(vd, "INIT") writeline(vd, "*TRG")%% Each ASCII reading requires 16 bytes,  which requires 16*256, or 4096, bytes of storage. correctData =  writeread(vd, "FETC?")%% Set up the DMM to return 257 readings (after ~1 sec) - data corruptionwriteline(vd, "*RST")writeline(vd, "SAMP:COUN 257") writeline(vd, "SAMP:SOUR TIM") writeline(vd, "SAMP:TIM 0. 01") writeline(vd, "TRIG:SOUR BUS") writeline(vd, "INIT") writeline(vd, "*TRG")incorrectData = writeread(vd, "FETC?")

Summary: Long processing time for the Model Advisor to check product license availability
Description: When executing checks, the Model Advisor verifies that the required product license is available. Sporadically, a long processing time occurs when the license checking mechanism is invoked by custom checks or when running edit-time checks.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup?activation and?updatediagram analyze all choices
Description: Incorrect Simulation results for modelscontaining inline variants with?startup ?activation combined when with?updatediagram analyze all choices? variant activations. The incorrect behavior is seen when all below conditionsare met: Model should contain variant source or sink withstartup ?activation and connected to another variant source or sink withupdate diagram analyze all choices? activation. Thevariant source or sink with update diagram?analyze?all choices  activationmust be inside the atomic subsystem.  Model is in Accelerator  modeSimulation or GRT code generation
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL code generation for Action Subsystems that are configured to be reusable can result in a validation model mismatch
Description: If Action Subsystems contain identical logic and are either marked atomic or SubsystemReuse  is set to Atomic and Virtual , the validation model could show a mismatch. Output selection logic on the Merge block may be wired incorrectly in the generated model and the resulting generated HDL code.
Workaround: Configure the Action Subsystems to not be reusable or set SubsystemReuse to off to avoid the validation model mismatch.>> hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'SubsystemReuse', 'off')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a model with message queues and configured for concurrent execution may cause memory corruption
Description: When a model has message queues and it is configured for concurrent execution, the generated code may introduce a race condition of multiple threads and this may cause memory corruption for these two cases:? The model contains a Queue block. In the model, there are other blocks that send messages to and receive messages from the Queue block with different rates, or multiple blocks send messages to the same Queue block with different rates. The model has multiple queues that have different rates and store messages with the same payload type. Message payload is of matrix or vector, bus, or fixed-point type, whose total size is greater than 8 bytes or you use row-major array layout.?
Workaround: If you don't need to allow concurrent execution, clear the?Allow tasks to execute concurrently on target?checkbox from Configuration Parameters dialog box.If you need to allow the concurrent execution, consider replacing messages with signals and use a Rate Transition block to transit between different rates. This generates the code with mutex but has a limitation as a signal can only has one value at any time step.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink incorrectly reuses memory for persistent data of type identical to a Simulink bus?in a MATLAB Function Block
Description: Code generated from a model that meets all of the following conditions might produce incorrect results: The model uses at least two instances of a MATLAB?Function Block from a library. The block has a persistent variable of bus type that has at least one variable-size field. The inferred bus type of the persistent variable is identical to a bus defined in the Type Editor (Bus Editor). For example, this can be achieved by assigning MATLAB Function input of bus type to the persistent variable at initialization. Two instances write different values to the persistent variable.?  The two instances might incorrectly use the same memory for their persistent variable.

Summary: Test execution can error out in SIL mode
Description: If a test case is configured to run in Software in the Loop (SIL) simulation mode and has signals logged in the Simulation Outputs section, then the execution can error out.

Summary: The installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler cannot connect to the internet to download MATLAB Runtime
Description: On Windows systems, when running the installer for the development version of MATLAB Web App Server in MATLAB Compiler, the installation fails and an error dialog displays the following message: "Call to download server failed (HTTP error code: 12007). Try to rerun the installer."?
Workaround: Follow instructions in the Limited Internet Connectivity Installation section of the?documentation.?

Summary: Assigning a custom name to ERTHeaderFileRootName?and generating code for a?model that uses a storage class generates an extra header
Description: The code generator generates an extra header file model .h ?when both of the following are true:  The model uses a storage class You assign a custom name to the configuration parameter ERTHeaderFileRootName ?  No other generated files reference the model .h  file.?The file might incorrectly include the header file configured in the storage class HeaderFile  property.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Generating an aggregated coverage report for test cases that use different test harnesses might error
Description: When generating an aggregated coverage report for coverage collected from different test harnesses the following error might occur:? Not Unique
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: On Linux platforms, the delete function errors when recycling is on
Description: On Linux platforms, when you attempt to delete a file with recycling turned on, MATLAB issues the error shown in this example code.  >> filename = fullfile(tempdir, "testfile.png"); >> fclose(fopen(filename , "w"));>> recycle("on");>> delete(filename);  An error occurred while initializing child process: While attempting to execute "mv -f ...
Workaround: You can work around this issue by turning recycling off.To determine if recycling is on, use the recycle function.>> recycleTo turn recycling off, call recycle with the "off" argument.>> recycle("off")To delete the file using operating system commands, use the system function.>> system("rm path/to/file")

Summary: Unused ports of PS-Simulink Converter block in generated HDL implementation model might cause error during HDL code generation
Description: Any unused output ports of PS-Simulink Converter block in generated HDL implementation model might lead to an error or incorrect result during HDL code generation.
Workaround: Terminate all floating/unused PS-S ports of simscape network or in the generated implementation model.

Summary: Security Issue: tab completion may execute code
Description: Using the tab completion feature for a command involving a MAT-file may execute code contained within the specified MAT-file.? If the MAT-file comes from an untrusted source, tab completion could have unexpected behavior.

Summary: Unable to apply custom filter designs to AD936x Transmitter or AD936x Receiver blocks.
Description: When you try to apply a custom filter to the AD936x Transmitter or AD936x Receiver blocks,?you get this error message even if you have already designed a filter in the?AD9361 Filter Design Wizard. Custom filter has been selected, but nocustom filter has been designed. You must design a custom filter first.
Workaround: To apply a custom filter to your AD936x Transmitter or AD936x Receiver block:Design the filter as usual in the AD9361 Filter Design Wizard, then click Apply filter design?and?close the wizard.Before clicking Apply under the Filter tab on the block mask, run this command at the MATLAB command line.your_filter_param =? get_param(gcb,'filterConfigStruct')Click Apply?under the?Filter?tab on the block mask.?Before running your Simulink model,?run this command at the MATLAB command line.set_param(gcb,'filterConfigStruct',your_filter_param)

Summary: Incorrect validation metrics when using the?trainNetwork?function and moving batch normalization statistics
Description: When you train a network with batch normalization layers using the?trainNetwork ?function with the BatchNormalizationStatistics ?training option set to?"moving" , ?the accuracy, RMSE, and loss validation metrics are incorrect.
Workaround: When you train the network, use an output function that reads from checkpoint networks and calculates the validation accuracy using the predict function.For an example, see the attached files.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Interacting with aggregated coverage results from subsystem harnesses run with fast restart iterations might error
Description: After simulating multiple harnesses for different subsystems in a model using fast restart iterations in the Simulink Test Manager, an error might occur when interacting with the aggregated coverage results. For example, when attempting to generate a coverage report for these aggregated results, you might see an assertion failure similar to:? failed assertion in B:\ matlab\ toolbox\ slcoverage\ src\ slvnv\ utils\ object. cpp at line 2471:? id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL?

Summary: HDL FIFO block generates an incorrect HDL code when the configuration parameters?Minimize clock enables and Minimize global resets are enabled.
Description: The Simulink model that contains HDL FIFO block generates an incorrect HDL code when you applied the following configuration parameters settings on the model: Minimize clock enables   is? 'On'? ? Minimize global resets  ? is? 'On'?   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.

Summary: MATLAB Java engine crashes when called from user defined threads
Description: If a call to MATLAB engine is made from a user defined Java thread, then the Java application crashes.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Security Issue: who -file might execute code
Description: Calling who -file  on a MAT-file can execute code contained within the specified MAT-file. If the MAT-file comes from an untrusted source, this function could have unexpected behavior.

Summary: "Error using attach_name"?when opening MATLAB apps on Windows with no internet connection
Description: When opening a MATLAB app on a Windows system without an internet connection, MATLAB throws this error:? Error using attach_name?  Unexpected internal error in shareEngine: System error: Failed to get current user groups?   Error in matlab.engine.isEngineShared (line 17)?   name = attach_name;?

Summary: The Comparison Tool errors because of missing SimBiology license
Description: If you have SimBiology installed, but do not have a license for SimBiology, opening the Comparison Tool throws the error: registerSimBiologyDiffGUIProviders requires a SimBiology license.
Workaround: Remove SimBiology from the path by executing the following commands in the MATLAB Command Window:rmpath(fullfile(matlabroot,"toolbox","simbio","simbio"))savepath

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0741?reports incorrect warning for variables used in condition action in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check updates to variables used in state transition conditions ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0741) ?reports incorrect warning for variables used in condition action. This happens when the variables are not updated inside the functions called in during action of state in Stateflow charts.

Summary: ecdf?might return incorrect answer for empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function when input data is double-censored
Description: The ecdf  function might return an incorrect answer for the empirical CDF, survivor function, or cumulative hazard function if double-censored input data is specified by using the?"Censoring"  name-value argument. An incorrect answer is returned by ecdf ?if the double-censored input data contains values at which there are: many right-censored observations no fully-observed observations no left-censored observations
Workaround: Interval-censoring is a generalization of double-censoring. Use the interval-censoring format by specifying the input argument y?as a two-column matrix with rows specifying the range of possible values for each observation. For an observation that occurs at the value t, the corresponding interval-censored format is:[t,t]?ˇŞ Fully observed at?t[¨CInf,t]?ˇŞ Left-censored at?t[t,Inf]?ˇŞ Right-censored at?t
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Logged signals not shown for tests using block diagram harness and parameter overrides if model dirtied during compilation
Description: A test that uses a block diagram harness and parameter overrides does not show the logged signals in the Test Manager if the model is dirtied by a callback during compilation, such as by an InitFcn  on a mask.
Workaround: Set the Dirty flag of the model to off in the postload callback of the test case:?set_param('<model_name>','Dirty','off');
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Nominal casting mode might remove unnecessary typecasts leading to warnings on compilers that expect explicit typecasts
Description: When you use set the configuration parameter Casting modes  to Nominal , the generated code contains minimum numbers of data type casting. Removing unnecessary typecasts might lead to warnings on compilers that expect explicit casts.Consider the below code: repro_Y. Out1 = ((uint32_T)repro_U. u < repro_U. u) + (uint32_T)repro_U. u;??  Here, the repro_U. u  is of type real32_T  and? repro_Y. Out1  is of type uint32_T . The conversion of uint32_T  to real32_T  happens without an unnecessary explicit casting. You may observe warnings on compilers that expect an explicit typecast.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Simulink Design Verifier generated test cases are exported to Simulink Test.
Description: MATLAB crashes if the test cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier are exported to Simulink Test when all the following conditions are true: The analysis is performed on a model block The referenced model contains Function Caller blocks without corresponding Simulink functions or blocks in the AUTOSAR Blockset Basic Software block library that model component calls to AUTOSAR Basic Software (BSW) services Harness source is set to Inport   The generated stack trace contains a line similar to: [? 0] 0x00007fb950ae67dc sl::simharness::HarnessMiscAPI::clearContTsSetting(double, slsvString, double)

Summary: Numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations when logged output has constant sample time
Description: When a model or subsystem under test has an output with constant sample time, you might observe numeric differences between SIL/PIL and normal mode simulations for these scenarios. Scenario 1:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Model reference  Show model reset ports ?- on   In the model under test, the output signal? with constant sample time is mapped to the Auto  storage class. The parent or harness model triggers the Reset  port of the Model block at a time greater than zero.  After the Reset? port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to zero, which is incorrect. Scenario 2:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  -?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  -?Model reference  Show model initialize port  - on  The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  At t = 0, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time is set to its final value, which is incorrect until the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal value must be zero. ?Scenario 3:  A Model block has these parameter settings: Simulation mode  - Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL)  Code interface  - Top model  Show model initialize port  - on   The parent or harness model triggers the Initialize  port at a time greater than zero.  After the Initialize  port trigger, the logged signal for the output with the constant sample time remains zero.Scenario 4:  A test harness is created for a subsystem in a model. An output of the subsystem has constant sample time. The SIL/PIL Manager or Simulink Test runs a SIL or PIL simulation of the subsystem under test.  The value logged for the output with constant sample time might be zero instead of the true value from the generated code.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 5, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Creating a Raspberry Pi connection fails in Desktop MATLAB
Description: In Desktop MATLAB with the MATLAB Support Package For Raspberry Pi Hardware installed, creating a connection to a Raspberry Pi board using the raspi  object fails. MATLAB displays the following error: picam.c:19:10: fatal error: RaspiCamControl.h: No such file or directory Updated standards in GitHub now require secure authentication using HTTPS. As a result, the raspberrypi/userland  repository in GitHub is not accessible from the existing installation scripts of the support package.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use either of the following:Install the latest support package updates for R2021a, R2021b, or R2022a.?For other releases, before creating the raspi object in MATLAB, run the following commands in a Raspberry Pi terminal:cd /optsudo git clone https://github.com/raspberrypi/userland.gitcd /opt/userlandsudo git pull originTip: You can use any software to SSH into Raspberry Pi to get the terminal window, or you can use the below link to launch a Raspberry Pi terminal window.https://magpi.raspberrypi.com/articles/terminal-help
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Description: MATLAB might crash while updating a model if the working directory is full or does not have write permissions, and any  of the following conditions are met:The model contains?a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function block. The simulation mode of the model is set to accelerator mode.  The crash might trigger an assertion or it might report a std::terminate() . On Linux, the crash error might be similar to this: ?<snip>?  ?------------------------------------------------------------------------  ? Assertion detected at XXXX-XX-XX XX:XX:XX +XXXX? ------------------------------------------------------------------------?  ?Assertion in void {anonymous}::llvmFatalErrorHandler(void*, const string&, bool) at vmrt/AutoExecutionEngine.cpp line XX:IO failure on output stream: No space left on device? ?<snip>  or?  ?<snip>?  ?------------------------------------------------------------------------  ? std::terminate() detected at XXXX-XX-XX XX:XX:XX +XXXX? ------------------------------------------------------------------------  ?<snip>? Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when applying a stereotype using the applyStereotype function
Description: MATLAB might crash when applying a stereotype using the?applyStereotype ?function in?the Command Window. MATLAB crashes if the profile that the stereotype is defined in is not imported into the architecture model or Simulink data dictionary.
Workaround: Ensure the profile is applied to the architecture model or Simulink Data Dictionary before applying a stereotype using the?applyStereotype?function in?the Command Window.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the?==?operator that compares handle objects might be incorrect
Description: If your MATLAB code applies the?== ?operator on handle objects, the generated C/C++ code might be incorrect.?  Specifically, the generated code might report that compared objects are different when they are actually equal. For example, consider this MATLAB code snippet: x = SomeClass();  % This line creates a handle object SomeClassy = someFunctionThatReturnsItsInput(x); % y is the same object as xout = x == y;  In the generated code, out ?might? return false ?even though its correct value is?true . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect prediction results can be created for sequential networks (those including one or more lstmLayers).
Description: For sequential networks that include? lstmLayers, incorrect predict results can be created. This issue typically occurs in networks with a large number of hidden state elements.

Summary: Stopping an external mode simulation that uses a TCP/IP or serial communication channel might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you stop simulation for models that simulate in external mode and use a TCP/IP or serial communication channel.

Summary: Using the datatip function to create data tips for primitive graphics objects such as Image causes command line errors
Description: Using the datatip  function to create datatips for primitive graphics objects such as Image  causes this callback error (reported as a warning): Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event MarkedClean defined for class  matlab.graphics.datatip.DataTip:  Error using matlab.graphics.chart.internal.convertDataSpaceCoordsToViewerCoords   The first input argument must be an axes handle.?   The datatip will be added, but in the wrong (default) location.
Workaround: In some cases, such as for line, it may be possible to resolve this by switching to the non-primitive version of objects. For example, instead of using a matlab.graphics.primitive.Line?object created using the line function, use a?matlab.graphics.chart.primitive.Line object created using the plot function. In other cases such as image objects, non-primitive versions of the graphics object are not available and there is no work around.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check position of signal labels (mathworks.jmaab.db_0097) displays an incorrect warning for signal lines
Description: Model Advisor subcheck?db_0097_a  displays incorrect warning for signal lines in Stateflow charts that are not controlled by the user, such as an output port in a Chart block.Warning  The following signals have labels which overlap otherobjects:?  Model/Chart/data

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When generating HDL code from MATLAB code with the MATLAB Datapath workflow, division operations between fixed-point values and constants may generate incorrect code
Description: Division operations may generate incorrect code when  generating HDL code from either of these options. A MATLAB function block that has? the HDL Block Property Architecture  set to?MATLAB Datapath  A MATLAB function with the Aggressive Dataflow Conversion ?parameter enabled in Optimizations? ?tab of the HDL Code Generation ?task in?the MATLAB HDL Workflow Advisor  In either case, division operations may generate incorrect code when both of these conditions are true. One operand of the division expression is a fixed-point value The other operand is a non-fixed-point constant value  For example, operations such as?x/0.5  and 2.1/x  may generate incorrect code if x  is a fixed-point value.
Workaround: To avoid generating incorrect code, follow one of these options:Cast the constant value to a fixed-point type, instead of using a double data type for the constant value. If the constant is on the right hand side of the division operation, consider multiplying by the inverse of the constant instead of dividing. For example, instead of x/0.5?rewrite the expression to be?x*2.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator might not correctly generate access function calls
Description: The code generator does not correctly generate access function calls when all ?of the following are true: The model contains a referenced model inside an iterator subsystem. The inputs and outputs of the iterator subsystem are mapped to signals that have the GetSet  access function storage class.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when Model block, Vector Concatenate block, and Root Outport block are connected in a series and the root outport maps to an access function storage class
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all of the following conditions are true: A Simulink model contains a?Model ?block. The output of the Model block is connected to a Vector Concatenate block. The Vector Concatenate?block is directly connected to the root outport. The root outport is mapped to an access function storage class with Access Mode  set to Pointer.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block for each output signal of the Model block that is connected to a Vector Concatenate block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR Classic model hierarchies that reference models containing global Simulink functions might generate incorrect code
Description: For AUTOSAR Classic model hierarchies, the generated code might be incorrect if models referenced in the hierarchy include a global Simulink Function block that contains external input or output ports.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Generated C++ code fails to use coder::bounded_array type
Description: In the affected releases, the C++ code generated for your MATLAB code might represent bounded variable-size arrays by using?a struct type named something like? emxArray_real_T_1x10 . In previous releases, such arrays were represented by using a C++ class template called coder::bounded_array .  This bug might impact the public interface of the generated C++ code. As a result, you might have to change your hand-written code that interfaces with the generated C++ code to use this alternate data type.?

Summary: Duplicate IDs when exporting SimBiology model to SBML result in an invalid model
Description: When you copy and paste model components in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, the IDs of components used for exporting the model to SBML are duplicated. Consequently, the exported SBML model is invalid because it does not satisfy the SBML standard that identifiers need to be unique.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: AUTOSAR compilation error: too many initializer values
Description: When there is a discrepancy between the arxml model regarding a structure layout (SwRecordLayout) and the corresponding structure, an error causes the compilation of a psar_prove_main.c  file to fail. This causes the failure of the verification of the corresponding AUTOSAR behavior.
Workaround: Edit the file psar_prove_main.c file to remove the incorrect initializer. For example, replace  static app_Appl_Map_Object const cst_init10264103644892694060 = Rte_InitValue_CData_Map_Object;with  static app_Appl_Map_Object const cst_init10264103644892694060 ; /* = Rte_InitValue_CData_Map_Object; */?Note that a file?psar_prove_main.c?is generated for every AUTOSAR software component. For each software component, the file is located in the subfolder of the results folder corresponding to the software component (the same subfolder as the file??psar_behavior.xhtml? for the software component). To locate this file:Open the file psar_project.xhtml that shows an overview of results for all software components.?Click on the Behaviors?tab to open detailed information about each software component behavior and then click the link to the appropriate psar_behavior.xhtml file. In your browser address bar, you can see the location of this file.Navigate to that location in your file explorer and find the corresponding??psar_prove_main.c.?Once you have the made the suggested edits to?psar_prove_main.c, rerun the analysis using the polyspace-autosar command. Use the option -do-not-update-autosar-prove-environment so that the file psar_prove_main.c is not regenerated and your edits are not overwritten.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Undefined MATLAB_MEX_FILE when you simulate custom code in a separate process (out-of-process execution)
Description: When you integrate custom code into Simulink using C Caller, C Function, Stateflow Chart, MATLAB Function, MATLAB System, or Truth Table?blocks and simulate that code in a separate process (out-of-process execution), the MATLAB_MEX_FILE  macro is not defined. As a result, code with an?#ifdef MATLAB_MEX_FILE  statement produces different results during simulation and code generation, with the expected answer during code generation.
Workaround: In the Configuration parameter dialog box, clear the?Simulate custom code in a separate process?parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Unexpected HDL code generation assertion when "SubsystemReuse" feature is turned on for the model
Description: This issue happens during HDL code generation. If code reuse is on for your model, during delay balancing, MATLAB may crash. You can further confirm that the crash is caused by this issue if you see similar stack trace as follows in your crash log: [? 0] 0x00007ffe07049533 pir::PirUtils::shortCircuit at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\util_hdl\PirUtils.cpp:564 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2022a_Update2\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)  [? 1] 0x00007ffe06e7d0ed pir::PathBalancerImpl::finish at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\PathBalancer.cpp:1003 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2022a_Update2\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)  [? 2] 0x00007ffe06e7cd13 pir::doitHelper at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\PathBalancer.cpp:2403 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2022a_Update2\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)  [? 3] 0x00007ffe06e7c8b5 pir::PathBalancer::doit at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\PathBalancer.cpp:2441 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2022a_Update2\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)  [? 4] 0x00007ffe0656dc4f pir::Ctx::balancePathDelays at B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\ctx.cpp:2544 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2022a_Update2\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll)
Workaround: Workaround is to turn off "SubsystemReuse". This can be done by running the following line in the Command Window:>> hdlset_param(model, 'SubsystemReuse', 'off')

Summary: Analyzing a model with an Observer Reference crashes MATLAB if any of the Observer Port blocks is inactive
Description: If you perform Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a model containing an Observer Reference  block, MATLAB will crash with a segmentation violation if any of the?Observer Port ?blocks is inactive during compilation.The generated stack trace contains a line similar to: [ 0] 0x00007ffa54d18148 DV::SLContext::cacheTraceInfo
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: On macOS Monterey?, minimizing or restoring a Simulink window or changing the display resolution might cause the Simulink Toolstrip to become unresponsive
Description: On macOS Monterey?, the Simulink Toolstrip might become unresponsive when you perform these actions: Minimize or restore a Simulink window Change display resolution or DPI, directly or by connecting or disconnecting external displays  The unresponsive Toolstrip continues to take actions when you click it and keyboard shortcuts work, but the Toolstrip provides no visual feedback to your actions.
Workaround: After you install R2022a Update 4 or later, running the attached p-file forceSimulinkUIRefresh might restore functionality. However, the script erases all the messages in the docked Diagnostics Viewer. To avoid erasing the messages, before running the p-file, undock the Diagnostics Viewer. If running the p-file does not work, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Input array of buses might be incorrectly treated as scalar when using?Build this subsystem
Description: Right-clicking a subsystem and selecting?C/C++ Code ?>?Build this subsystem ?might produce incorrect generated code or an unexpected error during the build process when both these conditions apply: One of the subsystem input ports receives an array of buses. A For Each block drives that input port.  The array of buses is incorrectly treated as a scalar.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect characteristic impedance computation of txlineStripline object
Description: getZ0  function computes an incorrect characteristic impedance for the txlineStripline  object.

Summary: Simulation results might differ based on whether an output signal of an intermediate Merge block is logged
Description: Simulation results might differ based on signal logging when all these conditions apply: The configuration parameter Diagnostics > Data Validity > Advanced parameters > Underspecified initialization detection  is set to Simplified . The model contains a Merge block driven by an output port of a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled, triggered, or action subsystem. Another Merge block drives the Outport block that corresponds to the output port of the conditionally executed subsystem. Signal logging is on for the output signal of the Merge block in the conditionally executed subsystem.  The simulation results might differ based on whether you log the output signal of the Merge block in the conditionally executed subsystem.
Workaround: Turn off signal logging for the output signal of the Merge block in the conditionally executed subsystem. Log the output of the final Merge block in the chain of Merge blocks instead.

Summary: Error when running a test harness for a Chart using the := operator
Description: When creating a test harness for a model with a Stateflow chart, where you use the :=  operator to assign a value to a Stateflow.Data  of scope Data Store Memory , the harness will have an unexpected source block driving a Data Store Write  for the variable used with the :=  operator.? Simulink will then throw an error when you try to update or run the harness:  Potential conflicting usages of identifier <DSM Name>: the signal connected to output port X of the block 'myHarness/Input Conversion Subsystem ', and a global data store memory <DSM Name>.
Workaround: Use the = operator instead of the := operator until the test harness is created. Once the harness is created, the := operator can be put back in the chart.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when tunable parameters in MATLAB Function block contain cell arrays
Description: MATLAB might crash when you use a structure variable that contains a cell array as a tunable parameter in a MATLAB Function block.

Summary: Build process might fail if models use C Caller blocks to interface external functions that use formal parameters of type pointer to matrix
Description: You may get build errors or warnings while building the C/C++ code generated from a model that meets these conditions: The model uses a C Caller block to integrate external functions. The functions use formal parameters of type pointer that point to matrix variables.??  Consider that a C Caller block integrates the following external function: typedef double myType[5];  double foo(const myType* in);  While building the generated, the code generator throws this error/ warning : cannot convert 'const real_T*' {aka 'const double*'} to 'const double (*)[5]'

Summary: The vision.ForegroundDetector object causes a MATLAB? crash when used with images of datatype uint8.
Description: The  vision.ForegroundDetector  object causes a MATLAB crash when trying to use it for detection. The failure occurs when:  You train a vision.ForegroundDetector  object using images of datatype uint8 .  and then, You save the vision.ForegroundDetector  object that was trained in a MAT file and load it into the workspace.  The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [ 0] 0x00007ffaa70a0910 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwforegroundDetector.dll+00067856 vision::ForegroundDetectorImpl<double,double>::operator=+00020224[ 1] 0x00007ffaa7097ef7 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwforegroundDetector.dll+00032503[ 2] 0x00007ffaa70a0176 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwforegroundDetector.dll+00065910 vision::ForegroundDetectorImpl<double,double>::operator=+00018278
Workaround: Convert the images to datatype single or double by using the?im2single or im2double?function before using the?vision.ForegroundDetector. Then, modify the InitialVariance name-value argument as needed. The default value for datatype uint8 is 30^2, and for type double and single, it is (30/255)^2.

Summary: Propeller block might calculate the wrong thrust and torque coefficients
Description: When you set Parameterization ?to Polynomial fit  and Blade pitch type ?to Constant , the Propeller block incorrectly adds the last polynomial term more than once. The block adds the last polynomial term M-1 times, where M is the length of the?kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) ?and?kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) ?parameters.
Workaround: Divide the last element of the kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) and kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0)?parameters by the vector length minus 1.?Alternatively, you can set Blade pitch type?to Controlled. Set the rows of the?kT table of polynomial coefficients (P/D, pN...p0) and kQ table of polynomial coefficients (P/D, pN...p0)?parameters to be duplicates of the kT polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) and?kQ polynomial coefficients (pN...p0) parameters, respectively.?

Summary: Certain mask parameters in Delta Sigma Modulator block reverts back to default values.
Description: If you edit certain mask parameters in the Delta Sigma Modulator block, they get overwritten by there default values and the edited values are not saved. The parameters are:? Coefficient 'a'  Coefficient 'g'  Coefficient 'b'  Coefficient 'c'

Summary: Blocks that have a ready output port assert ready while reset is high.
Description: In R2022a, the blocks assert the ready output while reset is high. Asserting ready during reset is misleading because the block drops any samples on the input data port while reset is high.? The bug affects these blocks and their equivalent System objects:  FFT and IFFT (when Architecture is Burst Radix 2) Discrete FIR Filter (when Filter structure is Partly serial systolic) FIR Interpolator  CIC Interpolator  Farrow Rate Converter FIR Rate Converter (when Enable ready output port is selected)

Summary: MATLAB crashes when imfinfo reads metadata from a TIFF image
Description: MATLAB crashes when imfinfo is used to read metadata from a TIFF image with unrecognized tags having non-ASCII characters.

Summary: Using the visadev function with a TCP/IP socket resource generates an error
Description: Constructing a visa TCP/IP socket resource with the visadev  function generates an error. For example: socketResource = "TCPIP0::127.0.0.1::5025::SOCKET";vd = visadev(socketResource);Unable to find library for given resource.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: Internal error thrown when generating code for delay block with enable port
Description: If a Simulink model contains a delay block with an enable port, vector inputs, and vector outputs, and those signals have different vector orientations (i.e. unoriented vector input, row vector output), you might see an internal error thrown during HDL code generation. The error can only be thrown if optimizations are requested such as resource sharing or distributed pipelining. This is an example of internal error shown: Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/createCGIR  Compilation errors when generating code for: example_model/DUT/Delay_Switch    Error during MATLAB code compilation:  Error Path:  C:\MATLAB\R2022a\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\emlauthoring\wireops\hdleml_switch_multiport.p  Error Location: (@Row: --> 1 @Column: --> 1)  Size mismatch (size [1 x 43] ~= size [43 x 1]).  The size to the left is the size of the left-hand side of the assignment.  Internal Error: This error occurred inside a MathWorks function.?
Workaround: Keep the dimensions the same between the input and output signals of the enabled delay. If the output signal needs to have the dimensions reshaped, consider using a Reshape block or Transpose block.

Summary: Crash while generating HDL code for model containing Constrained Output Pipeline requests.
Description: If a model contains Constrained Output Pipeline requests, MATLAB might crash while generating HDL code. The stack trace for the crash from this issue can contain these lines: pir::CtxIterator::advance() at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/dom_pir_core/iterator.cpp:70?  pir::ConstrainedRetiming::doit(pir::Ctx*, bool) at /matlab/src/cgir_hdl/optimizations/ConstrainedRetiming.cpp:434 ?
Workaround: To reliably avoid the crash, remove Constrained Output Pipeline requests by setting the ConstrainedOutputPipeline?option back to its default, 0. Another way to avoid the crash is to ensure there is no unnecessary/unused subsystem hierarchy underneath the subsystem containing the Constrained Output Pipeline requests. If the subsystem output is not used by downstream logic, HDL Coder considers it dead logic and removes it from the generated HDL code.

Summary: Error occurs while reading a TDMS-file with empty channel groups
Description: Reading a TDMS-file that contains a channel group without any channels, an error is thrown. For example, in the following code the file NoChannel.tdms  contains a channel group without any channels: tdmsread("NoChannel.tdms") Invalid argument at position 3. Value must be a scalar.

Summary: Some optimizations are disabled when using trainNetwork
Description: Some internal optimizations are disabled for trainNetwork . As a consequence, performance may be suboptimal when there are layer patterns that can be optimized, such as the Convolution+ReLU pattern.

Summary: The ilaplace function might omit a heaviside factor in some transformation results
Description: If the input of the inverse Laplace transform is of the form exp(-a*s)*g(s)  with a>=0 , then ilaplace  might mistakenly omit a heaviside factor in the result when it contains a sum of a dirac term and other terms. For example, ilaplace(exp(-s)*(s+1)/(s-1))  returns the wrong result dirac(t - 1) + exp(t - 1) + heaviside(t - 1)*exp(t - 1) , instead of the correct result dirac(t - 1) + 2*heaviside(t - 1)*exp(t - 1) .?

Summary: Improper characters are displayed when outputting non-ASCII characters from a standalone application
Description: Improper characters are displayed when outputting non-ASCII characters from a standalone application.? This happens when the characters are output using the MATLAB function disp  or fprintf .

Summary: Calling custom code function with constant pointer of an alias type argument in C Caller or C Function blocks may stall simulation and code generation
Description: Simulation and code generation may stall whencalling a custom code function using a C Caller or C Function block when thefollowing conditions are met:  There is aglobal Simulink.AliasType  (for example, INT )? A custom code function withthe following signature is called in a C Caller or C Functionblock   void customCodeFunction(const INT *  arg)  This issue is not observed for C structureand enumeration types.
Workaround: The stall can be prevented if the const-nessof the custom code function argument is removed.

Summary: Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted in model when test harness created for Subsystem Reference block
Description: If you create a test harness for a Subsystem Reference that contains a Requirements Table block, a Recovered Stateflow Charts block might be added to the model that contains the Subsystem Reference block when you close and then reopen the model.

Summary: Zybo Z7-10 Zynq Linux image hyperlink is broken in the?Define Custom Board andReference Design for Zynq Workflow?example.
Description: Zybo Z7-10 Zynq Linux image hyperlink used in the?Define Custom Board and Reference Design for Zynq Workflow? example is not working in R2022a.

Summary: Creating test harness for Subsystem model with Requirements Table block produces an error
Description: When you create a test harness for a Subsystem Reference model that contains a Requirements Table produces an error message with the following error stack:Error using bdIsLoadedInvalid block diagram name supplied. Valid block diagram names are the same as valid MATLAB variable names. Type "helpisvarname" for more information.Error in slreq.data.RequirementSetError in slreq.data.RequirementSet/dirtySlxModel ... ? Error in Simulink.harness.dialogs.createDialog/dlgPostApplyMethod - Show complete stack traceError using bdIsLoadedInvalid block diagram name supplied. Valid block diagram names are the same as valid MATLAB variable names. Type "helpisvarname" for more information.Error in slreq.data.RequirementSetError in slreq.data.RequirementSet/dirtySlxModel

Summary: Python virtual environments are not accessible on Windows platforms for Python 3.X versions
Description: When a Python executable in a virtual environment is used as the version for pyenv , the Python fails to start when called from MATLAB by using any py.<command>, for Python 3.X versions on Win64. For example:?? ?>> pyenv  ? PythonEnvironment with properties:    ? ? ? ? ? Version: "3.9"  ? ? ? ?Executable: "C:\Documents\Python\Envs\myenv\Scripts\pythonw.exe"  ? ? ? ? ? Library: "C:\Python\python39.dll"  ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Home: "C:\Documents\Python\Envs\myenv"  ? ? ? ? ? ?Status: NotLoaded  ? ? ExecutionMode: OutOfProcess  >> py.print('test')  Transport stopped.
Workaround: To use a Python virtual environment:1. Set the pyenv 'Version' to the Python used to create the virtual environment.?2. Add the Python library path of the virtual environment to the sys.path to access Python modules installed for that virtual environment.?Example:??Assume when you type python?at the command line, it runs the executable C:\Python\python.exe which is used to create a virtual environment called myenv?located in C:\Documents\Python\Envs\myenv. These commands provide access to the Python libraries installed for myenv?using the base executable C:\Python\python.exe.>> pyenv('Version',? 'C:\Python\python.exe');>> pypath = py.sys.path;>> pypath.insert(int32(0),? 'C:\Documents\Python\Envs\myenv\Lib\site-packages');

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor errors out when the interface protocol signals of AXI Master interface are not mapped to design under test (DUT) interface
Description: When you use AXI Master  interface?for your Simulink model, all the interface protocol signal for AXI Master interface?must be mapped to target DUT interface. HDL Workflow Advisor throws an error if all these AXI4 Master Read  and?  AXI4 Master W rite  channel interfaces are not mapped: Read channel ports, such as? axim_rd_data ,?axim_rd_s2m ,?axim_rd_m2s   Write channel ports, such as?axim_wr_data ,?axim_wr_s2m ,?axim_wr_m2s

Summary: Error about theaterPlot function can occur when simulating atest bench model that includes highway lane change planner
Description: When you simulate the test bench model from the GenerateCode for Highway Lane Change Planner example or Highway Lane Change Planner andController example, Simulink returns this error if you do not have a license for SensorFusion and Tracking Toolbox or Radar Toolbox: Undefined function 'theaterPlot' for input arguments of type'matlab.graphics.axis.Axes'.? ?

Summary: Crash or error during coverage simulation for external MATLAB function
Description: If you have a model which calls external MATLAB files with coverage enabled, MATLAB might error or crash if all of the following conditions are true: All MATLAB editors are closed before first coverage simulation The external MATLAB file used by the model is opened in editor before second simulation.  Error message in the MATLAB command prompt:  Simulation failed  Caused by:      Error using cvsim    Error in 'ModelName/ExternalMATLABFile' while executing C MEX S-function 'sf_sfun', (mdlSetupRuntimeResources), at time 0.0.          Error using cvsim        Unexpected Standard exception from MEX file.        What() is:basic_string::substr: __pos (which is 47) > this->size() (which is 0)

The connection line connects to a connection port of a linked Variant Subsystem block. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Some statements that include combinations of casting, rescaling, and bit concatenation operations using the rescale and bitconcat functions lead to incorrect code generation.
Description: Some statements that include combinations of casting, rescaling, and bit concatenation operations using the rescale  and bitconcat  functions lead to incorrect code generation.For example, this code produces incorrect code for VHDL:?if ?rescale(in1,1) >= bitconcat(a,b)?? ?out(:) = a;end?
Workaround: Move casting, rescaling, and bit concatenation operations?to separate program statements. For example:x = rescale(in1, 1)y = bitconcat(a,b)?if x >= y? ?out(:) = a;end

Summary: Lookup Table Editor stalls when opening a masked lookup table
Description: Lookup Table Editor stalls when opening a lookup table that meets the following conditions: The lookup table block has a mask. The block mask promotes parameters of the lookup table or assigns mask variables to dialog parameters of the lookup table. The mask dialog has a variable passed that matches the name of the promoted parameter or mask variable.  For example, a base workspace variable myTable  is passed to the mask dialog for a mask parameter myTable  that specifies the lookup table block parameter Table . Alternatively, if Table  is a promoted parameter, a base workspace variable name?Table  causes the issue.
Workaround: When the Lookup Table Editor stalls, press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C) at the command prompt to interrupt the stall and close the Lookup Table Editor.Rename any variables that meet the conditions listed in the description.Restart the Lookup Table Editor.

Summary: Providing CodeCoveragePlugin with both the CoverageReport and CoberturaFormat objects might result in missing or inaccurate reports?
Description: If you create a plugin to simultaneously generate code coverage reports in both the HTML and Cobertura XML formats, MATLAB might throw an error or the plugin might generate inaccurate reports. For example, this code might error or produce inaccurate reports. import matlab.unittest.plugins.CodeCoveragePluginimport matlab.unittest.plugins.codecoverage.CoverageReportimport matlab.unittest.plugins.codecoverage.CoberturaFormat  suite = testsuite(pwd);runner = testrunner("textoutput");reportFormat = [CoverageReport CoberturaFormat("report.xml")];p = CodeCoveragePlugin.forFolder(myFolder,Producing=reportFormat);runner.addPlugin(p)results = runner.run(suite);?
Workaround: Instead of specifying multiple report formats for a plugin, generate the code coverage reports using separate test runs. For example, generate both HTML and Cobertura XML coverage reports for the source code in a folder.import matlab.unittest.plugins.CodeCoveragePluginimport matlab.unittest.plugins.codecoverage.CoverageReportimport matlab.unittest.plugins.codecoverage.CoberturaFormatsuite = testsuite(pwd);runner1 = testrunner("textoutput");p1 = CodeCoveragePlugin.forFolder(myFolder,Producing=CoverageReport);runner1.addPlugin(p1);results1 = runner1.run(suite);runner2 = testrunner("textoutput");p2 = CodeCoveragePlugin.forFolder(myFolder,Producing=CoberturaFormat("report.xml"));runner2.addPlugin(p2);results2 = runner2.run(suite);??

Summary: Web browser displays a blank page when clicking a link to a Polyspace Access result
Description: Your web browser might display a blank page when you click a?link to a Polyspace Access result from: A web page containing a link to an Access result An excel report containing a link to an Access result Any other source referencing a Polyspace Access result
Workaround: To display the Polyspace Access page, reload the page in the web browser.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling the visdiff function on SimBiology comparison result objects with deleted models
Description: MATLAB crashes in the following workflow: Create a SimBiology comparison results diffResults  using the sbiodiff  function. Delete one or both models. Call visdiff(diffResults) .
Workaround: Instead of inspecting the diff results in the Comparison app using the visdiff function, check the?Comparisons property of the results object returned by sbiodiff. That is,diffResults = sbiodiff(model1,model2);diffResutls.Comparisons

Summary: HDL FIFO block with Local Reset Port parameter enabled?may generate incorrect Simulation and HDL code generation results
Description: The Simulation and HDL code generation results from a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a HDL FIFO   block Local reset port    parameter is set to  true

Summary: The output from 'IsNew' port of ROS 2 Subscribe block stays true in code-generation
Description: The output from 'IsNew' port of ROS 2 Subscribe block stays?true ?in code-generation?even if no new messages are received. It must stay false ?until any message is received, and only change to true ?thereafter.

Summary: Intermittent read timeout warnings or errors using visadev
Description: Performing one of the following read operations on a?visadev ?object might cause intermittent read timeout warnings or errors. readline  readbinblock  writeread   You might see one of these warning or error messages. Warning: The specified amount of data was not returned within the Timeout period for 'readline'.'visadev' unable to read any data. For more information on possible reasons, see visadev Read Warnings.  Warning: The specified amount of data was not returned within the Timeout period for 'readbinblock'. 'visadev' unable to read any data. For more information on possible reasons, see visadev Read Warnings.  The specified terminator was not read before the timeout period expired.

Summary: web command not honoring System Web browser preference
Description: The web ?command opens external websites in the system browser, even when the Use system web browser when opening links to external sites (recommended) ?preference in the MATLAB Web preferences is cleared. For example, the command web("www.mathworks.com")  always opens the?www.mathworks.com ?web page?in the system browser.
Workaround: Open the Preferences window and in the MATLAB > Web?preferences, clear the?Use system web browser when opening links to external sites (recommended) preference.Type web?in the Command Window to?open the MATLAB web browser.Enter the desired URL in the Location box.

Summary: Consistency checking might incorrectly flag issues when using zero-crossing detection with a fixed-step solver
Description: When you run a simulation that uses a fixed-step solver with zero-crossing detection enabled, solver consistency checking might falsely report issues for some models.

Summary: Compatibility check on an Atomic subsystem of a top model fails when parameter configuration is Automatically infer parameter specification or Determine from generated code
Description: Design verifier supports generating testcases for the code generated for a subsystem when we already have generated code for the top model. However, the compatibility check fails when parameter configuration is set to either Automatically infer parameter specification  or Determine from generated code.  Sample error message:? Code path provided was not usable for parameter analysis. Simulink Design Verifier uses the directory '<currentDirectory>/<TopModel>_ert_rtw' for parameter analysis on generated code. Regenerate code using embedded coder or copy generated code to this folder.?

Summary: Analysis terminates with an Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end message
Description: Simulink Design Verifier analysis may terminate with an Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end  message when the model has a function with the name of Outputs
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Restoring simulation from a saved operating point might give wrong answer for certain Stateflow charts
Description: Restoring simulation of a model containing a Stateflow chart from an operating point might give wrong answer when you save an operating point, modify the operating point by changing a chart data that is written only in entry actions, and restore the simulation from the modified operating point.  The restored simulation might not reflect your change in data.
Workaround: Put a breakpoint in the chart before restoring simulation.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Stack overflow exception with Polyspace Code Prover on Windows systems
Description: On Windows, when analyzing applications that exceed certain thresholds of size or complexity, Polyspace Code Prover might crash at Software Safety Analysis Level 0 with a stack overflow message such as: exception Stack overflow(Stack overflow) raised.

Summary: LibCacheDefine function does not add specified?buffer to <model>_private.h or <model>.c file in the generated code
Description: When using the TLC code configuration function LibCacheDefine ?to add a buffer to the <model>_private.h  or <model>.c  file, the generated code does not contain the buffer passed to the function as an argument. This might result in a compiler error.

Summary: Design Verifier incorrectly reports design errors in IRT Subsystems and System Objects as Valid
Description: In Design Error Detection workflow, for models containing design errors in the Init subsystem of IRT  models and initialization of System Object s,? Design Verifier reports them as Valid  even if they can be falsified .??
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Unrecognized function or variable error when displaying help in the?MATLAB File Help window
Description: If the graphics root object units are set to?inches,?centimeters,?normalized, or?characters, MATLAB is unable to display file help in the MATLAB File Help window and the error Unrecognized function or variable 'bottom'  displays in the Command Window.
Workaround: Set the graphics root object units to?pixels or?points. For example, enter this command in the Command Window:set(groot,'Units','pixels')

Summary: Simulink Coverage does not report the implicit default case as a decision outcome
Description: Simulink Coverage measures decision coverage for a switch statement and reports each switch case as a decision outcome. However, if a switch statement does not have an explicit default case statement, Simulink Coverage does not report the implicit default case as a decision outcome.For example, Simulink Coverage should report the following switch statement as having three outcomes but instead reports only two: switch (x) {  ? case 1:  ? ? ? ??/* action 1 */  ? ? break;  ? case 2:  ? ? /* action 2 */?  ? ? break;?  }
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: phased.Platform and Motion Platform block errors out in MATLAB and Simulink with multiple custom trajectories
Description: phased.Platform in MATLAB errors out when MotionModel property is set to Custom and multiple trajectories containing both position and velocity at each time stamp are specified. Similarly, Motion Platform block in Simulink errors out when the "Model of object motion" parameter is set to Custom. The error message reads "Too many input arguments.".
Products: Phased Array System Toolbox, Radar Toolbox
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Unable to export SLDV Testcases to the Test Manager
Description: While exporting generated SLDV  Testcases to Simulink Test  it fails with an error message  error occurred converting from cell to Simulink. SimulationData. Dataset . The error is shown for models with multidimensional output signals.

Summary: Setting for C++ mapping property 'Data Access' is not preserved in model
Description: For models with the target language set to C++, the 'Data Access' property configured in the code mappings is not written to the model file. If the parameter argument 'Data Access' is set to 'pointer', the value of the data access property is lost after the model is saved and reloaded.?
Workaround: The 'Data Access' property can be saved in a model workspace parameter by using the following steps:?1. Configure the model PostLoadFcn.a. Open Model Explorer.b. In the 'Model Hierarchy' panel, click the affected model.c. In the 'Model Properties' panel, select the 'Callbacks' tab.d. In the 'Model callbacks' panel, select 'PostLoadFcn'.e. Paste the following MATLAB code into the 'Model Post-Load Function' dialog box:hws = get_param(bdroot, 'modelworkspace');access = getVariable(hws, 'access');cm = coder.mapping.utils.create(bdroot);?cm.setData('ModelParameterArguments','DataAccess', access);2. Configure the model PreSaveFcn.a. Open Model Explorer.b. In the 'Model Hierarchy' panel, click the affected model.c. In the 'Model Properties' panel, select the 'Callbacks' tab.d. In the 'Model callbacks' panel, select 'PreSaveFcn'.e. Paste the following MATLAB?code into the 'Model Pre-Save Function' dialog?box:hws = get_param(bdroot, 'modelworkspace');hws.DataSource = 'Model File';cm = coder.mapping.utils.create(bdroot);access = cm.getData('ModelParameterArguments','DataAccess');hws.assignin('access',access);?

Summary: Comparison signal data type not saved in Simulation Data Inspector session file
Description: When you reload a Simulation Data Inspector session file, the data type of the comparison signal is double, regardless of its data type when you saved the file.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading a model that references a library that was saved before R2020b
Description: MATLAB crashes when all of these conditions are met:?Model Lib  is a library that has at least one signal object specification on one of its signal lines. The signal object specifies a non-Auto  storage class. Model?Lib  was? saved in a release prior to R2020b. Model A  references a block from library Lib . Model B ?uses model A  as a referenced sub-model.  In this case, MATLAB crashes when the find_mdlrefs  function is called on model B . A crash could also occur during model load.
Workaround: To avoid a crash, use one of these approaches:Open the referenced library before opening the model that references it.Open the referenced library and save it in a current release (R2020b or later). Then open the model that references the library.

Summary: Updating or compiling a software architecture model with a component containing a client-server port and a regular data port may fail and cause an error
Description: When updating or compiling a software architecture model that contains a client-server port and a data port, the model compilation or update might fail and you might encounter the following error:  Invalid setting in 'component/Out1' for parameter 'OutDataTypeStr.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails when?the model contains a root-level outport driven by an inactive state owner
Description: When a model contains a root-level outport driven by a state owner block that is located within an inactive variant subsystem, then the compatibility check fails with the following error message: Building model representation failed: The Outport block <block_path> has the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' turned on but Simulink cannot guarantee that the outport will be virtual in generated code due to the following reasons. One of the ways to correct this issue is to set the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' to 'off'.?  The error message states to set the parameter?Ensure outport is virtual  ?to?off  ? even? when the parameter is cleared.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for AUTOSAR model?with a multidimensional port parameter and ArrayLayout set to Row-major
Description: When a model is configured for AUTOSAR code generation, incorrect code might be generated when these conditions exist:Model is configured with ArrayLayout? set to  Row-major .  Model contains a parameter mapped as a port parameter.

Summary: Web apps access a webcam on the server machine instead of the client's machine
Description: A web app deployed to MATLAB Web App Server that uses the MATLAB webcam function accesses a webcam physically located on the server machine instead of the webcam on the client's machine.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0753?displays incorrect warning for?division operator used in transitions
Description: The?Sub-ID a1? of??Check condition actions and transition actions in Stateflow? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0753 )? displays incorrect warning when division operator is used in transitions in?Stateflow charts.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: graydist returns incorrect results for images with large number of pixels
Description: The gray-weighted distance transform performed by the graydist  function returns incorrect output for input images with?2^16 pixels or more.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using For Each subsystem in Switch Case Action or If Action?subsystem might produce incorrect simulation results in Accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when a model uses a For Each subsystem inside an Action subsystem and all the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple instances of the For Each subsystem. The function packaging  parameter of the For Each subsystem is set to either Auto ?or Reusable function . The For Each subsystem contains signals with continuous sample time. The model runs in Just-In-Time (JIT) accelerator mode.?  Note that this problem occurs only in JIT accelerator mode. Normal and classic accelerator mode simulations produce correct values.
Workaround: Switch to classic accelerator mode by using:set_param(0,'GlobalUseClassicAccelMode','on')
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Numerical error for output of lookup atan2?function in Trigonometric Function block with certain settings
Description: Simulation and generated code can produce a numerical error for output of the lookup atan2 ?function in the Trigonometric Function block when all of the following conditions are true: The?Approximation method? parameter?is set to Lookup .  The?Angle unit ?parameter is set to radian .  The?Table data type? parameter is set to a fixed point type that has an integer length less than 4 bits, for example: sfix16_En13 .

Summary: MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that includes 'sltopo::destinationBlocksOf'
Description: During model compilation or simulation, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that includes the text 'sltopo::destinationBlocksOf' . This crash mostly happens for models that include port blocks, such as Inport and Outport blocks, or variants.

Summary: The Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) block exerts force with the opposite mechanical orientation as indicated in the?Actuator mechanical orientation?parameter setting
Description: When Actuator mechanical orientation  is  Pressure at A causes positive displacement of R relative to C , the block behaves as if? Actuator mechanical orientation ?is? Pressure at A causes negative displacement of R relative to C.  When?Actuator mechanical orientation ?is? Pressure at A causes negative displacement of R relative to C , the block behaves as if? Actuator mechanical orientation ?is? Pressure at A causes positive displacement of R relative to C.
Workaround: Change the?Actuator mechanical orientation parameter to the opposite of the intended setting.

Summary: Creation of?python virtual environment fails on macOS Apple Silicon M1 when pyenv?is configured to use native arm64 python.
Description: When executing?rosinit? on macOS Apple Silicon M1 with  pyenv  configured to use native arm64 python installed by the homebrew, the following error is encountered while creating python virtual environment in R2022a.  Error text: [31mERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pyyaml (from versions: none)[0m  [31mERROR: No matching distribution found for pyyaml[0m  . Use pyenv function to set the path to the Python executable and retry the command.?
