Recreate the python virtual environment using the following command in MATLAB:? ? ? ? ? ? ros.internal.createOrGetLocalPython(true);?? ? ? ? 3. Execute rosinit?on macOS Apple Silicon M1.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect C code generated when function-call subsystem writes to its output conditionally.
Description: The code generator produces incorrect C code when these conditions are true: The top model contains multiple function-call subsystems.? Signal lines that connect the function-call subsystems represent data transfers (a function-call subsystem consumes the output of another).  At least one of the data transfer signals is?updated conditionally. The top model is configured with an AUTOSAR system target file.  In the generated code, the variable or buffer for the transferred data is allocated on a stack and initialized and updated conditionally, but is used unconditionally.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block before the outport of a function-call subsystem that writes to a data transfer signal that is updated conditionally. For the added Signal Conversion block, select block parameter Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 6, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Jobs with diary output submitted from R2019b and earlier to a multi-release MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster hang in the 'running' state
Description: Jobs and tasks from versions R2016a to R2019b that you submit to a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster running services from R2022a get stuck in the 'running ' state if they produce diary output.
Workaround: Disable diary output for MATLAB jobs and tasks from versions R2016a to R2019b using the following syntax:>> job = batch(...,'CaptureDiary',false);>> task = createTask(...);>> task.CaptureDiary = false;

Summary: Assignment statements in C charts might have incorrect number of condition coverage objectives
Description: Coverage results for a logical expression in an assignment statement found in a C chart might incorrectly show duplicated condition objectives. This issue occurs if the C chart is inside a For Iterator Subsystem or inside a model that is referenced multiple times in a model reference hierarchy. This issue does not occur for MAL charts. The number of duplicated condition coverage objectives increases each time you simulate the model in the same session, resulting in an incorrectly high number of total condition coverage objectives and an incorrectly low condition coverage percentage shown in the coverage report. The difference in the number of coverage objectives between subsequent simulations of the same model causes this error message during coverage aggregation:? Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.   This error appears for any coverage aggregation method, including Simulink Test Manager or manually adding two cvdata  objects.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: BackgroundColor?property of uidatepicker has no effect
Description: The background color of a date picker component appears as white (the default), no matter which color is specified. For example, this code creates a white date picker:? uidatepicker('BackgroundColor','red')

Summary: Bus struct typedefs appear in a nondeterministic order in generated header file
Description: If a model utilizes variants and references Simulink.Bus  objects with?DataScope ?set to Auto , then the corresponding typedefs may appear in the generated header files in a nondeterministic order that may change from one session to the next.
Workaround: Set the?DataScope?property of the Simulink.Bus objects to Imported or Exported.

Summary: Steaming and resource sharing optimization report can have incomplete information when requesting both optimizations on a subsystem
Description: When you enable both the streaming and resource sharing optimizations on a subsystem and generate HDL code, the resulting optimization report and highlighting diagnostics can be incomplete. In the resource sharing report, duplicate sharing group ID's might be used. In the streaming report, some blocks might not be highlighted that are part of the streaming group in the original model, if the streamed logic is also shared.

Summary: Analysis functions of dsp.Channelizer object return incorrect filters for non-maximally decimated cases
Description: For the dsp.Channelizer object, when DecimationFactor is less than NumFrequencyBands (non-maximally decimated channelizer), the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions depict incorrect filters. To obtain the correct channelizer filter, the prototype filter is supposed to be shifted by 2¦Đ?M, where M is the number of frequency bands. In the case of non-maximally decimated channelizer, the getFilters, fvtool, and freqz object functions shift the prototype filter by 2¦Đ?D to calculate the filters, where D is the decimation factor.  Note that the Channelizer object uses the correct set of filters in the implementation in the step function. The issue occurs only when you use the getFilters, fvtool, and the freqz object functions.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Model Advisor Exclusions menu does not get the correct block type for Stateflow blocks.
Description: On right-clicking a Truth Table block, the Model Advisor Exclusions menu item seen is "Exclude all blocks of type Stateflow", instead of "Exclude all blocks of type Truth Table". This might happen for any Stateflow related block like a Chart, Truth Table, or State Transition Table.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation of model with nested If Action Subsystem blocks that contain blocks using elapsed simulation time might produce incorrect numeric results
Description: Incorrect numeric results might occur in simulation when all of the following conditions are true. Model?Solver Type ?is set to?Fixed-step .  Model has a topology consisting of three layers:  Layer 1: An If block controlling an If Action Subsystem block execution.  Layer 2: An If block controlling an If Action Subsystem block inside Layer 1's If Action Subsystem block.  Layer 3: A Triggered Subsystem block containing a Discrete-Time Integrator block (or any block that uses elapsed simulation time) inside Layer 2's If Action Subsystem block.   The Action Port block of Layer 2's If Action Subsystem block has the States when execution is resumed  parameter set to  held .  The model runs into this sequence of steps during any part of the simulation:   ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Step 1? ? ? ?Step 2? ? ? ?Step 3 Layer 1's If block output:? ? ? ?Active? ? ? Inactive? ? ?Active   Layer 2's If block output:? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active   Triggered subsystem:? ? ? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active? ? ? ?Active     Under these conditions, incorrect elapsed time might be calculated and used in the computation of the discrete-time integrator's output at Step 3.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Model Advisor checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis do not work when combined with other checks requiring extensive analysis when using Advisor.Application API
Description: When using the Advisor.Application  API to run a Model Advisor analysis, if the provided configuration has a combination of checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis along with other checks requiring extensive analysis, the checks requiring Simulink Design Verifier analysis will wrongly show a passing status.

Summary: MATLAB Online or MATLAB Online Server might crash when calling rmdir on a project root folder in a function that closes the project
Description: MATLAB might crash when both  of the following conditions are met: You are using MATLAB Online or MATLAB Online Server. You programmatically removed a project root folder immediately after closing the project.?  Example code:proj = currentProject;rootFolder = proj.RootFolder;proj.close();rmdir(rootFolder);
Workaround: To avoid this problem, introduce a small pause between closing a project and deleting its root folder:Example code:?proj = currentProject;rootFolder = proj.RootFolder; proj.close();pause(0.2);rmdir(rootFolder);

Summary: MATLAB might crash, error or stall during multi-GPU communication on Windows
Description: Windows users with multiple NVIDIA GPUs upgrading to the latest graphics drivers might see crashes, errors, stalls or other problems for communicating work on multiple GPUs, such as multi-GPU training of a deep learning network.? The following conditions must apply to see this bug:? Windows machine with multiple NVIDIA GPUs At least one of the GPUs is using the WDDM driver model NVIDIA driver version is in the 500 series or later Code uses multi-GPU communication  For example, if you train a deep learning neural network using the trainNetwork ?function, with the "ExecutionEnvironment"  training option set to  "multi-gpu" , you might see a "CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS " error followed by a stall that can only be recovered by deleting the parallel pool. Your network might also fail to train, with the accuracy not improving or getting worse during the course of training. To check whether you are affected, run the NVIDIA System Management Interface utility by calling nvidia-smi ?in a Windows command prompt. The NVIDIA System Management Interface displays the driver version number and shows the driver model being used by each device under the TCC/WDDM heading.?
Workaround: If your GPU is not driving a display, you may be able to switch it to the TCC driver model. To switch call?nvidia-smi -i x -dm 1?in a Windows command prompt where x is replaced by the index of the GPU in question (starting at 0). To see how to select each of your devices, consult the NVIDIA documentation by calling?nvidia-smi -h. Reboot your machine for this change to take effect.An alternative workaround is to downgrade your GPU driver to a 400 series version. To download a 400 series GPU driver, visit the NVIDIA driver downloads website and select "BETA Drivers and Archive".

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading the MAT file generated by a Simulink model containingdashboard blocks that tune parameter values
Description: When a model contains a dashboard block that tunes a parameter value, the MAT file created by logging Dataset  data to a file is unavailable for modification after simulation ends. Operations that try to access the file result in a crash or an error. Whenyou try to load the MAT file, MATLAB crashes and the stack trace might containthese lines:  [  0] 0x00007ff87fa1908d mcos::InterfaceHeader::getInterface at B:\matlab\src\mcos_impl\cos_interface\matfileio.cpp:2521   [  1] 0x00007ff87fa18ffd mcos::File::getInterface at B:\matlab\src\mcos_impl\cos_interface\matfileio.cpp:3084   [  2] 0x00007ff87fa140b2 mcos::File::convertCOSInterfaceFromMatrix at B:\matlab\src\mcos_impl\cos_interface\matfileio.cpp:3912   [  3] 0x00007ff87fa201c6 mcosConvertFromMATFileArray at B:\matlab\src\mcos_impl\cos_interface\matfileio.cpp:5600   If you try to delete the MAT file, this message appears:  The action can't be completed because the file is open in MATLAB .   If you simulate the model a second time, you see this error:  Unable to create file 'out.mat' specified in  'Configuration Parameters' > 'Data Import/Export' > 'Log Dataset data to file'. Possible causes for this include: the specified directory does not exist, the directory or the file are not writable, disk quota is exhausted, the file is used by another simulation, or the specified file is a softlink pointing to a non-existing directory.
Workaround: Do not use the Log Dataset data to file?model configuration parameter to save simulation results in a MAT file. After simulation is complete, export simulation results to a MAT file from the Simulation Data Inspector.Disable logging Dataset data to file.set_param('modelName','LoggingToFile','off')Simulate the model.Export the data from the Simulation Data Inspector to a MAT file.runObj = Simulink.sdi.getCurrentSimulationRun('modelName');Simulink.sdi.exportRun(runObj.id,'to','file','filename','out.mat')

Summary: Export-function model called by branched function-call initiator might not provide expected diagnostics
Description: The expected diagnostic messages might not be thrown for an export-function model when all these conditions apply: The export-function model is referenced by a top model. The top model contains a function-call initiator that connects to a Function-Call Split block. The Function-Call Split block connects to the function-call port of the Model block that references the export-function model.  For example, Simulink might not throw the runtime diagnostic that checks whether the parent model triggers the function-call model at the rate specified by the Fixed-step size  parameter on the Solver  pane of the C onfiguration Parameters dialog box. This diagnostic applies when the export-function model specifies a fixed-step solver and contains one or more blocks that use absolute or elapsed time.

Summary: Using group-specific variants in parameter estimation fails when computing confidence intervals of type bootstrap and profileLikelihood
Description: When you use group-specific variants in the estimation of parameters in SimBiology, the computation of confidence intervals of type bootstrap  and profileLikelihood  errors unexpectedly.

Summary: trainNetwork errors when computing validation on a stateful network that changes dimensions
Description: When training a network with the trainNetwork  function, if the network has stateful layers and changes the dimensions of the input data, then the function can throw an error when performing validation. When training on a GPU, the software throws the error: Error using trainNetwork  GPU arrays support only fundamental numeric or logical data types.  When training on a CPU, the software throws the error: Error using trainNetwork  Conversion to single from cell is not possible.

Summary: Timing specification constraints are not automatically generated when the target frequency is specified in the MATLAB to HDL workflow
Description: When you specify a non-zero target frequency in the MATLAB to HDL Workflow, HDL Coder does not automatically generate timing specification constraints when running synthesis.

Summary: invalid vector subscript error?during code generation with? a variant?root outport
Description: invalid vector subscript ?error?during code?generation when all the below conditions are true:  Signal object is attached to the root outport block i. e.   get_param(gcb,   'MustResolveToSignalObject')  returns true for the root outport block.   The storage class of this signal object is custom.  The root outport has a variant condition.  There is at least one root outport "before" this root outport that has either no variant condition or the same variant condition as this root outport.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Default mappings override individual mappings for model reference outputs if both storage classes are built-in or predefined
Description: When you individually map a model reference output to a built-in or predefined?storage class (i.e., one not created from the Embedded Coder Dictionary) and you set that referenced model's default output mapping to a different built-in or predefined?storage class, the default output mapping overrides the individual mapping when building the top model. This causes an incompatibility because the referenced model honors the individual mapping. The individual mapping should override the default mapping in both the top model and referenced model.
Workaround: Apply the storage class by attaching a Simulink.Signal to the model reference output.

Summary: MATLAB might crash at startup when one or more add-ons are installed
Description: When one or more add-ons are installed, MATLAB might crash at startup. The crash log contains library?libmwaddons_registry_core. dylib? on macOS,  addons_registry_core.so  on Linux or? addons_registry_core.dll? on Windows.

Summary: Unexpected error when selecting parameter or residual types to display for fit results in Simbiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, when you create an?Act vs. Pred ?or Residuals  plot for the Fit Data  program results, you can turn on or off different parameter types (individual vs. predicted) or residual types (CWRES or IWRES) in the legend. ?This change to the plot might fail and show a dialog with the error Unrecognized field name CategoryBinValues .

Summary: Generating bitstream for processor configuration derived from shipping bitstream can result in errors
Description: Prior to R2022a_update2 when you create a processor configuration from a shipping bitstream and generate a bitstream from the processor configuration it could result in an error? such as a load_system error. For example if your performed the following steps  hPC = dlhdl.ProcessorConfig('Bitstream','zcu102_int8); dlhdl.buildProcessor(hPC)  The following error message is generated Error using load_system'/ mathworks/ devel/ bat/ BR2022ad/ build/ matlab/ toolbox/ dnnfpga/ dnnfpga/ model/ customLayers/ dnnfpgaAdditionLayerModel. slx'is not a valid Simulink object name and no matching file found. Error in dnnfpga. customLayer. LayerManager/ checkCustomLayerModelError in dnnfpga. customLayer. LayerManager/ checkLayerListError in dnnfpga. config. CNN5ProcessorConfig/ validateProcessorConfigError in dnnfpga. build. buildProcessorError in dlhdl. buildProcessor (line 33)????dnnfpga. build. buildProcessor(varargin{:}); This error occurs because the shipping bitstreams contain a registered absolute path to the custom layer model files used in the bitstream and these files might not exist in the user's computer. ?This error does not occur when you do not create a processor configuration from a shipping bitstream. For example  hPC = dlhdl.ProcessorConfig; dlhdl.buildProcessor(hPC);  Starting in R2022a_update2, a relative path to custom layer models is saved and there are no errors when building bitstreams from shipping bitstream processor configurations.

Summary: Error when opening Wireless Tire Pressure Monitoring System with Fault Logging example
Description: Opening the example Wireless Tire Pressure Monitoring System with Fault Logging from the documentation page or using the?slexTirePressureMonitoringSystem ?command causes an error indicating that the file slexTirePressureMonitoringSystem. zip  does not exist.
Workaround: Download the attached zip file for this example. Unzip the file and open the project (.prj) file.

Summary: Fit result plots might label axes with group index number instead of group name or identifier
Description: When you fit a model to data that has group identifiers that are not character vectors, the fit results plots might use the group index number instead of the corresponding group identifier. For example, you might encounter this issue when you are using strings or categorical as group names or IDs.
Workaround: Convert the group column in the dataset to a character vector or cell array of character vectors before performing fitting to automatically display the group identifiers. ?Otherwise, you can manually change the axes labels on the plot.

Summary: On Windows, the?mwdocsearch.exe?process is sometimes left running after MATLAB exits
Description: On some Windows machines, the?mwdocsearch.exe ?process is sometimes left running after MATLAB exits. This process is used for searching installed documentation. When the?mwdocsearch.exe  process is left running, it can interfere with the installation of R2022a update releases.?
Workaround: Use the Windows Task Manager to manually terminate instances of the?mwdocsearch.exe process left running after MATLAB exits. Alternatively, use this command in the Windows command prompt to terminate all running mwdocsearch.exe processes:taskkill /IM mwdocsearch.exe /F

Summary: Nested FMU crashes at termination stage if top-level FMU and sub-level FMUs are compiled with different compilers
Description: When exporting a Simulink model with FMU import blocks as a standalone FMU, the nested FMU might crash at termination stage if the nested FMU does not use the same compiler as sublevel FMU.
Workaround: Use the same compiler to generate the top-level FMU and sub-level FMU.

Summary: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" exits with warning when?checksum information is missing
Description: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" ?( ID: ?mathworks.codegen.SubsysCodeReuse) ?exits with a warning when a subsystem does not provide checksum information.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b

Summary: HDL Coder generates an error message when using For Each Subsystem that has?Show partition index output port?parameter enabled.
Description: When your Simulink model contains For Each Subsystem that has?Show partition index output port (zero-based indexing)? block parameter turned 'on ', following error message is generated during HDL code generation:? Error using slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/pirSanityCheckInternal Error: Could not connect the blocks in the model. Look for unconnected blocks or other warnings or errors and correct them to continue.   HDL Coder does not supports For Each Subsystem with?Show partition index output port  enabled.?
Workaround: Consider, turning off the?Show partition index output port (zero-based indexing)?option in the For Each Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between Outport block and signal object specified inside referenced model
Description: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between an Outport block and a signal object specified inside a referenced model, and instead simulates the model. When this issue occurs, the simulation and code generation results might not be the same, depending on which initial value is applied last.?? This issue might occur if the model has these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem has an Outport block with an explicit value specified for the Initial output  parameter, that is, not an empty matrix ([] ). The Outport block is driven by an output port of a Model block. Inside the referenced model, the signal driving the root Outport block corresponding to the output port of the Model block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The InitialValue ?field? of the signal object is not an empty string and the initial value is not the same as the initial output value specified for the Outport block in the top model.
Workaround: When a Simulink.Signal object with an explicit initial value is applied to a signal, make sure that the destination, conditionally executed subsystem Outport block uses an empty matrix ([]) as the initial value. Also, apply this workaround when the signal in question crosses a model reference boundary.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect results for custom block replacement rule when referring to sqrt block in sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib.slx
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may show incorrect results when: The BlockReplacement ?parameter is set On  in Design Verifier Configuration window. Custom block replacement rule in?BlockReplacementRuleList? refers to sqrt   block in?sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib  .    For example, sldvdemo_custom_blkrep_rule_sqrt.m   refers to sqrt  block in sldvdemo_custom_blockreplib  model ?and if it?is one of the rules in?BlockReplacementRuleList,? then SLDV analysis may return incorrect results.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: polyspace-configure ignores diab compiler options file
Description: If your build system uses a diab  compiler that has an options file name that starts with the capital letter E or O, and you run polyspace-configure  to generate a Polyspace project or Polyspace analysis options file from that build system, the compiler options file is ignored and you see a debug message similar to this: DEBUG: Semantic option ignored: -@<OptionFile>   The issue occurs because Polyspace misinterprets the -@ErestOfFileName  and -@OrestOfFileName  options as redirect options -@E<restOfFileName> ?(redirect standard error) and -O<restOfFileName> ?(redirect standard output). Because the options file is ignored, if you run an analysis using the generated Polyspace project or analysis options file, the analysis might fail.

Summary: Polyspace-configure fails to detect the compiler on Windows, but detects a nonexistent executed program C:\Program.exe
Description: On Windows, no compiler is detected during configuration, but C:\Program.exe  is listed as an executed program: ERROR: Executed programs are:ERROR:         program           C:\Program.exe This can happen if the build tool is invoking the compiler through the Win32 API function CreateProcess()  without escaping the compiler path correctly. Polyspace-configure uses the first argument as the program name, thus failing to detect the compiler.
Workaround: Either move the compiler to another folder so its path does not contain any spaces, or remove all the spaces from its current folder:For example, from this:C:\my workspace\my binary\compiler.exe To either of these: C:\my_workspace\my_binary\compiler.exeC:\another_directory\without_spaces\compiler.exe
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Models that use Bus?data type with elements smaller than double?might throw assertion during code generation for S-Function target
Description: If a model uses the Bus ?data type with bus elements that are of a type smaller than  double ?(e.g.,  single ) and there are blocks in the model that initialize that  Bus ?type to zero, then the model might throw an assertion during code generation for an S-Function target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for models containing If Action subsystem connected to Memory block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains an If Action subsystem that connects to a Memory block. The model configuration parameter?Reuse global block outputs ?is selected.  If the If Action subsystem and the Memory block have initial conditions, the?Reuse global block outputs? optimization might cause their block buffers to be reused incorrectly leading to a loss of one initial condition in the generated code.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, clear the Reuse global block outputs?parameter.

Summary: JMAAB check for na_0001?displays incorrect warning for logical operations
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of??Check Stateflow operators? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.na_0001 )? displays incorrect warning when logical operators &&  and ||  are used in Stateflow charts with C  action language.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Error loading aggregated coverage data from nested subsystem in linked library
Description: When you try to load coverage data that is aggregated from the coverage results for two or more harnesses, an error occurs when both of these conditions are true: At least one harness is a block diagram harness where the component-under-test is a model At least one harness is for a subsystem nested inside a linked library instance of a parent subsystem.  The error message might be similar to: failed assertion in B:\matlab\toolbox\slcoverage\src\slvnv\cv_root.cpp at line 661:  parentId = hier_locate_root_parent(rootId,tree)

Summary: Exporting driving scenario to ASAM OpenDRIVE file can cause error
Description: When you export a driving scenario to an ASAM OpenDRIVE file by using the Driving Scenario Designer  app or the export  function of the drivingScenario  object, MATLAB returns this error if the scenario contains a junction that is not connected to any roads:  Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 0.

Summary: MATLAB may crash if lsqr is used with gpuArray inputs.
Description: MATLAB may crash if any input to?lsqr ?is a gpuArray  and ?GPU memory becomes exhausted during the computation. GPU memory is more likely to become exhausted when lsqr  is used within a parfor- loop. If the parfor- loop is run using a thread-based pool, the MATLAB client may crash. ?If it the parfor- loop is run using a process- or cluster-based pool, the MATLAB worker may crash.
Workaround: Use?gather?to gather gpuArray?inputs to host memory before passing them to?lsqr. For example:[A,b] = gather(A,b);x = lsqr(A,b)?

Summary: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError on comparison of two files
Description: Error java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError  is thrown when following types of files are compared:Folder/ zip Requirements (slmx,  slreqx) Live Code Project metadata Project archive mat file  Error snippet seen: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/mathworks/toolbox/rptgenslxmlcomp/plugins/slx/two/NodeFindToStringFunction at com.mathworks.toolbox.slrequirements.comparisons.slreqx.SLREQXComparisonType?

Summary: Observing message routing blocks produces no message data
Description: When you observe a message routing block (Message Merge, Entity Input Switch, or Entity Output Switch), no messages appear in the observer model. These blocks are not supported for observing and should produce a compilation error.

Summary: SimBiology Model Analyzer might show the subplots of confidence intervals in the wrong order
Description: The axes in some confidence interval plots might be laid out incorrectly in the grid, such that the rows and columns of axes appear scrambled.
Workaround: Use the following steps to work around this issue.In the Browser panel of the app, right-click the confidence interval results object. (SimBiology.fit.ParameterConfidenceInterval?or?SimBiology.fit.PredictionConfidenceInterval)?.Select Export Data to MATLAB workspace.?Specify the variable name, such as CIObj.Run?plot(CIObj) at the command line.

Summary: Unexpected effect of rotational velocity on pressure and force in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block
Description: Increasing rotational velocity decreases rather than increases pressure and interface force magnitude in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Workaround: This workaround is equivalent to the expected behavior of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. Replace the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block with these three blocks:Single-Acting Actuator (IL)Rotating Channel (IL)Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)Make the following connections. These connections match the attached picture:Rotating Channel (IL) port A to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A.Rotating Channel (IL) port B, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A, and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port X.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port C, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port R, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C.Rotating Channel (IL) port W and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port W to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port W.Make the following parameter updates:Parameters in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block should correspond to parameters on the Actuator, Hard Stop, and Initial Conditions tabs of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. The value of the?Piston cross-sectional area?parameter in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block equals the value of the annular area in-between the?Piston outer radius?and?Piston inner radius?parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.Parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL) block should match parameters on the Fluid Channel tab of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. These parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block map to corresponding parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL)?with a different name:Fluid entry radius maps to Port B radius of rotation.Fluid channel cross-sectional area maps to Orifice area.The Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)?block parameter Mechanical orientation?should have the opposite setting of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL)?block. All remaining parameters in the Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) block map directly from parameters with the same names in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Sliders from the Explorer section might not be applied properly while running a Group Simulation program in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: While running a Group Simulation  program, you might either encounter a hard error or not have the active sliders applied. This can lead to unexpected simulation results.

Summary: MATLAB fails to start with system version of Java on macOS
Description: MATLAB might not find Java installed on a macOS computer when calling this command in MATLAB:jenv system  or calling this command from a terminal window: matlab_jenv system ?

Summary: Create Test for Model Component wizard creates baseline test case instead of simulation test case
Description: In the Create Test for Model Component wizard, if you select Define the verification logic in the created harness  as the Verification Strategy , the Test Manager incorrectly creates a baseline test case instead of a simulation test case.
Workaround: Use sltest.testmanager.createTestForComponent?and set TestType = "simulation"?to create a simulation test case for your component.

Summary: Data defined in linked atomic subcharts might not access contents of a data dictionary
Description: If you use the contents of a data dictionary to specify properties of Stateflow data objects, Stateflow might throw an error if all of the following conditions are true: A library chart contains some local data whose size, initial value, or other attributes are specified using parameters defined in a data dictionary This library chart is used as a linked atomic subchart in a main model The main model references the data dictionary which defines the parameters  ?
Workaround: Use parameters defined in the MATLAB workspace to specify the properties of the local data. You might need to select Model Properties > External Data > Enable model access to base workspace or, in Model Explorer for the data dictionary, Enable dictionary access to base workspace.

Summary: Error while launching the SLAM Map Builder app using the slamMapBuilder function when the session file is specified as a string
Description: Launching the SLAM Map Builder  app using the slamMapBuilder  function throws an error if the session file is specified as a string scalar instead of a character vector.For example:?slamMapBuilder('sessionFile.mat')  instead of slamMapBuilder("sessionFile.mat") . Error message seen: slamMapBuilder("slam_map_builder_session.mat")  Error using nav.slamapp.internal.MapBuilderController/setAppTitle  Error setting property 'Title' of class 'matlab.ui.container.internal.AppContainer'. Value must be a scalar.  Error in nav.slamapp.internal.SLAMMapBuilder (line 132)  obj.MapBuilderController.setAppTitle();  Error in slamMapBuilder (line 34)  nav.slamapp.internal.SLAMMapBuilder(varargin{:});?
Products: Robotics System Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox, ROS Toolbox

Summary: Unable to compile generated code when a Simulink.Parameter object with a non-Auto storage class has the value set to a parameter expression that contains an array
Description: The generated code for a Simulink model cannot be compiled when both of these conditions are true:The model uses a Simulink.Parameter  object with a non-Auto  storage class. The value of the Simulink.Parameter  object is set to a parameter expression that contains an array expression.  In this case, the code generator generates illegal C code that uses square brackets instead of curly brackets for array initialization. For example: int X = [VAL1, VAL2]; ? instead of :? int X = {VAL1, VAL2};

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing global average pooling layer followed by?nnet.keras.layer.ZeroPadding2dLayer?might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network that contains the following two layers in succession might produce incorrect results: Global average pooling layer nnet.keras.layer.ZeroPadding2dLayer   This issue affects all these targets: MKL-DNN, ARM Neon, cuDNN, ARM Mali, and TensorRT. Therefore, this issue affects both MATLAB Coder and GPU Coder workflows.

Summary: Argument was not found or found more than 2 hits or Unexpected number of elements in GraphicalIndex?error from SIL/PIL simulations of AUTOSAR model
Description: ?A software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation of an AUTOSAR model fails if both of these conditions apply to the model under test: The configuration parameter?CodeInterfacePackaging  is set to?'Reusable function' . Function Caller blocks call functions defined in Simulink Function blocks that are outside the model.  The simulation produces one of these error messages: Argument was not found or found more than 2 hits  Unexpected number of elements in GraphicalIndex
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Atomic subcharts with parameter mapping in hierarchy of models with For Each subsystem might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: ?During simulation initialization, MATLAB might crash when all of the following conditions are met: The parent model contains at least two child models. One of the child models has an atomic subchart with parameter mapping. The other child model has either a Stateflow chart with mask parameters or an atomic subchart with parameter mapping inside a For Each subsystem. Both child models use at least one parameter that directly or indirectly resolves to the same global Simulink.Parameter  object with bus data type defined either in the base workspace or in a Simulink data dictionary.

Summary: Changes to combobox custom attribute registry do not merge
Description: When you make changes to the custom attribute registry for combobox  type custom attributes for requirement sets or link sets and merge the changes, the auto-merge does not merge the changes to the target file. You have to edit the target file to manually make those changes.

Summary: Output to terminal is not displayed when calling?disp or any other function displaying output from Java Engine
Description: When using Java Engine, any call to a function to create terminal output such as disp  does not produce any terminal output. For example, the following code should print Hello World  to the terminal:? MatlabEngine eng = MATLABEngine.startMATLAB();  eng.eval("disp('Hello World');");  Instead this code produces no terminal output.

Summary: JMAAB check for jc_0641 reports incorrect warning for Delay and Trigger blocks
Description: Model Advisor check Check for sample time setting ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0641)? does not report warning for?Function-Call Subsystem  and reports incorrect warning for Delay  block at root level.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-1-1 false positive on ranged-based?for-loop
Description: Polyspace Bug Finder incorrectly raises a violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-1-1 when using a ranged-based?for -loop that does not use any literal values to define the range. For example: class MyClass{};MyClass * tab[100];for (auto &e : tab) {     // False positive of A5-1-1}

Summary: MATLAB crashes with a crash log containing "errorrecovery.dll" and "setErrorRecoveryTimeoutValueInSecondsFeature"
Description: MATLAB might crash when a user enters a command or runs a script containing a function whose definition is not visible to MATLAB. The crash log for this issue may contain the following two strings near the top: mwboost::archive::codecvt_null<wchar_t>::do_out MathWorks::ErrorRecovery::setErrorRecoveryTimeoutValueInSecondsFeature

Summary: anynan and allfinite functions produce incorrect results for half type
Description: The output of functions anynan  and allfinite  results in incorrect answers for half datatype. For example, the following calls produce incorrect results: anynan(half(nan))  returns? false allfinite(half(inf))  returns true.
Workaround: Cast the inputs to single precision before calling anynan and allfinite functions.

Summary: Normal simulation mode is displayed instead of SIL or PIL in Test Manager results
Description: When you use the Test Manager to run a SIL or PIL simulation in a subsystem harness that contains a Reusable Library Subsystem or an Atomic Subsystem, the simulation mode in the test result is displayed as Normal, instead of SIL or PIL.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Error about missing asset files while building scene from imported HD Map data
Description: When you build scene for HD map data imported from HERE HD Live Map or TomTom HD Map, Road Runner's Scene Builder reports errors and warnings about missing asset files. An example of the error when building scene is: ERROR: Failed to fetch '<PROJECT>/Assets/Signs/Sign_BlankWhite.rrsign': Asset file is missing.

Summary: Satellite scenario viewer does not show all closed communications links when adding a vector of link analysis objects.
Description: When adding multiple link analysis objects using the 'link' function, only the last link is shown on the satellite scenario viewer, even if the other links are closed. The following code demonstrates this:? >> startTime = datetime(2022,5,24,11,40,0);  >>?stopTime = startTime + days(1);  >>?sampleTime = 60;  >>?sc = satelliteScenario(startTime,stopTime,sampleTime);?% Create a satellite scenario?  >>?v = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);?% Launch a satellite scenario viewer?  >>?sat = satellite(sc,10000000*ones(1,2),zeros(1,2),zeros(1,2),zeros(1,2),zeros(1,2),[0 10]);?% Add two satellites?  >>?gs = groundStation(sc);?% Add a ground station?  >>?gim = gimbal([gs gs]); % Add two gimbals to the ground station  >>?tx = transmitter(sat); % Add a transmitter to each satellite  >>?rx = receiver(gim); % Add a receiver to each gimbal  >>?pointAt(sat,gs); % Point the satellites towards the ground station  >>?pointAt(gim,sat); % Point first gimbal towards the first satellite, and the second gimbal towards the second satellite  >>?lnk = link(tx,rx); % Add a link between first transmitter and receiver, and second transmitter and receiver  ?Although both links in lnk  are closed, only the second link is shown on the viewer.?
Workaround: Call show on lnk as follows:>> show(lnk);?

Summary: A model with periodic states and code interface packaging set to 'C++ Class' generates code that is uncompilable
Description: A model configured using periodic states and code interface packaging set to 'C++ Class' generates code that is uncompilable after building the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?ssdMergeLayer?might produce incorrect answer if at least one of the input channel sizes not divisible by the layer property?NumChannels
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network might produce incorrect answer if: The network contains an?ssdMergeLayer ?object The size of at least one of the input channels to the SSD merge layer is not divisible by the layer property?NumChannels   For example, consider this network: ? ? ? ? ? input1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? input2  4(S) x 4(S) x 8(C) x 8(B)? ? ? ?4(S) x 4(S) x 6(C) x 8(B)  ? ? ? ? ? ? |? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ? ? |? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ? ? |________ ssdMergeLayer ________|  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (NumChannels = 4)? ? ?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |?  Channel size of the? input2 ?layer (6) is not divisible by the?NumChannels ?property (4) of the SSD merge layer. For this network, code generation might produce a wrong answer for the output of?ssdMergeLayer .?

Summary: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for a model that uses standards compliant casting
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code when all ?of the following are true:? The model uses any of the following storage classes or access functions on the I/O of a referenced Model block or S-Function that uses TLC for code generation: A?GetSet  storage class. A user-defined storage class in an Embedded Coder Dictionary whose data access is Function.  A user-defined TLC storage class that uses different legacy APIs to get and set the data.  The Casting modes ?model configuration parameter is set?to Standards Compliant .  Embedded Coder might insert a void  cast before an access function call, leading to uncompilable C/C++ code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a

Summary: UseDeterministicExploitation?cannot be passed as a name-value pair input when constructing agent options
Description: UseDeterministicExploitation , is a property of the agent options objects?rlPGAgentOptions ,?rlACAgentOptions ,?rlPPOAgentOptions ,?rlTRPOAgentOptions , and?rlSACAgentOptions. ?It is used to set the stochasticity of the agent's policy in simulation. However passing this property as a name-value pair when constructing the object causes an unexpected error. For example,?the following line generates and error:? rlPPOAgentOptions("UseDeterministicExploitation",true)
Workaround: There are two workarounds.?Workaround 1 (Recommended):Instead of using?UseDeterministicExploitation in the agent options object,?use the UseExplorationPolicy property directly in the agent object.??For example,?obsInfo = rlNumericSpec([3, 1]);actInfo = rlNumericSpec([2, 1]);?agent = rlPPOAgent(obsInfo, actInfo);??agent.UseExplorationPolicy = false;? % This is equivalent to agent.AgentOptions.UseDeterministicExploitation = true;agent.UseExplorationPolicy = true;? ?% This is equivalent to agent.AgentOptions.UseDeterministicExploitation = false;Workaround 2:??Create an agent options object, and then specify the property value (to avoid using a name-value pair during the object creation).??options = rlPPOAgentOptions;?options.UseDeterministicExploitation = true;

Summary: Internal assertion hit while generating HDL code for design using clock-rate pipelining and locally disabled delay balancing
Description: When clock-rate pipelining is enabled for the model and there are subsystems that have the HDL block property?BalanceDelays  set to off , this internal assertion might be hit:  Assertion Failed With Debug Message: dom_pir_core/ClockRatePartition.cpp:545:d.getValid() == DelayData::VALID
Workaround: The assertion can be avoided by disabling the?ReduceMatchingDelays parameter for the model. You can use hdlset_param to disable the parameter.hdlset_param(<model_name>, 'ReduceMatchingDelays', 'off')?Note that disabling this parameter can?increase the register count in the generated HDL code.

Summary: The increaseExecutionOrder and decreaseExecutionOrder functions only temporarily modify the execution order
Description: In System Composer, running the increaseExecutionOrder  or decreaseExecutionOrder  functions on a systemcomposer.arch.Function  object?will only temporarily modify the ExecutionOrder  property. After updating the diagram with a CTRL+D , the ExecutionOrder ?property resets to its default state.

Summary: Figure windows might not update while waiting at input function prompt
Description: If the input function is waiting for input from the user, then MATLAB might not draw or update figures while it is waiting. Panning or resizing a figure window generally works, but changing properties of graphics objects might not take effect until the call to the input function is complete.

Summary: Saved sessions containing CTLE cause exception in SerDes Designer app.
Description: If you load a saved session from a previous release containing a CTLE block, the SerDes Designer  app shows an exception.?This is because the CTLE block in the latest release has additional parameters that are not saved in the session MAT file of an older release.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation if it runs out of memory
Description: Running out of memory during code generation may cause MATLAB to crash unexpectedly without any messages.

Summary: Incorrect results with Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation?when?Enable timer optimizations is selected
Description: Results given in the?Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation ?may be wrong when:Simulink Design Verifier analysis is run with the timer optimizations enabled through the use of the?sldvtimer(1) .  The incorrect behavior is observed across Simulink Test Generation, Design Error Detection and Property Proving workflows. Results obtained when the setting?Run additional analysis to reduce instances of rational approximation ?is OFF  are not affected.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Error when using Simulink.SimulationInput to provide a new value for a variable in a data dictionary
Description: If Simulink.SimulationInput  object is used to provide a new value for a variable which is defined in a data dictionary of a reference model and the variable has a data type which is also defined in the same data dictionary, the following error is thrown:  Error using Simulink.Simulation.internal.DesktopSimHelperTo use variable 'variable_name', data dictionary dd_name.sldd must be a data source of data dictionary dd_name.sldd.  The error will be thrown if the top model does not use any data dictionary.??
Workaround: Use a data dictionary for the top model as well. The data dictionary can be empty.?Here?is the documentation for setting up a model to use data dictionary.

Summary: Code generation might take excessive time if MATLAB code uses a collision geometry with a matrix of size greater than or equal to UINT16_MAX (65535)
Description: Code generation might take excessive time or hang if both ?these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code uses Robotics Toolbox to add a collision geometry to a rigid body The collision mesh of the rigid body contains at least 65535 elements

Summary: Commands can be truncated or split when launching MATLAB with the -nodesktop option on Windows.
Description: Use of the legacy MATLAB Command Window on Microsoft Windows (i.e., launching MATLAB with the -nodesktop option) can lead to spurious carriage returns being inserted in the middle of a command line in release R2022a. For example: Enter a command in a single row:  After pressing Enter. the command is incorrectly split into 2 rows ?
Workaround: Do not use the -nodesktop option when launching MATLAB on Windows.

Summary: Code generation fails if two different blocks load the same deep learning network containing a custom layer
Description: An error occurs when attempting to generate code for a Simulink model that satisfies these conditions:? The Simulink model loads the same deep learning network into two different blocks?from the same MAT file. These blocks could be MATLAB Function blocks or belong to the Deep Neural Networks block library. The network contains a custom layer.?  In such situations, code generation produces this error:? layers/DLTCustomLayer/DLTCustomLayerComp.cpp:157In updateCGIRInfo: CallExpr is not propagate size call method
Workaround: Differentiate the networks that you load into separate blocks using one of these approaches:?To load the network into a MATLAB Function Block by using?coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork, pass an extra argument to coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork to give your network a unique name. For example:?net = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net.mat', 'net_unique_name')?To load the network into blocks in the Deep Neural Networks library, create multiple copies of the network MAT file. Refer each block to a separate copy of the MAT file.

Summary: MATLAB may crash if the Spatial Contact Force block is used in a simulation that is over constrained.
Description: If the?Spatial Contact Force block is used to model contact between a File Solid geometry and another geometry type, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The issue may arise if the simulation is over constrained or mechanically invalid.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: IP core register initial value is incorrect for fixed-point values with non-zero fraction length
Description: When mapping a Simulink input port to a register in the IP core workflow, you can optionally specify an initial value for the register. If the mapped Simulink port has a fixed-point data type with non-zero fraction length, then the register initial value specified is incorrect in the generated code.
Workaround: You can cast the Simulink port data type to be a fixed-point data type with zero fraction length at the DUT boundary (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). You can then convert it back into the desired fixed-point data type inside of the DUT (using a Data Type Conversion block in Stored Integer mode). When setting the register initial value, set the value using the stored integer representation of the desired initial value.For example, if the original Simulink port had data type fixdt(0, 16, 15)?and the desired initial value is 0.5, cast the port data type to uint16 at the DUT boundary and set the initial value as?storedInteger(fi(0.5, 0, 16, 15)).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Simulink cannot find files in open project during tool-coupling FMU export
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU from an open project, Simulink shows the following error message during the export process: No matching files named '<temporary file path>' were found.

Summary: Deployed application instances generate segvs on 128 core Centos 7.x system
Description: MATLAB increased the number of threads created by a deployed application.? Starting many instances of a deployed application might exhaust the max user processes configuration given the Centos defaults this to? 4096. Launching multiple concurrent instances of a deployed application on a 128 core Centos 7.x system causes many of them to segv.
Workaround: Increase the max user processes configuration. This limit can be increased by modifying /etc/security/limits.conf and?/etc/security/limits.d/20-nproc.conf.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow chart hard codes some size information of non-scalar symbolic dimension
Description: When generating code for a 2-D matrix that is defined by symbolic dimensions,  the resulting code inserts a numeric offset in the matrix assignment based on the current value of one dimension of the matrix.?This causes an issue if you specify a different dimension post code generation since the numeric offset will not match the matrix dimension.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Code generated for models that contain blocks that are configured to generate code in a shared location?might cause a missing file error for model.h
Description: Compiling generated code might cause a compilation error for models that contain blocks that are configured to generate code in a shared location. The compilation error resembles the following, where model  ? is the model name : model .h: No such file or directory
Workaround: Remove the line #include "model.h"?from the files in the shared location, then recompile the code.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a MAT file that stores a Simulink.ActorSimulation object
Description: When you load a MAT file that stores a Simulink.ActorSimulation  object, MATLAB displays this warning message:  ˇ°Cannot load an object of class 'ActorSimulation': No matching constructor signature found.ˇ±?  When you proceed to use the stored Simulink.ActorSimulation  object, for example by calling its getAction  function, MATLAB crashes.

Summary: In Simulink,?gpucoder.reduce might throw an error when used with row-major layout
Description: Use of gpucoder.reduce  in Simulink might result in an error if all  these conditions are true:gpucoder.reduce  is used inside a MATLAB Function? block. The function calling gpucoder.reduce  uses row-major array layout. Configuration parameter GPU acceleration  ?(for simulation) or Generate GPU code  ?(for code generation) is enabled.

Summary: Error when running an Earth-centered radar scenario with clutter generation and multiple surfaces
Description: An error is encountered when running an Earth-centered radar scenario with clutter generation and multiple land or sea surfaces when at least one surface has terrain (in the case of land surface) or a spectral model (in the case of sea surface), and at least one other surface does not  have terrain or a spectral model. The error message is?Unable to resolve the name 'fusion.internal.frame.lla2enu' .

Summary: The JMAAB check for?jc_0802?does not analyze enumerated data types correctly
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for implicit type casting in StateFlow (id: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0802)? does not analyze enumerated data types correctly in a Stateflow Chart with MATLAB as the action language.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Generate default AXI4 slave interface option is sometimes ignored during the IP core generation
Description: When generating an IP core for a generic platform, you can optionally map allthe DUT ports to External Ports  and disable the option Generatedefault AXI4 slave interface  to avoid the generation of AXI4 interfaces in theIP core. However, the option is ignored in these cases: In R2021b:  If?one of the ports has been assigned to an AXI4 Slave interfaceduring the working session, the generated IP core always has the default AXI4 slaveinterface, even if the Generate default AXI4 slave interface  option isdisabled.? In R2022a:  The Generate default AXI4 slave interface ?option in the HDL WorkflowAdvisor GUI has no effect.
Workaround: In R2021b:1. Close the Simulink model.2. Clear the MATLAB workspace3. Re-open the model and start a new HDL Workflow Advisor session.4. Map all ports to External Ports and disable the Generate default AXI4 slave interface option.In R2022a:Run the HDL Workflow with a script that includes this HDL parameter setting:hdlset_param(ˇ®model/DUTname, 'GenerateDefaultAXI4Slave', 'off');

Summary: Calibration levels for acousticFluctuation and acousticRoughness are incorrect
Description: The?acousticFluctuation  and acousticRoughness  functions output inaccurate level values because of incorrect calibration.
Workaround: To compensate for the inaccuracy, set the microphone calibration factor to twice the measured value:      [x,fs] = audioread("Engine-16-44p1-stereo-20sec.wav");? ?   caliblvl = 6.1;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?     ?% Computed by calibrateMicrophone? ?   acousticFluctuation(x,fs,2*caliblvl)?     % Double the calibration factorIf you are using the default value of sqrt(8) for the microphone calibration factor, change all function calls to use twice that value:      acousticFluctuation(x,fs,2*sqrt(8))? ?     % Double the default calibration factorThis bug and workaround apply only to?acousticFluctuation?and?acousticRoughness.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using gpucoder.reduce?with dimension specification might?produce incorrect answers for variable-sized inputs and row-major layout
Description: When using row-major layout with variable-sized inputs (bounded or unbounded), the code generated for gpucoder.reduce  containing a dimension ('dim' ) specification might initialize the output to a wrong value resulting in an incorrect final value.
Workaround: When using?gpucoder.reduce, always use column-major layout.

Summary: RoadRunner Scenario Writer block writes incorrect values to Actor Pose (Driving Scenario Compatible) topic
Description: When you set the Topic  of the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block to Actor Pose  (Driving Scenario Compatible ), the corresponding actor pose does not update correctly. The Roll , Pitch , Yaw  and Position  fields of input structure type BusActorPose  do not convert to the expected actor pose values.
Workaround: Manually convert the Roll, Pitch, Yaw and Position fields of input structure type BusActorPose to the Pose field of input structure type BusActorRuntime. Then, write the BusActorRuntime structure type with newly calculated Pose to the Actor Pose topic using the RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.

Summary: Simulation results might be wrong for subsystems that have a Reinitialize Function block and Stateflow charts with Simulink states
Description: Simulation might produce incorrect results for a subsystem when all the following conditions apply:? A Subsystem block has Show subsystem reinitialize ports  selected. The subsystem contains a Reinitialize Function block and Stateflow charts. The Stateflow charts have Simulink states. The Simulink states do not define another reinitialize function with the same event name as the Reinitialize Function block.

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0126?reports incorrect warning for events in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check definition of Stateflow events ?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.db_0126) ?reports below incorrect warning for events used in parallel states. ? Warning  The following Stateflow events are not defined at theirsmallest scope:?  Event? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Smallest Scope  model/Chart/E??? model/Chart/A/A2  model/Chart/F? ? model/Chart/A/A1?  This warning message is incorrect as the events are defined at the smallest possible scope.??
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 8, R2022b

Summary: Error on Linux when working with?HDF5 files on NFS4 network locations
Description: An error is thrown when using Linux platforms and working with HDF5 files in network locations that are mounted as NFS4. For example, the error can happen when using h5writeatt  or h5write  after h5create . >> outFile = 'myfile.h5';>> h5create(outFile, '/mysignal', [10 1])>> h5write(outFile, '/mysignal', ones(10, 1))Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);Error in h5write (line 108)    file_id    = H5F.open(Filename,flags,fapl); It can also happen when re-opening the file for writing using HDF5 Library Packages, as shown in this call to H5F.open . >> fid = H5F.open('myfile.h5','H5F_ACC_RDWR','H5P_DEFAULT');Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open the file because of HDF5 Library error. Reason:UnknownError in H5F.open (line 130)file_id = H5ML.hdf5lib2('H5Fopen', filename, flags, fapl, is_remote);
Workaround: This issue is specific to Linux platforms. To work around it, set the environment variable HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE before launching MATLAB. For example, to set the environment variable in a?C-shell terminal, and then launch MATLAB from the same terminal session, use these commands.% setenv HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING FALSE% matlabTo set the environment variable in a bash terminal, use the "export" command, e.g.:% export HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING='FALSE'% matlabSetting this environment variable to FALSE?disables the file locking behavior that was introduced in HDF5 1.10.x versions (more details can be found here:?https://docs.hdfgroup.org/hdf5/rfc/RFC_THG_2014-01-10.pdf). In terms of file locking and protection from file corruption, setting HDF5_USE_FILE_LOCKING to FALSE is equivalent to using a MATLAB release earlier than R2021b, when we upgraded to HDF5 1.10.7. But disabling file locking like this is not recommended by The HDF Group (the developers and maintainers of HDF5).

Summary: Compatibility check may fail with top model and referenced model linked to different data dictionaries
Description: If the Rebuild model representation  option is set to If change detected  and if the top model and reference models point to different data dictionaries, subsequent runs of Design verifier analysis could fail with the following error: Validating cached model representation from 30-March-2022 13:07:52. . . Errors occurred during compilationThe data dictionary '<ModelName>_replacement_DD. sldd' was not found. '<ModelName>_replacement' is incompatible for test generation with Simulink Design Verifier.
Workaround: Set Rebuild model representation option is set to Always

Summary: Code generation does not work for RGB to BGR, BGR to RGB, and BGR to intensity options in the Color Space Conversion block
Description: Simulation works as expected but code generation does not work for all these Conversion  options in the Color Space Conversion block:RGB to BGR  BGR to RGB  BGR to intensity

Summary: Renamed signal in Signal Editor not saved with correct name in MAT-file if signal dimension greater than 1
Description: In Signal Editor, if you rename a signal that has a dimension size greater than 1, the updated name is reflected in the Signal Editor interface, but is not saved with the new name in the MAT-file.

Summary: Label UI components in a fitted?grid layout manager?might not appear
Description: Components in a grid layout manager might clip or disappear when all of these conditions hold:A grid layout manager is created using the uigridlayout  function. A label created with the uilabel  function is placed in the grid layout manager.? The ColumnWidth? property of the grid layout manager contains 'fit'  in the entry corresponding to the column containing the label.
Workaround: You can alternatively use a numeric value for the grid ColumnWidth property.fig = uifigure;g = uigridlayout(fig);g.ColumnWidth = {120};

Summary: Results download from Polyspace Access using the?polyspace-access?command fails.
Description: If you use the?polyspace-access ?command from a Polyspace product version R2022a or earlier to download results from Polyspace Access version R2022a_update1 or later, the download fails and you see an error message similar to this:?   Error: no chunk size found while parsing data from server answer.
Workaround: To work around this issue, upgrade the Polyspace client product that you use to download results from Polyspace Access to version R2022a_update1 or later.

Summary: Unable to load saved model workspace parameter value during model load/open operation
Description: Unable to load saved parameter values from model workspace during model load/open operation for?Narrowband Transmit Receive Array  or Monopulse Feed  Blocks (from Phased Array System Toolbox ). The issue is specific to values coming from the model workspace and in this case the block is loaded with the default settings coming from the library?
Workaround: Right click on the model and choose Model PropertiesClick the Callbacks tab and modify PostLoadFcn and CloseFcn as given below:In PostLoadFcn, add the model work space variables into the base work space:? ? ? ? hws = get_param(gcs,'modelworkspace')? ? ? ? a = getVariable(hws,'a')? ? ? ? e = getVariable(hws,'e')? ? ? ? s = getVariable(hws,'s')In CloseFcn, clean up the base work space.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crash during "Check for Run-Time Issues" step in the MATLAB Coder app
Description: In the MATLAB Coder app, the Check for Run-Time Issues ?step might cause MATLAB to crash. The error message mentions "CycleEquivalence. cpp line 216:?In getClass: edge has no entry in CycleEquivalence::getClass ". A similar error might occur when simulating a model that contains MATLAB System blocks that have the Simulate using  ?parameter set to Code generation.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, clear?Collect MATLAB line execution counts?in the MATLAB Coder app during the Check for Run-Time Issues?step.See the attached image.To avoid this issue in Simulink, change?Simulate using?of the affected MATLAB System block to Interpreted execution. Alternatively, download and extract the attached .zip file. Run ">> SimulinkWorkaroundG2681332" in MATLAB command window.

Summary: Signal Multiresolution Analyzer crashes MATLAB on mouse movement while panning or zooming across multiple axes
Description: In Signal Multiresolution Analyzer, a zoom or pan action can be initiated on an axis by clicking and holding the mouse button. To perform the zoom or pan operation, the mouse must move to a new location. If you start a zoom or pan on one axis and release the mouse on another axis, the app stops working correctly. If this action is repeated, MATLAB might crash. ?The stack trace generated by MATLAB contains lines similar to:?  [? 0] 0x00007ff81392d684 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\webscopes_foundation.dll+00710276 webscope::WebScopeStatics::setUseWebTimeScope+00004276[? 1] 0x00007ff813930541 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\webscopes_foundation.dll+00722241 webscope::WebScopeStatics::setUseWebTimeScope+00016241[? 2] 0x00007ff8734b6c74 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\toolbox\shared\spcuilib\webscopesasyncio\bin\win64\webscopesasyncio\webscopesfilterplugin.dll+00027764 createFilter+00018404[? 3] 0x00007fffdbf01d92 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\asynciocore.dll+00073106 asynciocore::SynchronousOutputNotPossible::SynchronousOutputNotPossible+00005490[? 4] 0x00007fffdbf04968 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\asynciocore.dll+00084328 asynciocore::OutputStream::read+00000040[? 5] 0x00007fffdbeffd89 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2021b\bin\win64\asynciocore.dll+00064905 asynciocore::Converter::init+00037961

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code that uses gpucoder.reduce
Description: A MATLAB crash might occur when generating GPU code for gpucoder.reduce  when a function handle passed to gpucoder.reduce  uses functionality intended for the generation of GPU kernels. Examples of such functionality include: GPU kernel pragmas such as coder.gpu.kernel ?and coder.gpu.kernelfun  Functions such as gpucoder.reduce  and gpucoder.stencilKernel  that are implemented to be run on GPU

Summary: Experiment Manager app shows incorrect export options after you refresh a custom training experiment running as a batch job
Description: After you refresh the results of a ?custom training experiment running as a batch job, the Experiment Manager app shows the options Export  >?Trained Network?and?Export?>?Training Information, instead of the correct option?Export?>?Training Output.?Additionally,  the option?Export?>?Results Table?is incorrectly disabled. This problem persists until you close and reopen the experiment results page.

Summary: Unexpected results using absolute time temporal logic when using a large temporal logic threshold
Description: A Stateflow absolute time temporal logic condition such as after(x, sec)  might not evaluate to true  at the expected time after entering a state when the following conditions are true: The chart has a periodic discrete sample time. The chart logic makes the state remain active for greater than?2147418?units of time. The units of time are the smallest time units in any temporal logic expression used by that state. For instance, if the state has two outgoing transitions, one using?after(x, sec) ?and the other using?after(x, msec) , the units of time here are msec (milliseconds) .  Note that typically, the length of time you can stay in the state is bigger than?2147418?units of time. This depends on the sample time of the chart and the units used in the temporal logic expression.

Summary: Code generation for yolov2ObjectDetector?might fail for row-major layout
Description: Code generation for yolov2ObjectDetector  might fail for row-major array layout with the error: ??? Input passed to predict and activations method must be a fixed size variable for code generation.

Summary: Connecting a Message Triggered Subsystem block to a root Inport block might cause a MATLAB crash
Description: Using a Message Triggered Subsystem block might cause a MATLAB crash during model compilation if the following conditions are true: Trigger port of the Message Triggered Subsystem is connected to a root-level Inport block. The output port of the Trigger block inside the subsystem is unconnected or connected to virtual blocks for example, Outport block, From or Goto blocks, or Signal Specification block, that are not connected to nonvirtual blocks. By default, the Trigger block?is connected to the subsystem's Outport block and the crash occurs when the subsystem's output port is unconnected.

Summary: Exporting models with mixed datatypes in bus outputs as tool-coupling FMUs might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, the simulation results from the FMU might be incorrect if the following conditions are true: The model has a bus or nested bus output. The bus or nested bus has elements or sub-bus with mixed datatypes (boolean, double, int...).
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: An atomic subsystem with no inports or outports and containing a referenced model may crash during code generation
Description: When an atomic subsystem with no inports or outports contains a referenced model with a bus input and no outports, the model may crash during code generation.

Summary: Generating?multi-instance?code for a model with Message Merge block might cause a CGIR assertion error or MATLAB crash
Description: Building a model with Message Merge block might cause a CGIR assertion error or MATLAB crash for one of the following scenarios: You build the top model when the?model configuration parameter Code interface packaging ?is set to Reusable function.  You build a referenced model when the?model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model ?is set to Multiple.

Summary: Tests that specify "Stop simulation at last time point" and use external inputs for a test harness different than Test Case inputs might error during execution?
Description: When you run a test that has?Stop simulation at last time point ?selected in the Test Manager?Inputs ?section, and you use an external input signal? from a test harness with both of these conditions: The harness fails to compile. The harness is not the one you specified in the Test Manager?System Under Test ?section.  This error occurs: Invalid Simulink object name: <harness_name>.  The block diagram '<harness_name>' is not loaded.
Workaround: Deselect Stop Simulation at Last Time Point in the Test Manager Inputs section.Specify a Stop Time in the Simulation Settings and Release Overrides subsection?of the System Under Test section.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: An error during HDL code generation can occur for a model that has streaming on and contains an Assignment block
Description: When generating HDL code from a Simulink model with a subsystem that uses streaming and has an Assignment block with vector data input, this error during code generation can occur:  Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/createCGIR  Code generation failed with the error message : Undefined function 'Dimensions' for input arguments of type 'hdlcoder. tp_ufixpt'.

Summary: The generated MATLAB function is missing training code?for a model loaded from a saved session in Classification Learner or Regression Learner
Description: For a model loaded from a previously saved session in Classification Leaner or Regression Learner, the generated MATLAB function for training a machine learning model with new data is missing some code. The?problem occurs when you click Generate Function ?in the Export  section of the?Classification Learner ?or Regression Learner ?tab after selecting a model that was trained in a previous app session. The generated code does not include MATLAB function calls necessary for training the machine learning model (for example, fitctree ) and performing cross-validation. As a result, after running the generated code, the trainedModel  output structure does not contain a field with a trained model, and using the predictFcn  function of the output structure returns an invalid output.
Workaround: Duplicate the selected model by clicking?Duplicate?in the Models section of the?Classification Learner or Regression Learner?tab. For optimizable models, specify the hyperparameter values used by the original model in the Model Hyperparameters section of the model Summary tab. That is, in the duplicated model Summary tab, clear the Optimize check boxes and specify the hyperparameter values under Values.Train the duplicated model and then generate code.

Summary: When comparing variables, the Comparison Tool might fail or use the wrong comparison type
Description: The Comparison Tool might fail or use the wrong comparison type if both  conditions are true: You are comparing two variables. The names of the variables match names of files or folders on the MATLAB search path.

Summary: SQO report generation fails when report generator version is older than Polyspace Access version
Description: If you generate an SQO report from Polyspace Access results, and if the version of the Polyspace product that you use to generate the report is older than the Polyspace Access version, the report generation fails. You see an error message similar to this:? Polyspace Report GeneratorLoading resultsError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_AUTOSAR_LIB_NON_COMPLIANCEError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_INCORRECT_VALUE_FORWARDINGError: internal - unable to find check definition for: Defect_NOEXCEPT_FUNCTION_THROWS...?  The issue occurs when the Polyspace Access SQO definition that you use to generate the report contains defect or coding rule checkers that are not available? in the older Polyspace product version.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Log into PolyspaceAccess.Create a custom SQO definition where you select only defect andcoding rule checkers that are available in the older Polyspace product version.Apply the custom SQO definition to the project for which you want to create an SQO report.?Even if you are able generate an SQO report with the original SQO definition, use the custom SQO definition instead to avoid seeing defect and coding rule checkers that are not available in the version of the product used to generate the report.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Generating a coverage report for a harness model containing a truth table block might cause an error
Description: MATLAB errors if you perform all the following steps in order: You simulate a harness model that contains a truth table block with coverage enabled You apply a coverage filter that filters out the truth table block? You close the harness model You generate a coverage report  At the final step, MATLAB shows an error that looks like: Error using cvi.TopModelCov.setUpFiltering (line 174) IDENTIFIER: 'ObjectParamContainsInvalidHandle' input must be a valid MATLAB message identifier. For more information, search for "message identifier" in the MATLAB Help.    Alternatively, if you run your tests using Test Manager in Simulink Test: You open the model under test.
