You run a test in Test Manager which simulates the harness that contains a truth table block and has a coverage filter applied on the truth table block. You generate a coverage report.  MATLAB shows the same error. In this case, if the harness model is not open when you initiate the test, Test Manager closes the harness model automatically when the test is complete.
Workaround: Do not close the harness model until after you generate the coverage report. If you are using Test Manager in Simulink Test, open the harness model before running tests so that Simulink Test does not close the harness upon test completion.

Summary: Custom Rule 20.1 is incorrectly raised when the number of characters in a line is equal to the limit
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a violation of Custom Rule 20.1 when a line length is equal to the specified line length limit. The violation should occur only when the line length is greater than the specified limit.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Enable-based constraints file do not get packaged in IP core folder for Vivado version older than 2017.4
Description: When you select enable-based constraints for a model, the generated constraints file (.xdc or .sdc) is packaged in IP core/constraints ?folder. The constraints file fails to get packaged in IP core ?folder if you are using Vivado 2017.4 or older.

Summary: Simscape compilation might fail with the error message "Invalid parameter type set" the first time a model is compiled or simulated
Description: When a Simscape block has a mask initialization function that sets the value of a parameter on the block, simulating the model can result in a confusing error with the message:  Invalid parameter type set at <parameter name>. Expected type ({logical,real,integer,index},~). Got type string.  This happens when a parameter that controls the visibility of other mask parameters is not properly evaluated before compilation.
Workaround: There are two ways that you can work around this issue:1) Call set_param twice in the mask initialization function. For example:>> set_param('circular', 'false');>> set_param('circular', 'false');?2) Call SIM twice and ignore the first error, for example:>> try;? sim(mdl); end;>> sim(mdl)?

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6 with static local variable
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a false positive violation of rule MISRA-C:2012 18.6 when the address of a static local variable is returned by a function.? For example: int *foo1(int *obj) {    static int *p;     p = obj;   // False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6    return p; } int main(void) {  static int *p1;   int *p2;    p2 = foo1(&p1); }

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor?Set Target Interface task error messages not thrown when using External Port interface
Description: During the Set Target Interface  task in the HDL Workflow Advisor, you can map ports in your design to IP core interfaces. These mappings are validated when you run the Set Target Interface  task. However, if any of your ports are mapped to the External Port  interface, validation errors for other interface mappings may not get properly thrown. If there are underlying validation errors on the other interface mappings, failures can occur during code generation, which should have been caught during the Set Target Interface  task. If there are no underlying validation errors, then this will have no effect.
Workaround: If you clear all External Port interface mappings, you can see any underlying validation errors on the other interface mappings. After resolving these issues, you can remap the desired ports to the External Port?interface.

Summary: License checkout can fail for Audio Toolbox when using a borrowed license
Description: When using?audioDeviceReader ?with a borrowed license, you might get this error: Unable to check out license for Audio Toolbox.
Workaround: Explicitly check out the borrowed toolbox license before using?audioDeviceReader. Use capital initial letters for the product name:license('checkout','Audio_System_Toolbox') % With the capitalization of ASTAn alternative workaround is to call an Audio Toolbox function before using?audioDeviceReader. For example, callmel2hz(2595)before?audioDeviceReader?to check out the borrowed license.

Summary: Continuous block inside model reference that uses local solver might generate wrong answer during normal mode simulation
Description: These blocks might produce an incorrect answer when they exist inside a model reference that uses a local solver and the top solver takes time steps that are smaller than the fixed step size for the local solver:? Rate Limiter block Derivative block Variable Transport Delay block Transport Delay block First Order Hold block with input signal that uses continuous sample time
Workaround: Do not use a local solver for model references that contain any of the affected blocks. You can disable the local solver for a model reference programmatically using the set_param function:??set_param(mdl,"UseModelRefSolver","off")For mdl, specify the model reference.?

Summary: polarhistogram can produce a chart with overlapping bins
Description: For some data where the range is close to 2¦Đ, polarhistogram  can produce a chart where the bin edges have a range larger than 2¦Đ. This results in bins that are overlapping in the chart. For example: data = [0.1 1.1 5.4 3.4 2.3 4.5 3.2 3.4 5.6 2.3 2.1 3.5 0.6 6.1] + 2*pi/3;h = polarhistogram(data);
Workaround: To work around this issue, set the BinLimits property of the polarhistogram.data = [0.1 1.1 5.4 3.4 2.3 4.5 3.2 3.4 5.6 2.3 2.1 3.5 0.6 6.1] + 2*pi/3;h = polarhistogram(data,'BinLimits',[0 2*pi]);

Summary: The evaluateSemanticSegmentation function returns a NaN value for boundary F1-measure (BF) scores.
Description: The evaluateSemanticSegmentation   function returns a NaN value for BF scores when the network predictions do not contain values for every class.

Summary: Reduced Order Flexible Solid block with ten or more interface frames gives erroneous?results
Description: When a Reduced Order Flexible Solid block has ten or more interface frames, the internal mapping between interface frames and connection frame ports on the block is incorrect, giving erroneous simulation results.

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with multiple callsites of the same function or system object analyzed by Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might cause MATLAB to crash in rare instances when running Simulink Design Verifier with  Test Generation or Design Error Detection being run with MCDC objective enabled.  Multiple calls to the same function or multiple instances of the same system object in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for inpolygon?might return answer different from MATLAB or cause MATLAB crash
Description: The inpolygon  function requires the polygon input to be specified as loops of vertices in order. The polygon input can be either closed (explicit) or open (implicit) loops of boundary points. If the input contains implicit one-point polygon loops, the result produced by generated code does not match MATLAB. For example:  tf = inpolygon(X,Y,[0 0 1 1 NaN 0.5 NaN],[0 1 0 1 NaN 0.5 NaN])   If the input polygon is a degenerate point and this point is implicitly passed as input, running the generated MEX causes a MATLAB crash. For example:?  tf = inpolygon(X,Y,0.5,0.5))

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Single-rate sharing/streaming can cause a silent wrong answer when the clock-rate region also has extra clock-rate latency
Description: A Modelsim simulation failure or a numerical mismatch between the original model and the generated model might occur if these conditions are met: ? Sharing Factor or Streaming Factor is set to be greater than 1 Clock-Rate Pipelining is being used Amount of clock-rate latency in the clock-rate region containing resource serialization is greater than the oversampling factor

Summary: Signal label mismatch during test harness compliation
Description: If you have a test harness for a subsystem whose output signal is explicitly named, and signal label propagation is enabled, this error might occur during compilation:Signal label mismatch: <harness_name>/Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name>, should match <harness_name>/Output Conversion Subsystem output port 1 label <signal_name><propagated_signal_name>.
Workaround: 1) Manually set Show propagated signals?to ON?for the?signals that are outputs of the signal conversion subsystem.2)? Set Signal label mismatch?to none in the?Diagnostics?> Connectivity?category of Model Configuration Parameters.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you try to load half-precision data using the From File block
Description: MATLAB crashes when you try to use the From File block to load a timeseries  object that contains half-precision data from a Version 7.3 MAT file. The From File block does not support loading half-precision data. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: Stack Trace (from fault):  [? 0] 0x00007ff982176f1f C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00028447  [? 1] 0x00007ff982196cf9 C: \Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00158969 H5LoggingSnapshot::getNSamples+00015497  [? 2] 0x00007ff98219b429 C: \Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00177193 H5LoggingSnapshot::getNSamples+00033721  [? 3] 0x00007ff9821a340f C: \Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00209935 H5LoggingSnapshot::getNSamples+00066463  [? 4] 0x00007ff9821a4de1 C: \Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00216545 H5LoggingSnapshot::getNSamples+00073073  [? 5] 0x00007ff9821a4420 C: \Program Files\MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\libmwsl_fileio.dll+00214048 H5LoggingSnapshot::getNSamples+00070576

Summary: Reading map data from HERE HD Live Map web service can cause error
Description: MATLAB can issue an error when you read map data from the HERE HD Live Map web service using these options: The Driving Scenario Designer app The roadNetwork  object function of the drivingScenario  object The read  object function of the hereHDLMReader  object  An example of the error when constructing hereHDLMReader object is:? The '' protocol specified in URL, 'catalogs?verbose=1&organizationType=here&layerType=versioned&q=hdlm-protobuf', is not supported. Specify the URL with the protocol 'http://' or 'https://'.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Sharing can result in numeric mismatch when there are hierarchies inside the subsystems to be shared
Description: HDL code generation might result in numeric mismatch between the original model and the generated model if the following conditions are met: Resource sharing is turned on. There are hierarchies inside the subsystems to be shared.  Note that this bug only affects the generated model and not the generated HDL code.
Workaround: A workaround for this issue is to flatten the hierarchy for all subsystems inside the subsystems to be shared.For example, if you are sharing two subsystems shared_subsystem1?and shared_subsystem2, and both of them contain a subsystem culprit_subsystem. Flatten the hierarchy for culprit_subsystem using these commands:>> hdlset_param('model/DUT/shared_subsystem1/culprit_subsystem', 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on');>> hdlset_param('model/DUT/shared_subsystem2/culprit_subsystem', 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on');Perform HDL code generation after running these lines and there is no longer a numeric mismatch in your validation model.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-1-1 false positive on negative constant
Description: Polyspace Bug Finder incorrectly raises a violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-1-1 when using a negative constant in a type initialization. For example: static constexpr double C{-90.0};   // False positive of A5-1-1

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0627?displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator blocks
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of??Check usage of Discrete-Time Integrator block? (id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0627 )? displays abnormal exit warning for Discrete-Time Integrator  blocks when both Upper and Lower saturation limits  are defined by parameter objects whose data types are not auto .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: JMAAB check for?jm_0012?incorrectly flags syntax for events that are not local
Description: The?sub-id: b of the? Model Advisor check?Check for usage of events and broadcasting events in Stateflow charts ?(id: mathworks.jmaab.jm_0012 ) incorrectly flags syntax for events that are not local.

Summary: Xilinx Vivado IP core generation and project creation fails when there are unsupported characters in the project path
Description: When running IP core generation or project creation using Xilinx Vivado, certain characters are unsupported in the project's path. Having these unsupported characters causes the IP core generation or project creation to fail with an error such as: ERROR: [ProjectBase 2-104] Project name 'C:\work\Project (1)' is illegal.
Workaround: Use a directory that does not contain any unsupported characters in its path. See Appendix B of the Vivado Users Guide for supported characters and Vivado project and file naming conventions:https://www.xilinx.com/support/documentation/sw_manuals/xilinx2019_1/ug895-vivado-system-level-design-entry.pdf?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Concatenating empty variable-sized signals might produce incorrect results
Description: Incorrect code generation and rapid accelerator simulation might occur when Vector Concatenate blocks accept empty variable-sized signals as inputs.See the attached model for an example. The model contains a Vector Concatenate block which accepts a variable-sized signal from an Enabled Subsystem as an input. The block concatenates the variable-sized signal with another signal. When the Enabled subsystem is disabled, it passes an empty output, and as a result the Concatenate block just passes through the other signal. The incorrect generated C code from the example model: ? repro_DW.concatenateBlk_DIMS1 = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] + 1;  ? loop_ub = repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[0] * repro_DW.Enabled_SS._DIMS1[1] - 1;  ? for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) { // -------------------> When the output of Enabled Subsystem is empty, this loop is never executed  ? ? tmp_data = repro_B.Enabled_SS.u; // -----------------> This is trying to copy output of Enabled Subsystem to tmp_data  ? }  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[0] = tmp_data; // --------------> tmp_data is still copied to output of Concatenate block  ? repro_B.concatenateBlk[1] = repro_P.Constant3_Value;  The generated code treats the empty signal as non-empty and incorrectly copies the signal to the output of the Concatenate block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: In Wide Bus Sheets in Parallel Link Designer, SPICEsimulations fail when Standard Load Sims checkbox is selected in SimulationOptions.
Description: In Wide Bus Sheets in Parallel Link Designer, and Standard Load Sims checkbox is selected in Simulation Options. SPICE simulations error with??the following error: Error,   Too many actual parameters in subcircuit "wb1_tsc" (xwb1_tsc_l)
Workaround: Uncheck Standard Load Sims checkbox is in Simulation Options for WideBus sheets

Summary: Changes to workspace variables are not applied to constellation diagram block or plot
Description: Changes to workspace variables that are used to compute Constellation Diagram configuration are not applied to the Constellation Diagram plot output when you rerun the simulation.
Workaround: Update the Constellation Diagram configuration settings directly in the dialogs to ensure the constellation diagram plot output will reflect changes when you rerun the simulation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Merge block and a Unit Delay block or signals specified to have a Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets either of the following scenarios: Scenario 1: The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystems are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to a Unit Delay block and then fed back to the input(s) of any of the conditionally executed subsystems. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.  Scenario 2: The model contains a Merge block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystem are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class. The input of any of these subsystems is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class with the same variable name as the output of the Merge block. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.?
Workaround: Connect a Signal Conversion block to the output of the Merge block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB may crash if an invalid CAD File is input to a File Solid block and the Convex Hull?output is selected.
Description: If the?File?Name ?input refers to an invalid or unsupported CAD file, and the Convex Hull  output is used by a Spatial Contact Force block, MATLAB may crash when running the simulation. The File Solid block cannot read CAD drawing or assembly files, and supports the following versions of CAD formats:? ACIS - Up to 2019 Autodesk Inventor - Up to 2020 CATIA V4 - Up to 4.2.5 CATIA V5 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) CATIA V6 - Up to V5-6 R2019 (R29) Creo-Pro/E - Pro/Engineer 19.0 to Creo 6.0 I-deas - Up to 13.x (Nx5), Nx I-deas 6 IGES - 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 NX - V11.0 to NX 12.0, and 1899 Parasolid - Up to v32.0 Revit - 2015 to 2020 Rhino3D - 4, 5, 6 Solid Edge - V19 - 20, ST - ST10, 2020 SolidWorks - From 97 to 2020 STEP - AP 203 E1/E2, AP 214, AP 242?
Workaround: Validate the CAD file in the File Solid block visualization pane prior to running the contact simulation.

Summary: Unexpected error may occur during HDL Code Generation for a large model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation may error out for a model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block with frame-based output. This error happens for bigger models like the Wireless HDL Toolbox reference application models. Before R2022a, this block was named NCO HDL Optimized, and was included in the DSP System Toolbox HDL Support library. Starting in R2022a, the block is named NCO and is in the DSP HDL Toolbox library. The error message is:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/addZeroProtection  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.  Contact support@mathworks.com  with reproduction? steps. Assertion Failed With Debug Message:  pir_transforms/PropagateInitVal.cpp:205:getInitVal(s) == InitVal::NOT_VISITED?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Highlighting analysis results may take long time or throw an error for models containing Simulink Function blocks connected in a loop
Description: If a model contains Simulink Function  blocks connected in a loop and if design error detection analysis is performed on one such block, then highlighting analysis results may have one of the following behavior: If highlighting is performed?from the Results Summary  window, then it may take a long time and the text InspectorUtils.extendRangeInfo  may be displayed in the MATLAB Command Window If highlighting is performed using sldvhighlight  API, then the following error may be thrown:Out of memory. The likely cause is an infinite recursion within the program.

Summary: Excessive array expression hoisting when generating PLC code
Description: Users may encounter statements like the following when generating PLC code for certain models: temp1 := testCycleNum;cycle_U := tb_U[temp1];temp2 := testCycleNum;cycle_Y := tb_Y[temp2];  Here, the temporary variables are unnecessary and yet they are created just so that every array index expression is hoisted.?

Summary: ?Import operation in?Mixed-Signal Analyzer?app is highly time consuming.
Description: Importing Cadence??simulation results in the?Mixed-Signal Analyzer ?app takes equal amount of time as generating those data in Cadence??ADE (Analog Design Environment). Currently, whether you import the data in the app by generating a mat file and then load it in the app or directly import the data through Cadence??database, both methods take significant amount of time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might fail to produce an error if the output of an extrinsic function?is assigned to a variable that holds an empty array
Description: Suppose that, in your MATLAB code, you assign the output of an extrinsic function call to a variable that was previously assigned an empty array. In such situations, code generation might ignore the return value of the extrinsic call and continue as if the extrinsic call returned an empty array.? For example, consder these two MATLAB functions:? % main.m:function out = maincoder.extrinsic('extout');out = '';out = extout();end% extout.m:function out = extoutout = '123';end Generating MEX code for main  and running the resulting MEX file does not produce an error about size mismatch for the variable?out . Instead, the MEX execution returns a 0x0  array.? The correct behavior of the code generator would be to check if the extrinsic call returns an empty array. If it does, the value of the variable would not change. If not, the code generator would produce an error. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: AUTOSAR Component Quick Start always changes the model system target file to either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc.
Description: When configuring a model using the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start, the system target file automatically changes to the default system target file based on the selected platform once the wizard has finished. The system target file the model was using is replaced with either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc, even if the model target was already AUTOSAR compliant.
Workaround: Manually change the system target file after the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start has finished.

Summary: Functions estimatePerformance and hw.compile error when the network has zero padding followed by group convolution pattern
Description: When the network has zero padding layer (nnet.keras.layer.ZeroPadding2dLayer) followed by a group convolution layer pattern,?estimatePerformance  and hw.compile  error out with the following message: Unconnected input. Each layer input must be connected to the output of another layer.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-2-2 when using C++ casts {}
Description: Bug Finder incorrectly shows a violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A5-2-2 when you cast in C++ by using {} , such as?int{0} .
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for?a model containing a Bus Creator block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Bus Creator block inside a Conditional or an Enabled subsystem. An input to the Bus Creator block has Inf  sample time.

Summary: Incorrect notice claiming zscore normalization is implemented in hardware
Description: This problem occurs when the addition module is on, the multiplication module is off, and the hardware normalization is set to 'auto'.? The compiler was correctly turning off hardware normalization for z-score because of the unsupported multiplication, but it was printing an incorrect notice claiming that the layer was split into an image input layer, an addition layer, and a multiplication layer for hardware normalization.

Summary: C++ and AUTOSAR code generation may not reflect Shared utilities identifier format setting
Description: The model configuration parameter Shared utilities identifier format , also known asCustomSymbolStrUtil  when configured programmatically, may not be used properly by Embedded Coder to generate code for a model?if the following conditions are true: The model uses autosar.tlc  or system target files derived from it or uses ert.tlc  with the Language  parameter set to C++ . Code was previously?generated for the model and artifacts from that codegeneration still exist. The?CustomSymbolStrUtil ?setting was changed (by using set_param ) since the code was previously generated.
Workaround: Togenerate code correctly for an AUTOSAR model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated: The     code generation directory (for example,     <modelName>_autosar_rtw?) The     relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,     slprj/autosar/<modelName>) The     Simulink cache file for the model (for example, <modelName>.slxc)After you haveremoved the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.?To generate code correctly for an ERT C++ model, delete the following artifacts that may have been produced when code was previously generated:The code generation directory (for example,?<modelName>_ert_rtw?)The relevant subdirectory of the slprj directory (for example,?slprj/ert/<modelName>)The Simulink cache file for the model (for example,?<modelName>.slxc)?After you have removed the previous code generation artifacts, generate the code again.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The initial condition of an output port block may be ignored if the output port block is inside an inactive variant subsystem block.
Description: An output port block inside an inactive variant subsystem may be removed according to the configuration of the variant subsystem block. This may lead to the loss of initial condition and cause answer mismatch while simulating in normal or rapid accelerator modes as the behavior when the output port block is removed in these two modes could be different according to the setting.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: An error during HDL code generation might occur for models with code reuse and clock-rate pipelining on
Description: When generating HDL code from a Simulink model that has code reuse on and uses clock-rate pipelining, an error during code generation could occur that looks like this:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/reduceMatchingDelays  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation? Assertion Failed With Debug Message:?pir_transforms/MatchingDelayReducer.cpp:341:n->getNumInstances() == 1??
Workaround: Use the?hdlset_param?function to disable ReduceMatchingDelays.

Summary: SFStudio.internal.variantTransitionRF?error when selecting a transition in Stateflow
Description: Quickly changing the selection to and from a Stateflow transition might cause the MATLAB command prompt to show a spurious error message that contains the following: MATLAB stack trace:  > In SFStudio.internal.variantTransitionRF  Warning: An error has occurred during refresh of Action named variantTransitionAction.?  Warning: Generator should return a DAStudio.ToolSchema.?  Warning: object[id]->isa == isa

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiplefunction-call signals driving through variant blocks may cause incorrect codegeneration
Description: Multiple function-call signals driving through variant blocks may cause incorrect code generation, when all the below conditions are met: Variant Source  or Variant Subsystems  which merge multiple function-call signals conditionally. Variant blocks  have code compile  activation and model is configured for ERT based code configuration. Function-call subsystems which are set to be Reusable  or Non reusable  and are driven by Variant blocks.

Summary: MATLAB on Windows 11 hangs while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device using?ble object
Description: MATLAB installed on Windows 11 displays a busy icon and remains unresponsive while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device by using the?ble ?object.

Summary: Simulink Edit time - 'From' Block has a 'warning' highlight when referring to 'Goto' Block inside a variant subsystem library link
Description: Given a Simulink model with Edit-time violations enabled, with the following conditions, ? 1) Top-level From and Goto  blocks in global scope ? 2) A variantsubsystem, where each subsystem contains a library link ? 3) Each library linkcontains nested From  and Goto  blocks which refer to the corresponding top-levelblocks in global scope? The top-level ˇ°Fromˇ± block will be highlighted in orangewith the warning message "No Matching Goto block". Running thesimulation will not produce warning or error messages in the Diagnostic Viewer.

Summary: Segmentation fault when Static Code Metrics are computed
Description: A crash might occur when the Embedded Coder or MATLAB Coder requests the Static Code Metrics. The stack trace points to a line in code_info.cpp .

Summary: Simulink Real-Time simulation may produce an error if Measure function execution times is selected
Description: Consider a model that contains an S-function with methods that use an empty code block. If you select the model configuration parameter?Measure function execution times ?and run a Simulink Real-Time simulation, the simulation may produce this error: Array indices must be positive integers or logic values.

Summary: Switching the sort style of blocks in the standalone?Library Browser?when there is no library selected causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If no library is selected in the standalone?Library Browser  tree, changing the sort style of the library blocks from library model order to alphabetical order or vice versa causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when input to log or log10 is negative
Description: When a Simulink model contains computation of log  or log10   and the input to this computation is negative, The result of this computation is likely NaN  in Normal mode, and The result of this computation might be -inf  in Accelerator mode, Rapid Accelerator mode, and generated code.  This computation of log  or log10 might come directly from a Math Function block, or indirectly from other logic that computes   log  or log10.     This issue affects Simulink, Simulink Coder, and Embedded Coder.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Selecting or mousing over a Simulink block might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you click or mouse over a Simulink block. The exact reproduction steps are not known, but might include clicking a block in a Simulink editor after: Opening or closing other Simulink editors Switching between open Simulink editors The generated stacktrace contains lines similar to: [  0] 0x00007ffb481ac2d0      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\glue2.dll+01426128 GLUE2::Editor::getGraphics+00000000[  1] 0x00007ffb3c69a58b      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_editor.dll+10331531 Simulink::CreateAnnotationCommand::openAnnotationEditor+00294235[  2] 0x00007ffb3c69a900      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_editor.dll+10332416 Simulink::CreateAnnotationCommand::openAnnotationEditor+00295120[  3] 0x00007ffb486de57f      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\glue2.dll+06874495 GLUE2::SearchCompletionProvider::setSearchProvider+00027359[  4] 0x00007ffb486dd203      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\glue2.dll+06869507 GLUE2::SearchCompletionProvider::setSearchProvider+00022371[  5] 0x00007ffb486a00a4      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\glue2.dll+06619300 GLUE2::BaseSingleSelectionActionTool::updateActions+00000164[  6] 0x00007ffb4869e234      <matlabroot>\bin\win64\glue2.dll+06611508 GLUE2::BaseSingleSelectionActionTool::onElementHover+00000836

Summary: An extra empty signal entry is created in the Dataset output when a model's root Outport has a forward slash in the block name
Description: An extra empty signal entry will be created in the Dataset  output of a model when the following conditions are all true:? The Simulink model has a root Output port with a "/" in the name e.g. my/out1 The model is configured such that the SaveFormat = "Dataset"  The mode is simulated using rapid accelerator mode?

Summary: Math Function block with reciprocal functiongenerates an incorrect HDL code for the?fixed-point inputs that have fractionallength greater than word length
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Math Function?block with reciprocal? function  Architecture is s et to? Math  in the HDL block property for Math Function Reciprocal block  The inputs to the block are fixed-point type with fractional length greater than word length?, such as? sfix8_E13 numerictype(0, 8, 13)  Output data type  parameter is set as other than 'Inherit: Inherit via internal rule'   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect diagnostic error message during compatibility check
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly errors out during compatibility check when the model has a parameter that is used in Discrete State Space  blocks and the parameter used in the denominator is initialized to zero.  The error message is:?"The parameter used by 'mdlName' has a non finite value. Simulink Design Verifier does not support non finite values."

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor Task 4.2 (Generate Software Interface) fails for library and subsystem reference blocks
Description: When using the IP core generation workflow in the HDL Workflow Advisor, Task 4.2 (Generate Software Interface) fails when: The DUT is a library block or a subsystem reference block, and Generate Simulink software interface model   is enabled
Workaround: Wrap the existing library or subsystem reference block inside of an additional subsystem, then use the newly created top-level subsystem as the DUT. Alternatively, use a model reference block for the DUT instead of a library or subsystem reference block.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when calling Simulink.findVars?function in STF_make_rtw_hook file?during code generation
Description: Generating code using a customized build process that executes the Simulink.findVars  function in a?STF_make_rtw_hook ?file causes MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result might occur when using fixed point data type in S-Function Builder
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect simulation results when you use fixed point signals in your S-Function Builder block, and the data type override is set to a value other than off .
Workaround: First, manually edit the generated source file for the S-function.Open the generated source fileIn the mdlInitializeSizes method, update the data type override flag to 0. The override flag is the last field in function ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint or ssRegisterDataTypeFxpSlopeBias. For example:DTypeId DataTypeId_0 = ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint(?S,?OUT_0_ISSIGNED,?OUT_0_WORDLENGTH,?OUT_0_FRACTIONLENGTH,?0 );Then, recompile the source file before running the simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated C/C++ code for constant MATLAB character vectors that includes both printable and non-printable characters
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect C/C++ code if:  Your MATLAB code includes constant character vectors. The vector includes non-printable characters followed by printable characters.?  This issue occurs when the source MATLAB code contains use of non-printable characters that are represented as hexadecimal literals.? ? For example, if the MATLAB source contains the following a character vector: x =??[ char(1) 'a' ];  The generated C/C++ code represents char(1)  as \x01 , and the next character 'a'  is concatenated behind, producing incorrect result "\0x01a ". The vector is represented as a single character instead of having a two-character vector containing '\0x01'  and 'a '. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: ReferenceBlock error when running a test suite in Simulink Test Manager
Description: When you run a test suite in Simulink Test Manager, you might encounter an error similar to the following: Error using block2chart>is_an_sflink (line 49)  block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'ReferenceBlock'

Summary: Generating code for a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source might cause invalid index error
Description: When you generate code for a model that contains a subsystem that takes input from a muxed source (for example, a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block), the code generator might throw an error like the following: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.     Caused by:     Invalid index.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: UTF-8 characters outside of ASCII range emitted as unexpected characters
Description: The presence of non-ASCII characters in a null terminated string causes unexpected characters to be generated?in MATLAB Code: function x = foo()   x = [char(124) char(125) char(126) char(127) char(128) char(0)]; % <------- char(128) is non-ASCIIend>> codegen foo  The code generator throws this warning:foo.c:18:31: warning: initializer-string for array of 'char' is too long? and produces this code in foo.c :static const char_T cv[6] = "|}~?";??// <-------- notice the unexpected character in generated code
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB may crash for AUTOSAR models while navigating the Code Mappings Editor
Description: Opening certain tabs of the Code Mappings editor may result in a crash for AUTOSAR models if the following conditions are true: 1. There are mapped modeling elements (such as signals or blocks with states) that resolve to an external signal object. 2. The mapped modeling element belongs to a library and the library is not loaded when the Code Mappings editor is opened.?

Summary: Incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode for?a model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in rapid accelerator mode for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block. The Function-Call Subsystem has?an inplace specification (that is, the same input and output variable).

Summary: MATLAB Parallel Server jobs on Windows HPC Pack 2019 frequently appear failed on completion
Description: Jobs submitted using Parallel Computing Toolbox to a MATLAB Parallel Server cluster running Windows HPC Pack 2019 will often appear to have failed at completion. This might happen for every job or only for some jobs. The job and tasks will have state 'failed'  in both MATLAB and in the HPC Cluster Manager, even if the submitted work has completed successfully. Any outputs are still available via the OutputArguments  property of the Task  object.
Workaround: To fetch the outputs of a job that is failed:outputs = [job.Tasks.OutputArguments].';

Summary: HDL Coder generates an internal assertion when Flatten Hierarchy is turned On for the Filter blocks.
Description: HDL Coder generates the following internal assertion when you turned On?Flatten Hierarchy ?for the Filter blocks. Compilation errors when generating code for: <Block_path> ? Error during MATLAB code compilation: Error Path: B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\emlinterface\EMLInterfaceForHDL.cpp:407 Error Location: (@Row: --> 15 @Column: --> 36) Undefined function or variable '<variable_name>' . The first assignment to a local variable determines its class.?
Workaround: Consider disabling Flatten Hierarchy. To disable hierarchy flattening by using the HDL Block Properties dialog box:Right-click the subsystem.Select?HDL Code?>?HDL Block Properties.Turn Off?Flatten Hierarchy block property.?

Summary: Code generation report fails to generate
Description: The code generation report does not generate correctly when the configuration parameter 'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'  is set to Warning  or Error . This error can cause SIL/PIL simulation failure.
Workaround: Set the configuration parameter?'CheckMdlBeforeBuild'?to 'Off'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystem inport connected to signal with union sample time might produce incorrect code
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a reusable subsystem. The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems ?(BufferReusableBoundary ) is selected.? The reusable subsystem inport is connected to a signal with union sample time. The signal with union sample time originates from one of these blocks or subsystems: Outport of Function-Call Subsystem with multiple callers. Outport of?Simulink Function with multiple callers Outport of a Model Block connected to an Exported Function with multiple callers. Outport of?Model block with merged signals. Root Inport block with multiple destinations if the model configuration parameter Allow multiple tasks to access inputs and outputs  is selected.
Workaround: Clear the?Buffer for reusable subsystems?(BufferReusableBoundary) model configuration parameter.

Summary: Aggregating cvdata objects by using the "+" operator might cause an error
Description: Aggregating cvdata  objects that contain a coverage filter by using the "+" ?operator might fail with the following error message: Error using horzcat? Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent.
Workaround: Set the cvdata.filter property of the objects being aggregated to be row vectors of character vectors.

Summary: Root-level model compilation fails when a model with a Function-Call Feedback Latch block is referenced multiple times
Description: For root-level model that references multiple instances of another model that contains a Function-Call Feedback Latch block, the following error occurs when the root-level model compiles: The model <model name> has changed between the time when two instances of this model were compiled. This is not allowed for models referenced in Normal Mode.?   This error occurs because the name of an underlying subsystem of the Function-Call Feedback Latch block changes at every compilation.?

Summary: Clicking a link callback in a Simulink annotation that closes the currently active model might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash?if you click a Simulink annotation link callback that closes the current model, for example if the callback uses?gcs ?as a parameter to?close_system.

Summary: Quickly  navigating in and out of MATLAB Function blocks?and Stateflow MATLAB functions may cause disappearance of function body
Description: When you enter and exit the editor used by MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts before the editor fully loads, the function body might disappear. ?
Workaround: Before navigating out of the editor, wait until it fully loads.The behavior has been improved in R2022a and R2021b Update 3, and the bug is expected to occur less frequently in those releases.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when comparing anunsigned variable with a signed constant
Description: The generated code might be incorrect if itmeets all of the following conditions: CastingMode   property is set to Standards . TheMATLAB code in which an unsigned variable?is compared with a signedinteger constant using?relational operator?(>, <, >=, <=, ==. !=). TargetLang   is set to C++ .  In the below example, an unsigned variable iscompared with a signed constant. Sample MATLABcode:  function [output] = func_swa(input)  output = cast((input == 10),'double');   end  Inthe generated C++ code, an input not equal to zero wrapper is added which givesunexpected results. Generated C++code:  double func_swa(unsigned char input)   { return static_cast<double>((input != static_cast<unsigned char>(0)) == 10ULL);   }
Workaround: Set the CastingMode property to a value other than?Standards.

Summary: Lookup Table Dynamic block using Simulink.AliasType objects?might be unnecessarily stubbed during analysis
Description: If a model contains a Lookup Table Dynamic  block which uses Simulink.AliasType  objects, Simulink Design Verifier stubs the Lookup Table Dynamic  block and the model is incorrectly marked as partially compatible.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: J1939 Transmit block does not inherit discrete sample time
Description: The J1939 Transmit block sample time is always set to continuous even if the inputs to the block are discrete in time. The block does not inherit discrete sample time.?This can unintentionally result in a multi-rate model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for models containing custom TLC S-function blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model under the following circumstances: The model contains custom TLC S-Function blocks that create and accept expressions in the generated code. The output port of the TLC S-Function block that creates an expression is connected to the input port of a Selector block, selecting elements other than the first element. The input port of the TLC S-Function block?that accepts an expression?is connected to the output port of the Selector block.  The generated code for the Selector block incorrectly selects the first element, and the element according to the start index.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Model Advisor block constraint checks might error when running on Stateflow charts
Description: A Model Advisor block constraint check might error when running on a Stateflow chart when the chart does not have the parameter specified in the constraint.? The Model Advisor shows an error similar to:Abnormal exit: SubSystem block does not have a parameter named 'OutDataTypeStr'?

Summary: Using a Data Store Read block with a GetSet storage class that feeds into a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block might generate extraneous set function calls
Description: When you map a global Data Store block to a GetSet  storage class and you connect a Data Store Read block directly to a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block, the code generator might produce set? function calls in addition to get  function calls. The set  function calls are unnecessary for a Data Store Read block and can reduce performance.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: AXI4-Lite interface does not write data to the correct register when simultaneous read and write requests occur
Description: When generating an IP core that uses an AXI4-Lite interface, if a read and write request are simultaneous, the address of the write access register is masked by the address of the read access register causing the write data to not go through to the right register.
Workaround: Before R2022b, if support for simultaneous read/write operations is needed, use the AXI4 interface instead of AXI4-Lite in the IP core generation workflow.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In single clock mode, the timing controller can be incorrectly generated when requesting Oversampling and not utilizing Clock-Rate Pipelining.
Description: In rare instances, the timing controller generated by HDL Coder might not be appropriately clocking the design. The issue is evident during simulation of the HDL code because all or parts of the design have the clocking gated to 0. The issue occurs when the model has a base rate which is not used inside the DUT Subsystem. The model base rate, the fastest rate in the DUT, and oversampling factor all must have a certain relation in order for the timing controller to not be generated correctly. For example, if the model base rate is 1, the fastest rate in the DUT is 4, and the oversampling factor is 2, this issue can occur. The issue does not occur in most cases where an oversampling request creates an unused clock.
Workaround: To avoid the issue, choose one of these two workarounds:Alter the sample time of elements outside the DUT so that the DUT contains the fastest rate reported by the Simulink Timing Legend.Remove the oversampling request by setting Oversampling to 1, its default.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: visadevlist and visadev functions return an error or incomplete instrument list
Description: The visadevlist  function might not list any available instruments when it is unable to fully identify all of the instruments found during device discovery. Similarly, the visadev  function might treat a resource string as invalid when it cannot fully identify the resource, such as when an instrument does not respond to a *IDN?  query.  For example, the visadev  function might error when attempting to use a Keithley SCS4200: >> v = visadev("GPIB0::17::INSTR");Resource string is invalid or resource was not found. In this case, the instrument failed to respond to a "*IDN?"  query, which returns an error.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox

Summary: Simulink.Bus.createObject?function generates an error for an input structure that contains a?timeseries object with a string
Description: Creating a bus object from a structure with the Simulink.Bus.createObject  function produces an error when the input structure contains a timeseries  object with a string. For example, entering these commands in the MATLAB Command Window produces the error. ? ? ?struct.a = timeseries("A");  ? ? ?Simulink.Bus.createObject(struct); ? The error message is: Invalid value specified for parameter. Value must be a numeric array, fi object, enumerated value, structure whose fields contain valid values, or an expression.
Workaround: In the input structure, specify a string instead of a timeseries object with a string.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on Macs with M1 CPUs when rendering isosurfaces with volshow or the Volume Viewer app
Description: MATLAB crashes on Macs with M1 CPUs when rendering isosurfaces with the volshow  function or the Volume Viewer app. These are the situations in which volshow ?and Volume Viewer render isosurfaces and causes MATLAB to?crash. 1. When passing a 3-D logical array to volshow  or importing a 3-D logical array into Volume Viewer, the volume is automatically rendered as an isosurface. For example, this code causes MATLAB to crash.? load mri;  V = squeeze(D);?  BW = V > 50;?  volshow(BW);   2. When passing a 3-D numeric array to volshow , setting the "Renderer" property as "Isosurface". When importing a 3-D numeric array into Volume Viewer, changing the Rendering Editor dropdown to "Isosurface". For example, this code causes MATLAB to crash. load mri;  V = squeeze(D);??  volshow(V,"Renderer","Isosurface");?
Workaround: When calling volshow with a 3-D logical array, specify the "Renderer" name-value argument as "VolumeRendering".load mri;V = squeeze(D);?BW = V > 50;?volshow(BW,"Renderer","VolumeRendering");When importing a 3-D logical array into Volume Viewer, first convert the array to uint8 using the?im2uint8?function, and keep the Rendering Editor dropdown set to "Volume Rendering".?When passing a 3-D numeric array to?volshow, do not set the "Renderer" property as "Isosurface".?When importing a 3-D numeric array into Volume Viewer, do not change the Rendering Editor dropdown to "Isosurface".

Summary: Compatibility check and block replacement API fails with error about incorrect block execution priorities for models with Initialize Functions and model references
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with error about incorrect user-specified block execution priorities if the model consists of following types of blocks:Initialize Function  Model Reference  Global data such as Data Store Memory  or Simulink.Signal  objects  Similarly, the replacement model generated by sldvblockreplacement  API for such models also fails to compile with the same error about incorrect user-specific block execution priorities: The sample error message reported in such cases is as follows:  Unable to honor user-specified block priorities in task 0 to schedule block execution with a discrete or continuous sample time
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Design Verifier compatibility check fails for a multi-rate model containing bus element ports
Description: Ifa model contains function-call subsystems with distinct sample times and buselement ports, Design Verifier fails to construct the correct block replacement model,and compatibility checking fails.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Missing line number in table indexing error messages
Description: If your script or function includes an invalid indexing expression on a table or a timetable, then the error message displayed in the Command Window does not include the line number for the invalid expression. For example, if your file yourfunc.m  indexes into a table using a row number that is outside of the table, then this error message is displayed without a line number. >> yourfunc(t)Error using yourfuncRow index exceeds table dimensions.
Workaround: You can view the line number by using the MException.last command to examine the error at the top of the error stack.For example, suppose that you have a file yourfunc.m that indexes past the last row of the input table, as shown in this code.function x = myfunc(t)    h = height(t);    x = t(h+1,1); % This line intentionally errors.endTo get the line number of yourfunc.m where the error occurs, call MException.last and examine the?stack property of its output.>> t = table((1:3)');>> yourfunc(t)Error using myfuncRow index exceeds table dimensions.>> MException.last.stack(1)ans =  struct with fields:    file: '/MATLAB Drive/yourfunc.m'    name: 'yourfunc'    line: 3

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for State Writer block writing to Outport block of reused conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model if these conditions exist: Model contains a State Writer block. State Writer block writes to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, an Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem). Model is configured with these model configuration parameter settings: System target file ?is set to 'ert.tlc'. Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  is set to Individual arguments .  For conditionally executed subsystem, block parameter?Function packaging ?is set to Reusable function . Conditionally executed subsystem is inside another nonvirtual subsystem for which block parameter?Function packaging ?set to Reusable function .  When these conditions are met, the generated code for the State Writer block might write to a global variable instead of writing to the argument variable passed in to the reusable function for the subsystem, causing an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Chart outputs modified by exported functions might have different results in Accelerator mode
Description: When a block in your model calls a Stateflow exported function that modifies a chart output, the chart output might not be correctly updated in Accelerator mode.?This behavior occurs if the caller of the exported function comes after the chart in model execution order. As a result, simulation in Accelerator mode might produce different chart outputs than code generation or simulation in Normal and Rapid Accelerator modes.

Summary: Some Simscape models might crash target computer
Description: Real-time applications built from some Simscape models might exceed the available stack size. When run, these applications cause the target computer to crash.

Summary: Generate Tests?in Simulink Design Verifier simulates the model before generating tests
Description: When generating test cases using Simulink Design Verifier, clicking Generate Tests  button simulates the model before generating tests. This issue occurs after clicking Simulate Top Model And Generate Tests  button.
Workaround: Close and reopen the model

Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a model that contains a C Function block, if all the following conditions are true:? The C Function block uses an imported Simulink bus or enumeration.? The custom code included by the model contains an anonymous C structure or enumeration.  The generated stack trace contains lines similar to: [  0] 0x00007ffe2abfa6b6 \MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+02664118 RTWCG::ManagedCustomCodeScope::doesStructOrEnumWithSameNameExist+00000566[  1] 0x00007ffe13b23550 \MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\cgxe.dll+00406864 CGXE::AddCustomCodeGlobalVarAccessFcnsForOutOfProcess::processFcn+00018400[  2] 0x00007ffe13b230ad \MATLAB\R2022a\bin\win64\cgxe.dll+00405677 CGXE::AddCustomCodeGlobalVarAccessFcnsForOutOfProcess::processFcn+00017213

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function blocks in feedback loops which introduce latency can cause Modelsim failure
Description: If a MATLAB Function block exists in a feedback loop and these two options are set for the block: An optimization such as?DistributedPipelining    is enabled An optimization which introduces latency, such as a non-zero value for?OutputPipeline  The HDL architecture  is not set to?MATLAB Datapath   A Modelsim failure can occur.
Workaround: For the MATLAB Function block that is causing the Modelsim failure, set the HDL Block Property?HDL Architecture to MATLAB Datapath.

Summary: MATLAB Function block with native MATLAB functions that have matrix inputs may cause failures
Description: When generating code for a Simulink model, MATLAB Function block with native MATLAB functions that have matrix inputs (for example, bitand ) may cause this error:?  "'In propAssign: Assertion failed: lhsType.isMatrix() == rhsType.isMatrix() || lhsType.pointerLevel() > 1'??failed in 'b:\matlab\src\cgir_construct\utils\rtwcg_prop.cpp:2248"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect for a model with Product of Elements block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Product of Elements block with CollapseMode  set to All dimensions .? Output data type differs from the input data type for Product of Elements block.  The code generated results in loss of precision when the elements are multiplied.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 1, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Generated code for deep learning network with transposedConvolution2dLayer?might throw a runtime error for cuDNN target library on the Windows platform
Description: Generated code for deep learning network with?transposedConvolution2dLayer ?might throw a runtime error for cuDNN target library on the Windows platform.  For example, running the Generate Digit Images on NVIDIA GPU Using Variational Autoencoder ?example on the Windows platform throws the following error when executing the generated MEX: CUDA error 700 (illegal memory access)?
Workaround: In the cuDNN deep learning code configuration object, set the AutoTuning?property to false.For example:??cfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('cudnn');cfg.DeepLearningConfig.AutoTuning = false;?

Summary: Experiment fails to update when name contains invalid characters?
Description: When an experiment name contains invalid characters, running the experiment does not populate the results table. In addition, actions such as "Stop" and "Cancel" are unresponsive. Valid experiment names contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes (-), and underscores (_).
Workaround: Use a valid experiment name that contains only alphanumeric characters, dashes (-), and underscores (_).

Summary: Security Issue: MATLAB Compiler might include proxy server credentials when packaging deployable artifacts
Description: If you have configured ˇ°Web Preferencesˇ± in MATLAB to ˇ°Use a proxy server to connect to the internetˇ±, MATLAB Compiler includes Proxy host and Proxy port information when packaging an application. If ˇ°Use a proxy with authenticationˇ± is also selected, the Proxy username, and Proxy password are included as well.
Workaround: To prevent this, before packaging your applications using MATLAB Compiler, follow these steps:Open the MATLAB Preferences dialog.Select Web.Deselect Use a proxy server to connect to the internet.

Summary: The misra check for?mathworks.misra.CompliantCGIRConstructions?displays an error when model contains c2000 block
Description: The check Check for bitwise operations on signed integers  (mathworks. misra. CompliantCGIRConstructions ) displays an error when model contains Simulink blocks from following libraries: Embedded Coder Support Package for Texas Instruments C2000 F28M3x Concerto Processors? Embedded Coder Support Package for Texas Instruments C2000 Processors? ?

Summary: Mixed-Signal Analyzer app does not import signals with alias name designated in Cadence??ADE.
Description: If the Cadence? ADE output is setup where the signals have alias names, then the waveforms associated with those signals do not get imported in the Mixed-Signal Analyzer  app. The list of signals with alias names are displayed in the MATLAB? command window?with following error message :  There were issues accessing waveforms for following nodes: <list of nodes>

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for reusable subsystem with array of nonvirtual Bus Element inputs
Description: Simulink Coder generates incorrect code for a reusable subsystem under the following circumstances: The input port of a reusable subsystem is connected to the output of a Vector Element Selector block, where an element is selected that is not the first element in the source vector The input port of the Vector Element Selector block has a array of nonvirtual buses as its input type The input port block of the reusable subsystem is connected to a Bus Element Selector block The Bus Element Selector block is selecting a non-scale element from this bus  In the generated code, the element offset from the array bus selection is incorrectly subtracted from the Bus Element selection. This may lead to negative indexing or compliable code if the selected Bus Element is connected to a Vector Element Selection block that selects an element that is not the first element.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Attempting to access an undefined custom code global array variable in a Stateflow chart might crash MATLAB
Description: Attempting to access an undefined custom code global array variable from a Stateflow chart can cause MATLAB to crash, if the model configuration parameter?Import Custom Code ?is selected.
Workaround: Provide a definition for the global variable in the custom code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code with Optimize global data access set to Minimize global data access
Description: Embedded Coder might generate incorrect code for a model under the following circumstances: The Optimize global data access  option is set to Minimize global data access  The model contains nested reusable subsystems (more than 1 layer deep)  The optimization might replace global variables with local variables that do not contain the same value as the replaced global variable.
Workaround: Set the Optimize global data access model parameters option to none.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when loading an app with a custom UI component
Description: When an app contains a custom UI component, MATLAB might crash when loading the app if both of these conditions are true: The component is defined as a subclass of the matlab.ui.componentcontainer.ComponentContainer  base class The component implements custom reload behavior by overriding the loadobj  function  The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [  0] 0x00007ffb1f0a9ab0 gbt::COSWeakPtr::isNULL at B:\matlab\src\hg\hg\gbtobjects\utils\COSWeakPtr.cpp:147 [  1] 0x00007ffb1ef2e2dc gbtobjects::PositionDefaultsHandler::doDefaultsApplied at B:\matlab\src\hg\hg\gbtobjects\mixins\PositionDefaultsHandler.cpp:61 [  2] 0x00007ffb1ef3281d ui::internal::mixin::UIOuterPositionable::doDefaultsApplied at B:\matlab\src\hg\hg\gbtobjects\mixins\UIOuterPositionableMixinImpl.cpp:171[  3] 0x00007ffb1f27b940 ui::internal::mixin::UIOuterPositionable::DefaultsApplied at B:\matlab\src\hg\hg\gbtobjects\mixins\UIOuterPositionableMixin.cpp:1428

Summary: Compiler error in Simulink when using custom code from different directories
Description: Simulink might report a compiler error when you simulate a model that uses multiple custom code files from different directories.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect precision used for fixed-point arithmetic for Stateflow charts using C action language
Description: In a Stateflow chart, with C action language, in an arithmetic expression with same input types the computation is not done in full precision (For addition and subtraction the accumulator type does not use an extra bit to accommodate possible overflow).  For example in the following expression out = in1 + in2; ?where in2 and in2 have 16 bit wordlength the computation is performed in 16 bits instead of additional bit to avoid overflow.

Summary: Error when comparing requirement set file revisions?
Description: When you compare different versions of a requirement set file with highlighting enabled, you might see an error message that says?Unable to locate Requirement Set . This occurs when a version of the requirement set that you are comparing is already opened in the Requirements Editor .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported signal with a constant value
Description: Embedded Coder might not initialize an exported or imported variable that is mapped to a constant (infinite sample time) signal if the model is using bus element ports at the root-level.
Workaround: Avoid using bus element ports at the root-level and replace those with regular Inports, Outports, and Bus Selector blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation in Accelerator mode using an enabled subsystem and signals of non-matching data type sizes provides a wrong answer
Description: When a signal inside an enabled subsystem uses a signal size that is inconsistent with all other signals in the model, wrong values might be caused outside of the subsystem. This problem only is present in the JIT accelerator mode. You can observe this issue by comparing simulation values in normal and JIT accelerator mode. The normal mode and the classic accelerator mode simulations provide correct values
Workaround: When encountering this issue, switch to classic accelerator mode using the following command:?>>?set_param(0, 'GlobalUseClassicAccelMode', 'on')After issuing this command, the simulation in accelerator mode will produce correct results for models that use enabled subsystem and signals of non-matching data types sizes.

Summary: Coverage objectives?incorrectly reported as Unsatisfiable?or Dead Logic?inside?reachable MATLAB function
Description: If a MATLAB function is called only with constant arguments, coverage objectives inside the called function might show as Unsatisfiable ?or Dead Logic ?even if the objectives are reachable. This might occur due to the constant folding optimization for MATLAB Coder. For information, see?https:/ / www. mathworks. com/ help/ coder/ ug/ matlab-coder-optimizations-in-generated-cc-code. html
Workaround: Disable function inlining by setting?coder.inline?to?'never'?at the function call site:function y = fcn()? y = locFcn1();endfunction y = locFcn1()? coder.inline('never');? y = locFcn2(1);endfunction y = locFcn2(u)? if(u > 0)? ? ?y = 1;? else? ? y = 2;? endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Hidden block inserted to terminate signal logging might cause different results for normal mode simulation and code generation
Description: Normal mode simulation and code generation might have different results when all these conditions apply: Output signals from one or more Terminate Function blocks in a referenced model connect to a Merge block. The output signal from the Merge block connects to a block that represents an output port of the referenced model. The output signal from the Merge block is marked for logging.  The execution order of the hidden block differs for normal mode simulation and code generation, which includes code generation of the model reference simulation target that accelerator mode simulation uses. The different execution orders cause the different results.
Workaround: Use normal mode simulation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b

Summary: Unable to clone a Git repository with submodules when the current folder contains a subfolder with the same name as a submodule
Description: Cloning a repository warns when both  of?these conditions are true: The repository contains submodules. The current folder contains a subfolder with the same name as one of the submodules.  The following issue was encountered retrieving files from the repository  Unable     to clone repository from https://github.com/my/repo.git to     /home/user/repo.The folder     "/home/user/repo" is not empty. Select an     empty folder.Unable to clone the submodule "mysubmodule"'mysubmodule' exists and is not an empty directory (-4x3) Do you want     to continue?  If the user ignores the warning, MATLAB completes the clone without the submodule.
Workaround: To clone the repository with all submodules, ensure that the current folder does not contain a subfolder with the same name as one of the submodules in the repository.If you ignored the warning, you can add the submodules manually:?In the Current Folder Browser, right-click and select Source Control > Submodules.?In a project, on the Project tab, in the Source Control section, click Submodules.

Summary: Simulation mismatches for the selector block
Description: Generated code may have simulation mismatches for the selector block when Input is 1-D vector? Number of dimensions parameter is 2? Index options table rows are as follows Index vector (dialog) Index vector (port)

Summary: Parallel jobs may fail to start on Windows when the MATLAB_PREFDIR environment variable is set
Description: Parallel jobs may fail to start or error when all of the following conditions are true:? The workers are running on Windows. Multiple workers are starting at the same time, for example through parpool()  or batch(..,'Pool',N) .  The environment variable MATLAB_PREFDIR is set.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block containing sub-function with in-place specification on inputs with variable dimensions might result in incorrect generated code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a MATLAB Function block that meets these conditions: The MATLAB function contains a sub-function. The sub-function has an in-place specification (that is, the same input and output variable). The in-place input variable has variable dimensions.  For example, in the below representative code pattern, a MATLAB Function block is the function?top_function ?which contains the?sub_function . The P1 and P2?inputs to?sub_function ?is in-place and has variable dimensions. function [P1,P2,P3] = top_function(In1,In2)...[P1,P2,P3,P4] = sub_function(P1,P2);...end  function?[P1,P2,P3,P4] = sub_function(P1,P2)...end

Summary: Internal assertion occurs during HDL code generation when Altera Mega Functions floating-point library is used for matrix multiplication.
Description: An Altera hard floating-point (Altera Mega Functions ) based design fails to generate HDL Code because of an internal assertion during code generation when the model contains matrix multiplication.  The assertion states that unsupported component is visited in the backend VHDL/ Verilog emitter.

Summary: Loading requirement set unexpectedly loads additional requirement sets
Description: When you create a link from a requirement in requirement set A to a requirement in requirement set B, requirement set B becomes registered to the link set associated with requirement set A. If you delete the link between the requirements, requirement set B remains registered to the link set. When you load requirement set A and its link set, this also loads requirement set B, which is unexpected. Without performing the steps described below, using the?updateRegisteredReqSets? method for the  slreq.LinkSet ?object does not solve this problem.
Workaround: Load a requirement set file, and then manually close the requirement sets that you want to unregister. Then, get a handle to the link set object associated with the requirement set by using slreq.find?or slreq.getCurrentObject. You can check the requirement sets registered to the link set by using getRegisteredReqSets(myLinkSet), where myLinkSet?is a handle to the slreq.LinkSet?object. Unregister the requirement sets that no longer have links to the requirement set of interest by using updateRegisteredReqSets(myLinkSet).

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Screen reader does not work with Command History in MATLAB Online
Description: When using a screen reader in MATLAB Online, the screen reader does not announce the opening of Command History window. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB ?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.

Summary: Rapid accelerator mode incorrectly errors if?fixed-point objects are present in the model
Description: When attempting to use the rapid accelerator mode feature with fi  (fixed-point) objects in the model, the simulation fails to execute and produces the following error: "Brace indexing is not supported for variables of this type"

Summary: Graphical issues in RoadRunner when opening on nonprimary monitor.
Description: If you use multiple monitors in Windows?, one monitor is defined as your primary monitor. If your monitors have differing resolutions and you open RoadRunner on the nonprimary monitor, then text and UI graphics in the RoadRunner application might be scaled incorrectly. This issue occurs because RoadRunner obtains resolution data from the primary monitor only.
Workaround: Open RoadRunner only on the primary monitor.

Summary: Coverage incorrectly reported as unsatisfied for MATLAB functions
Description: Coverage objectives for the code in a MATLAB function might show missing coverage even if that code is executed, if the function is only called with constant arguments.??This might occur due to the constant folding optimization for MATLAB Coder. For information, see?https:/ / www. mathworks. com/ help/ coder/ ug/ matlab-coder-optimizations-in-generated-cc-code. html
Workaround: Disable function inlining by setting?coder.inline?to?'never'?at the function call site:function y = fcn()? y = locFcn1();endfunction y = locFcn1()? coder.inline('never');? y = locFcn2(1);endfunction y = locFcn2(u)? if(u > 0)? ? ?y = 1;? else? ? y = 2;? endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR generated code might write uninitialized value if array data is conditionally and partially written to root outport
Description: AUTOSAR generated code writes an uninitialized array value if all of the following conditions exist: The model contains a conditionally triggered subsystem. The subsystem contains Stateflow charts that output a conditionally written signal containing array data. The chart logic partially writes the array signal in different states. The signals from Stateflow charts feed into a Merge block. The signal output of the Merge block is written to a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to ExplicitSend .  Under these conditions, the generated code might write an uninitialized variable to the root outport.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Merge block and the subsystem outport that feeds into the root outport.

Summary: MISRA-C:2012 17.8 false positive violation on function parameters
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a violation of MISRA-C:2012 17.8 when?a function parameter is passed by address as?const  to another function. For instance: int foo(const int*);void bar(int param){    foo(&param); //False positive violation}
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Using a protected model or Simulink cache file that was created on a different computer or in a previous release might produce an error
Description: Using a protected model or Simulink cache file that was created on a different computer or in a previous release might produce an error when all of the following conditions apply: The MATLAB installation paths are different The operating system of each computer is Linux or Mac  For a model hierarchy that references the protected model, top model simulation produces an error. For a model hierarchy that references the model associated with the Simulink cache file, if the Simulink cache file contains a model reference simulation target, top model simulation and code generation produce an error or hang.
Workaround: Create a symbolic link on your computer with the same MATLAB installation path that was used by the computer that created the files.

Description: MATLAB? crashes when using the h5write  function to write data to HDF5 datasets that have a chunk size that is much larger than the actual dataset dimensions.  Consider the HDF5 file below where the ChunkSize  is greater than the dataset Size  : h5disp('myfile.h5', '/MyDset');?  HDF5 myfile.h5 Dataset 'MyDset'? ? Size:? 19066? ? MaxSize:? Inf? ? Datatype:? ?H5T_STD_I16LE (int16)? ? ChunkSize:? 201536? ? Filters:? deflate(1)? ? FillValue:? 0?  Using the code below, to write data, will crash MATLAB. data = int16(rand(19066,1))';h5write('myfile.h5', '/MyDset', data); ?? This crash occurs when using the H5D.write  function as well.?
